listings.title: Diana's house
listings.description:
Bright & Stylish Flat in the Heart of West Hampstead, London

Welcome to your peaceful retreat in vibrant West Hampstead. This beautifully designed split-level flat combines modern elegance with homey comfort. The open-plan living space boasts high ceilings, soft lighting, and stylish furnishings—perfect for relaxing after a day in the city. Whether you’re curled up on the comfy sofa or enjoying a quiet night in the serene sleeping area, you’ll feel right at home.
listings.area_description:
Located in one of London’s most charming neighbourhoods, West Hampstead offers a perfect balance of village atmosphere and city convenience. You’re just a short walk from artisan cafés, boutique shops, parks, and lively pubs. Excellent transport links (Jubilee Line, Overground, and Thameslink) put you within 15–20 minutes of Oxford Street, Camden Market, Regent’s Park, and King’s Cross.

Ideal for solo travellers, couples, or business guests looking for a quiet base with quick access to Central

listings.title: Beautiful penthouse in modernist building, in Hampstead, North London (huge roof terrace)
listings.description:
My home is the Penthouse of the Isokon Building, in Belsize Park (Hampstead), north London.

The building was originally built in 1934, and Jack and Molly Pritchard - the couple behind it - lived in the penthouse until 1969.  It's a beautiful modernist building (and Grade I listed), with some fascinating ex residents - founders of the Bauhaus, Agatha Christie, many Soviet spies, etc. It's featured in many magazines and TV programmes, and even has its own on site museum (https://isokongallery.org
listings.area_description:
The Isokon building is in beautiful, leafy Belsize Park, a short stroll to the wonderful Hampstead Heath. 

You're close to various tube stations, as well as plenty of lovely pubs, coffee shops and restaurants. 

It's a super quiet and calm place to live, where you can wake up to the bird song.

But best part is its location. The large green expanse of Hampstead Heath is right at the door stop, while the upscale Hampstead village with its cobbled streets, gourmet restaurants and artisan shops is a short walk away.

details.full_description:
2025 TRAVEL ORGANISED! Sorry, no more availability except possibly UK in October half term.

Our London home is the perfect mix of traditional Victorian and Modern architecture and is just two minutes from the open spaces of Hampstead Heath, a 790 Acre open park and woodland. We are on a quiet tree-lined road, with great connections to the rest of central London (20 minutes by bus or tube).  Our sun-filled living area and kitchen extension was completed in 2019, with a British Oak kitchen, under floor heating, warm materials and vaulted ceiling  which leads to our private garden.

- A welcoming neighbourhood with easy access to great restaurants, bars and gastropubs in Hampstead, Highgate, Kings Cross, Primrose Hill, Regents Park and Camden
- A 15 minute tube journey or 20 minute bus ride into central London
- 2 minutes away from Hampstead Heath with stunning parkland, playgrounds,  open-air Lido pool, swimming ponds and ancient woodland
- Walk to historic Keats House or Kenwood House for a taste of English grandeur

There are local shops for essentials, a chemist and cafe at the bottom of the road, and ten minutes away is the village-like shopping area of South End Green with Marks and Spencer, a fruit and veg stall, pubs, Independent book shop Daunt Books, chemist and enough bakeries to visit a different one each day of the week!

The overground station (Gospel Oak or Hampstead Heath) and tube station at Belsize Park on the Northern Line are a very short walk away, as is the bus stop which takes you into Central London.

We have 2 double bedrooms, and 2 single bedrooms.  All the rooms are good sizes. There are two bathrooms and we have a laundry room with washing machine and tumble dryer. The kitchen is well equipped with a 2 oven range, boiling hot water tap, dishwasher, microwave and coffee machine. We love to cook so you will find everything your need.  We have Netflix, Disney +, Amazon Prime, lots of board games and books you can read.

The  light filled open-plan living space and kitchen with huge door to our private garden are on the ground floor with one double bedroom, one single and a bathroom with walk in shower. 

We're lucky to have a secluded garden with sun in the morning until late afternoon.

In the lower level there are two further bedrooms - a double room with skylight and a spacious single room. An extra bed could be added here. This level also has a bathroom with bath and shower, and a separate laundry room with washing machine, dryer and sink.

We can offer the option of a cleaner to visit during your stay for a small fee.  Depending on when you arrive either I or a relative will meet you at the flat to give you keys and a quick tour. Then it's all yours!

We do have a cat, very easy going, who goes in and out independently, she just needs food twice a day.
details.full_area_description:
We have friendly neighbours on this quiet and calm street yet it's very close to Central London on the tube, bus or even walking. Popular with families and young people. The best area of London in our opionion!

- Buzzy restaurants: Berbere Pizza, Jin Kichi, Granary Square Kings Cross
- Cool bars: The Magdala, Southampton Arms, Bull and Last, lots in Kentish Town and Camden for Pubs and Bars
- Cozy cafés: Greenberry, Euphorium
- Retail therapy: Between Cool Camden and High End Hampstead
- Bit of culture: Cinemas, Theatres and galleries nearby, Kenwood House.
- Walks in the park: The expanses of Hampstead heath are 2 minutes away
- Spot of exercise: Swimming in the Lido or Hampstead Ponds, Running on the Heath or cycling around Regents Park
- Day tripping: Easy access out of London by car. We are 15 minutes by bus from Kings Cross train station where you can hop to the major cities of the UK and even Paris or Amsterdam on the Eurostar.

details.full_description: Our large, elegant 4 floor family home in historic Hampstead features 4 bedrooms (2 double, 2 single), 4 bathrooms, 2 studies, a double reception room and a utility room.  The house retains many historical Victorian features, and was renovated to include a superb architect-designed rear extension, featuring a modern kitchen and a large family room overlooking a wooden terrace and an unusually generous 50 foot rear garden (with very large pear tree, plum tree, fresh vegetables and herbs), plus full size table tennis table, 4K TV with Amazon Prime etc.
details.full_area_description:
Hampstead is one of the most upscale, picturesque and vibrant areas in London, located only 20 minutes by tube or double-decker red bus from the heart of London. 

Our house is located on a quiet and neighbourly Victorian street, steps away from Hampstead Heath - 800 acres of countryside where you can get right away from the noises of the city and enjoy spectacular views of the whole of London and beyond. The area of the Heath nearest to us includes a fabulous extensive playground (5 mins walk), tennis courts, swimming and paddling pools, as well as natural ponds for swimming.

Hampstead itself has a village atmosphere and is full of interesting architectural and historical sites, such as Sigmund Freud and Keats’ Houses, as well as great shops, restaurants and cafés. It is also an easy walk to Primrose Hill, Regent’s Park and Camden Lock market, plus Belsize Park, with its wide range of delis, cafes and arthouse cinema.  

The frequent 24 double decker red London bus travels from the end of our road direct to many London landmarks, including Trafalgar Square, the Houses of Parliament/Big Ben and Leicester Square.  The house is also close to Belsize Park tube station on the Northern Line, which is another quick route into the centre of the city.  We are also very near Hampstead Heath overground train station and various useful buses.
details.full_other:
Large, mature garden, with fruit trees and herbs
Minutes away from London's largest green space 
Close to Central London,with great transport links

NOTE We have severe animal allergies in the family so are not able to exchange with any home with dogs, cats etc.  NOTE We also only want to exchange with homes of similar size/quality.

Our lovely home is a truly beautiful Georgian House built in 1860 and quintessentially English, with a coastal charm through its whitewashed wood floor, exposed beams and a working fire place.

We have 2/3 bedrooms and 2 Bathrooms plus one open plan eat-in kitchen and living room, with a small patio to eat outside.

Thanks!!

listings.area_description: Located in one of the best neighbourhoods in London, Islington offers the perfect mix of charm, convenience, and vibrant city life. The area is extremely well connected, with easy access to public transport and all major London airports. You’ll find everything you need just around the corner - from supermarkets and independent shops to cosy cafés and great restaurants. It’s an amazing place to enjoy your time in London, whether you’re here to explore, relax, or simply live like a local.

Very convenient for public transport, busses, underground, overground trains

listings.title: Meg and Matt's Islington flat - great central location

It's a short walk to lots of shops and amenities - Upper street is 10 minutes away with great bars, restaurants and shopping. Our flat is a small one and for the sake of noise and not disturbing our neighbours, we would only like 2 people to stay maximum (we have 1 king size bed) 

No pets

No smoking in the flat or on the balcony - please leave the building and smoke/vape outside :)

listings.title: Modern 4BR Kings Cross | Patio + Balcony

listings.area_description: Steps from trendy Coal Drop yards with high end boutique shopping, numerous restaurants, nearly nightly entertainment options, clubs, and 14 minute walk from the best connected train station in Zone 1.

listings.area_description:
We’re based right by Coal Drops Yard, one of our favourite parts of London. It’s a beautifully restored old industrial area that sits along the canal and has a really calm, tucked-away feel, while still being right in the middle of the city.

There are great cafés, restaurants and independent shops on your doorstep, plus lovely walks along the water and around Granary Square. It feels creative and lived-in rather than touristy, and is especially nice in the mornings and evenings when it’s quiete

listings.title: Tobias' home

listings.area_description: Islington is both leafy and lively. You can be in the centre in 20 minutes via regular buses or the tube (or even less if you cycle!), but it's also quiet and friendly. Picturesque Georgian and Victorian architecture, great pubs, restaurants, cafes and parks are all a few minutes walk away - I can give recommendations - and you can get to Dalston, Stoke Newington, Clerkenwell and even Camden, Hampstead, etc. within about 20 minutes.

listings.title: Mid-century Modern London Town House

The house is newly renovated with modern furnishings, with a private entrance and rear balcony. The 2 bedrooms, 1 office/gym (with inflatable mattress), 2 bathrooms, kitchen and living room are split over 3 floors.

listings.area_description: Plenty of local restaurants, cafes and pubs, with easy access to all areas of London via public transport.

The open plan kitchen/living room is a great space to host with the fireplace and then you have a spacious bathroom next to the bedroom with a little patio next to the bedroom.

It is also very well located, only a 15 min walk from either Highbury & Islington (Victoria Line - blue line) or 12 min Angel station (Northern line - black line) which are both 24 hour tub

There are also plenty of local activities to do such as the Angel Comedy Club or the Almeida theatre that is just round the corner (4 min walk). The renowned Sadler's Wells Theatre, which hosts famous ballet companies, is also just a 20 minutes away.

details.full_description:
Hannah has lived in this house since she was born after her mother bought it in 1976. Now we live there as a family, Hannah, Jonny and Ysolde (born October 2021) and our black & white cat. 

It’s an old terraced house and we have lodgers living in the basement who share the same entrance but live separately. On the ground floor there is a music room with a double sofa bed available. Our lodgers use the music room to practice as they are professional musicians/singers. You may hear them practising. This room has a TV, harpsichord, piano and a working fire place. 

There is a toilet and wash basin on this floor. On the first floor is our bedroom with a super-king bed. 

We have one double bed, one single floor bed and a double sofa bed. CAN ONLY SLEEP 5. HomeExchange automatically states it can sleep 6 but it’s 5. 
On the top floor is a cosy kitchen (WE DO NOT HAVE A MICROWAVE) and small bathroom which we plastered ourselves in the Moroccan tadelakt style. There is also a small kid's bedroom with a single mattress floor bed which can be used by an adult (this is on a wooden frame, but is basically on the floor.) We have a large garden out the back with BBQ, table & chairs and hammock available.

The photos were taken by a Location company which is why they look professional!

OCTOBER 2023 update
We had some decorating done for the last three months. Photos of the new rooms will not be professional. 

It is our primary home so is only available when we happen to be away.

There are a lot of stairs in the property.
details.full_area_description:
Our house is in Barnsbury in Islington, just north of King's Cross. It has great transport links to the centre of town and from all the airports. Caledonian Road underground station (Piccadilly line) and Highbury & Islington underground station (Victoria line) are both 10 mins walk away. Also the Overground Line has a station on our road (Caledonian Road & Barnsbury) which links further afield - very easy to get to Hampstead Heath for example. 

In our opinion one of the best London pubs ‘The Hemingford arms’ is on the corner of our road. 

A very popular brunch spot called ‘Sunday’ is round the corner and people travel from all over London to eat there. 

Nice walk to local market (farmers' market on a Sunday morning) and shopping centre at The Angel. A short walk from the ever popular Upper Street with its trendy shops and restaurants. A magical location in a residential area, with great access to transport, the centre of town and a most popular shopping street.
We are very close to the canal which we used to live on when we lived on a houseboat. You can walk along the canal to Camden Town, Regent’s Park etc 

We are also not far from the new exciting Coal Drops Yard development at Kings Cross
details.full_other:
We would hope that you could feed our cat for us and water our plants. 

Be aware that we have lodgers living in our basement flat. We all share an entrance but live separately. 

We sometimes have musicians or music groups / ensembles rehearsing in the music room. If this is happening during your stay we will let you know beforehand. They will not come into your living space. 

Our washing machine is in a small shed in the garden. No dryer. 

There are lots of stairs in our property and there are stairs to the entrance also.

There is a cleaning fee of £40

listings.area_description: Islington was built mainly in the late 1700's and early 1800's and has some of the best Georgian architecture in London. You can walk through the tree lined streets that (apart from the cars) have not altered in appearance in 200 years.Some local pubs used to be Coaching Inns where horse and carriages would stop on their way through London. Kings Cross has just been redeveloped with Coal Drops Yard an amazing new retail area with restaurants and shops in a chic reclaimed industrial setting.

A rare chance to stay on a narrowboat on the Regents Canal. Situated on a secure private mooring in the heart of central London.

Our boat is hooked up to the electricity mains and water. 

It has heating, hot water, a fully equipped kitchen.
The bathroom has a hot shower and a compost toilet. 
There is one bedroom and also a pull out double sofa bed.

The boat is not suitable for young children.

There are lots of ducks and swans on the canal.

Very easy to get to the West End/Soho/Covent Garden. Regent's Canal is a minutes walk away and is worth a walk down. The canal will take you to Camden and further along into
listings.other_notes: We would love to swap this flat with someone with a similar space in Europe. Paris and Barcelona are on the top of our list but open to offers from anywhere. Long weekends or week long holidays preferred, possibly open to longer!

listings.title: Central London apartment. King size bed, Pilates studio, fully equipped kitchen and cozy living-room
listings.description:
Welcome to my apartment! You have one bedroom with a king size bed and one pilates studio with a reformer, dumbbells, mat... for your workout. 

I love cooking and you'll find everything you need in the Kitchen to make any Ottolenghi recipe. 

There's all the basics: high speed internet, heating, hot water... but no A/C, no TV and access by stairs only. I’m on the first floor.

I have installed triple glazing so it's super quiet day and night.
listings.area_description: The apartment is perfectly located in the heart of Upper Street Islington with 100 restaurants, 8 theatre, 4 concerts halls and many many shops within 10 minutes walking distance. You're also 5 minutes from Highbury & Islington Station which is on the Victoria Line, the best tube line in London, stopping by Kings Cross, Oxford Circus, Victoria Station... and there's also 4 super convenient bus lines stopping right across the street.

listings.title: Beautiful 2 bed 2 bath in Shoreditch in the old Alfred Hitchcock filming studios
listings.description:
With wonderful access to Regent’s Canal and Shoreditch Park, this fantastic two-bedroom apartment is situated on the 4th floor of the iconic Gainsborough Studios, the ex Alfred Hitchcock filming studios.

Gainsborough Studios is a gated development with 280 apartments and a 24-hour concierge. It was designed by the renowned architecture practice Munkenbeck & Marshall in 2003, who refurbished the old film studios used by Alfred Hitchcock. It occupies a site between the Regent’s Canal on one side
listings.area_description:
Gainsborough Studios is within walking distance of the City, the shops and restaurants at Angel, and the galleries and nightlife of Shoreditch and Hoxton. Old Street station provides Underground access to the West End and the City, and there are excellent local bus routes into central London.

The neighborhood is safe and with a 24hr concierge on hand, you'll always feel safe and supported.

listings.title: Lucy's house
listings.description:
I'm offering a 3-bedroom house spread across two floors, located on the top level in the heart of Islington, London. The property features a spacious living room of approximately 1000 square feet and a large balcony of the same size. As I'm getting older, I'm seeking a house swap for a flat with a lift for easier accessibility.

I am only interested In exchanging for a two bedroom in Islington for a permanent exchange.

Sources
listings.area_description: Islington, Essex Road

listings.title: Michele's house
listings.description:
Traditional turn of the century London terraced house. Recently renovated; all mod-cons. 5 minutes walk from Highbury and Islington Tube (12 minute into Oxford Street). Small courtyard with easy walk to two large parks. 4 Stories. 4 Bedrooms. 1 Study, Two double sitting rooms. Dining room/Kitchen. BBQ in courtyard. Bikes you can borrow.

I cannot reply instantly as too many messages to keep track of. Please bare with me. I don't mean to offend I just cant keep up.  Warmest.
listings.area_description: From the modern apartment buildings and busy shopping hub of Angel to the leafy Georgian squares that lie just a few blocks away, Islington’s diverse qualities attract residents from all walks of life. Recent years have seen its popularity boom and prospective buyers are queuing up to gain a foothold in the area. Islington residents are as likely to be found searching for antiques in Camden Passage as they are tucking into some tasty international food from Exmouth Market, or sipping cocktails i
listings.other_notes: Ideally keen on guests who can do all days as hard to do change over when we are away plus we need our cat looked after/loved x will try too to accommodate bookings that match each other and ask each to be understanding  with change over logistics  x michele

listings.title: Islington Townhouse
listings.description: Period terrace house dating from 1848 in Barnsbury conservation area. Close to tube stations and London Overground and a short walk to the vibrant shops and restaurants of Upper Street. A fifteen minute walk to Kings Cross and the shops and restaurants of Granary Square. The house has recently been refurbished and decorated throughout. We have a new office garden room built.
listings.area_description:
Close to all the shops, cinemas, theatres  and restaurants  on Upper Street, a vibrant area in a very central part of London. The house s in a conservation area and the local area  has some outstanding architecture.  
Tube stations and over ground station all within a short walk.  Kings Cross is a longer walk at 15 mins. 
There are lots of bus routes- it takes a short time to get to most areas of central London. 

- Buzzy restaurants: there are many close to hand ! 
- Cool bars: there are many

listings.title: Olesya's quiet and spacious flat
listings.description:
Spacious 2 bedroom flat on a quiet street close to Old Street (10 min) and Regent’s canal. Angel is 15 minutes away.
Fast optic fibre internet. Desks (one with a monitor) enable you to work remotely if needed.
Bed linen is 100% cotton.
Off street car park in the underground is available on request
listings.area_description: Close to canals, Angel, City and buzzy Shoreditch. Around the corner from Victoria Miro gallery. The area around my place is very quiet which is great for resting after a busy day out!

listings.title: Central London Warehouse Conversion
listings.description:

It is also close to restaurants, shops, cinemas, theatres and has excellent transport links to Europe and the rest of the UK. It is on the doorstep of 5 tube lines; St Pancras station for Paris and Euston for the rest of the UK.

it is in a quiet, secure, clean and safe gated development and is ideal of a visit to
listings.area_description:
It is near a canal where you can walk along to Regents Park and the famous Camden market.  You'll love it guaranteed.

If you're tired of London you're tired of life!
listings.other_notes: The flat is in a secure gated community and has all the amenities you will need, it is not suitable for children.

listings.title: Lucy's house

We are very centrally located.  It is a pleasant walk through local streets to bath the Angel and Kings cross both  located within zone 1. For quick access into central London we are in Zone 2, Canonbury overground station is a 5 minute walk away giving easy acc
listings.area_description:
Our home is is Islington but within easy distance of Dalston

Our home is in Islington one of London’s most buzzing boroughs. It’s where north London officially begins and along its main drag, Upper Street there’s plenty going on. Islington is particularly good at pubs and you’ll find some of the best pubs in London on Islington’s quiet neighbourhood streets. There’s some excellent restaurants too, as well as one of the capital’s best cinemas, plus theatres, shops, and art galleries.

Busy Dalst

 The flat is a short walk from King's Cross Station, the heart of London transport. It is very easy to get anywhere in London or the rest of the UK.
listings.other_notes: It is a top rated AirBNB with average of 4.93 stars after 27 bookings.

A Co-op supermarket is located just across the street, open seven days a week until 10pm, ensuring everyday essentials are always within easy reach.

MAGICAL GEORGIAN MAISONETTE, central London N1

NO GUEST POINTS

It is 35 mins from Heathrow without traffic.
5 mnute taxi to Kings cross and the Eurostar

listings.title: London Apartment in a Trendy Neighborhood

Our home is a peaceful and quiet retreat, located on the top floor (4-store building) with plenty of natural light throughout the apartment. Every room has windows, creating a bright and airy atmosphere. The bedroom features a comfortable queen-sized mattress, perfect for two people.

The living room is cozy and inviting, equipped with a record player for enjoying music and a projector for a great film-watching experience. It’s the perfect space to relax and unwind.

listings.other_notes: Cleaning fee per stay is £100.

listings.title: Large Victorian house in a cool area of East London.

Check in is best on Mondays from 2pm (after our cleaner has been)

The House is 150 years old has many period features as well as a modern basement level. It's full of modern and antique furniture, paintings and photographs from 17th century to modern contemporary art.

Its a good mix tastefully put together to give a high quality, warm and co

Victoria Park is just moments away — a dream for families:

We are on many bus lines and a 15 minute walk to the overground transit station which takes you right to Liverpool Street in 10 minutes. Bonus, we have a nice green grocer across the roa

There’s also lots to do in the local area including independent shops, cafes, restaurants… and several great pubs too. Dalston, Shoreditch and Broadway market are also nearby and offer all sorts of other options for restaurants, bars and nightlife!

Welcome to our inviting 5-bedroom family London home, nestled in the heart of one of the city's charming neighbourhoods.

The highlight of out home stay is the open-plan kitchen and outdoor space— a private garden oasis where you can unwind and enjoy some fresh air on deckchairs. 

The kitchen space is spacious and bathed in natural light due to the large areas of glazing, it can sometimes fell like you're outside! Fully equipped kitchen, complete with modern appliances and ample counter space a
listings.area_description:
15/20 mins to central London on the overground and underground transport system.

Located within walking distance of local shops, restaurants, and attractions, our family London home offers the perfect blend of comfort and convenience for your next getaway. Whether you're exploring the iconic landmarks of the city or simply soaking in the vibrant atmosphere, this charming retreat is sure to make your stay unforgettable.

listings.title: Anna's house is above the charming independent shops of Wilton Way Hackney East London
listings.description:
Close to London Fields Park the quiet neighbourhood location offers much to those who want contemporary living on their doorstep but tranquility within their home.
The space: Four bedrooms include a master ensuite, kids room with bunks, a single room with a double bed and another bedroom with a double bed. The finishes have been beautifully styled with parquet flooring, Bert and May tiles and the owners collection of modern art.
listings.area_description: London Fields is a vibrant, creative East London neighborhood known for its trendy atmosphere, with excellent transport links to central London and the West End. The area is adjacent to London Fields park, which features green spaces, a lido, and tennis courts. Guests can easily walk to notable spots like Broadway Market, Victoria Park, and Shoreditch, and it's close to Hackney Central and London Fields Overground station

listings.title: Cosy East London Maisonette
listings.description:
Nestled on a quiet, leafy street in vibrant East London, our charming three-story maisonette offers the perfect East London escape. Inside, discover a charming home with warm wooden floors, filled with books, musical instruments, and art.

Inside you'll also find:
3 Bedrooms || A main bedroom and two flexible office spaces that easily convert into comfortable guest bedrooms (with sofa beds).
2 Bathrooms || One with a refreshing shower, one with a relaxing bathtub.
Fully Stocked Kitchen, perfect
listings.area_description:
Just a 5 min walk from Hackney Central Overground station, you'll have swift access to all corners of London, while enjoying the best of local living on your doorstep, with an array of fantastic local cafes, pubs, eateries, music venues, breweries, and parks.

Hackney is a vibrant and diverse neighborhood in the heart of London's East End, known for its creative energy, rich history, and lively atmosphere. It offers a perfect blend of urban grit and trendy sophistication, making it a popular des

listings.title: Stylish home with two balconies in heart of Hackney. Full of books, plants, records and artwork.
listings.description: My flat is perfectly located to explore Hackney and surrounding areas but also well connected to get into central London in less than 30 mins. So many great restaurants, bars and cute places to explore in walking distance. I love art, fashion, photography and music and I think you'll feel that staying here! Spend a afternoon chilling on my sofa looking through photography books after a morning exploring Broadway Market. Then head into Dalston later on for a taste of London's finest nightlife. Tr
listings.area_description: You are absolutely spoilt for choice here for restaurants, bars, venues for live music. Catering for all budgets and tastes. Also lovely green spaces with London Fields, Victoria Park and hackney Downs all in walking distance. So many cute boutiques too through Dalston, Clapton, Stoke Newington.

listings.title: East London light-filled 2 story flat - seeking reciprocal exchanges in major cities only  ⭐️
listings.description:
Light-filled, large two-story apartment in Dalston/Hackney (E8) and close to a variety of fantastic restaurants, bars, shops, and activities. Easy access to central London via Hackney Downs (6-minute walk) or Dalston Kingsland (10-minute walk) stations.

This apartment is full of charm and character and features two beautifully preserved non-working fireplaces, tall ceilings and period details like crown moulding. Light pours in through two nearly floor to ceiling windows downstairs and multiple
listings.area_description: Dalston, in Hackney, East London, is a vibrant and culturally diverse neighborhood known for its creative energy and dynamic arts scene. With a mix of Victorian terraces, modern apartments, and colorful street art, it's a hotspot for nightlife, independent cafes, and global cuisine. The iconic Ridley Road Market reflects the area's multicultural vibe, offering fresh produce and exotic foods. Dalston’s excellent transport links, lively music venues, and green spaces like Dalston Eastern Curve Gar
listings.other_notes: I am always easily reachable through WhatsApp. I want you to have a lovely East London experience and will do my best to ensure you are comfortable during your stay. :)

listings.title: Stylish Mews Home in Hackney
listings.description:
Welcome to our quiet, contemporary mews home in the heart of Hackney – a hidden oasis behind the bustle of East London.

Our house is part of a small, friendly development tucked away on a private lane. With clean lines, high ceilings, and large windows, the space is filled with natural light throughout the day.

The home offers 2 comfortable bedrooms + a sofa that is comfortable to sleep on, 2.5 modern bathrooms, and a fully equipped open-plan kitchen and living area – perfect for unwinding, co
listings.area_description:
Our home is tucked away in a quiet mews in the heart of Hackney, one of London’s most creative and vibrant neighbourhoods.

Despite its central location, the mews offers rare peace and privacy – a hidden retreat just moments from the energy of East London life. Within a short walk, you’ll find local favourites like Broadway Market, London Fields, Columbia Road Flower Market, and Regent’s Canal – perfect for weekend strolls, coffee stops, and browsing independent shops.

The area is rich with caf

listings.title: Cosy scandi and mid-century inspired 1-bedroom flat in the heart of Hackney, East London.
listings.description:
A lovely top floor East-facing flat in the heart of Hackney, East London.

It’s a recently renovated one-bedroom space that feels calm and cosy. Everything is modern and easy to use, with new appliances including a washing machine and induction hob, a beautifully light bathroom, and a comfortable bedroom with a warm, peaceful feel and a king-size bed and desk.

The building is incredibly quiet thanks to the 1950s style brick building which is well insulated and sound proofed.
listings.area_description:
You’re right in the middle of Hackney here - just a 5-minute walk from some of the best pubs, restaurants, wine bars and coffee spots along Chatsworth Road, and a short stroll to Broadway Market. There’s also plenty of nature nearby, with Victoria Park, London Fields and the canal all within easy walking distance.

It’s the best of both worlds - a neighbourhood that feels vibrant and local, while central London and all its culture is just a quick Overground or Tube ride away.
listings.other_notes:
My work and travel plans can be flexible - if you're interested in staying or swapping sometime then let me know, and we can see if it could work!

Linen and towels provided. Small cleaning fee per stay may be needed, to be discussed.

listings.title: Direct exchange only in August - Victorian  family home in London Hackney- Canal Path, Olympic Park
listings.description:
Please note only UK or France exchanges  only as we have a dog that will travel with us . 

There is a possibility of offering an exchange in October, Easter and May half term but our guests will need to commit to looking after our dog as he will stay home. 
Family home, spacious, cosy and comfortable. It is more traditional rather than modern but quite charming in our and many of our friend's opinions.
listings.area_description: Good transport to central London, so much to do from vibrant music and entertainment events nearby in Olympic Park and east lovely escape to country walks through the marches, canal path, beautiful parks. Friendly  neighbours and community that will welcome and look after you during your stay
listings.other_notes: There are numerous events in the area at different times of the year to choose from, from the sport at the Olympic Park to dance, theatre, live music, fashion and art, dotted around Hackney.

listings.title: Nuala's house
listings.description:
My home is in trendy Hackney, East London. Ours is a Victorian terrace, typical of London. We have 2 gorgeous cats, Eric and Ernie who are very friendly and would just need to be fed during your stay. They have a cat flap so come and go as they please. We have 2 boys, 12 and 14. We have bikes and and other kiddie friendly items like board games, rackets. 
I am a stylist and my husband is a therapist. 
Our home is calm and peaceful. We have a small garden in the back.
listings.area_description:
Hackney is a vibrant neighbourhood in East London which makes a great base for a holiday sightseeing in London. 

We are a 10 minute walk to the Hackney marshes and a 15 minute walk to the Queen Elizabeth Olympic park. We are a 10 minute walk from the gorgeous Victoria park and 20 minutes from Broadway Market. Columbia Road flower market is a short bus ride away. 

Hackney is a multi cultural area, with a vast  mix of ethnicities which creates a vibrant, lively neighbourhood. 

The nearest stati

listings.title: David's house
listings.description:
Our recently remodeled 1930s Arts & Crafts home is the best home we’ve had both because of the location as well as the character and the work we’ve put in it. It offers the perfect balance of London charm and modern comfort. Nestled in Hackney, one of the city’s most vibrant and creative neighbourhoods, we’re surrounded by beautiful green spaces and within easy reach of everything London has to offer.

Inside, the home blends original Arts & Crafts features with a light-filled, contemporary reno
listings.area_description: Step outside, and you’re moments fromp several parks, including one of the most beloved parks,  Victoria Park,  and Hackney Downs — perfect for morning runs, picnics, or just soaking up Lo upndon’s quieter side. Excellent transpo rt links put you in the heart of Central London in around 20 minutes, yet our street feels calm and reside ntial with it also being on a cul-de-sac. Within the walking distance (15 minutes or even less), you can get to many lovely cafes, restaurants, Victoria Park Villa

listings.title: Jeff's house
listings.description:
My home is a little oasis in the heart of East London (the best place to be). My favourite part is the balcony/terrace that flows right off the living area. It faces South/West/North and is the best place in the city to enjoy the sun all day until it sets, on one of the two deck chairs! In the summer it overlooks the tree-lined streets and is a gorgeous place to have a dinner al fresco.

It has an open plan kitchen / living room area with 3 seater sofa and an arm chair. There's an Alexa speaker
listings.area_description: I live in Hackney. This is the main creative hub of the city and my favourite borough! The apartment is conveniently located between 2 key stations (Hackney Downs and Hackney Central) that provide quick access to a lot of London. We're also near several parks that are within walking distance (Hackney Downs, Victoria Park, London Fields). There's a big M&S, Tesco, Co-Op and also a little Sainsbury's, as well as local grocery stores at your doorstep. There are also plenty of cafe's and internation
listings.other_notes:
No families / kids / babies / pets. The home isn't set up to support that!
Max 2 people.

listings.title: Family home in Hackney
listings.description: A comfy family home with plenty of space to relax. There is a large kitchen with  room to seat 8 around the table.  There is a spacious through lounge with a couple of sofas and a piano. Upstairs there are two double bedrooms, and one single bedroom. In the basement there is an extra door to the garden from the utility room and storage room. We have a small patio garden with table and chairs. We are a few minutes walk from local pubs, restaurants, cafes and a lovely Sunday Market is at the end o
listings.area_description:
We live in a friendly and interesting part of the city just a few streets away from a range of  restaurants and independent shops. The nearby Olympic park and Hackney Marshes offer plenty of green space but the trainline 
means that we are an easy ride (35mins) from the heart of London with its world famous museums and attractions. 
The main railway stations in the city allow for day trips out to Windsor, Cambridge, Oxford and Bristol making our house a perfect base to explore the rest of the co
listings.other_notes: If you are planning days out in London we would recommend the Tate Modern gallery and the South Bank of the Thames as a great destination. The 242 bus from the end of the our road will take you through the heart of the financial district where passing by the world famous Bank of England you can stop

details.full_description: A comfy family home with plenty of space to relax. There is a large kitchen with  room to seat 8 around the table.  There is a spacious through lounge with a couple of sofas and a piano. Upstairs there are two double bedrooms, and one single bedroom. In the basement there is an extra door to the garden from the utility room and storage room. We have a small patio garden with table and chairs. We are a few minutes walk from local pubs, restaurants, cafes and a lovely Sunday Market is at the end of the road!
details.full_area_description:
We live in a friendly and interesting part of the city just a few streets away from a range of  restaurants and independent shops. The nearby Olympic park and Hackney Marshes offer plenty of green space but the trainline 
means that we are an easy ride (35mins) from the heart of London with its world famous museums and attractions. 
The main railway stations in the city allow for day trips out to Windsor, Cambridge, Oxford and Bristol making our house a perfect base to explore the rest of the country.
details.full_other: If you are planning days out in London we would recommend the Tate Modern gallery and the South Bank of the Thames as a great destination. The 242 bus from the end of the our road will take you through the heart of the financial district where passing by the world famous Bank of England you can stop off at St Pauls and cross the river at the Millennium Bridge. Following a visit to the gallery you can walk along the river bank to eat out in Borough Market. Nearby is Shakespeare's Globe Theatre and also the less well known Rose Theatre where Marlowe's Tamberlaine was first performed!

listings.title: Hackney home & garden, London
listings.description:
For Summer 2026 we are looking for a UK exchange only.  

Lovely family home with beautiful garden in a quiet pocket of Hackney, East London with free car-parking port.  There’s a garden room in the garden which is fully insulated and has heating, workspace and very comfortable double sofa bed.  In addition there’s a single chair bed in the garden room so our property sleeps 6 comfortably and 7 at a squeeze.
listings.area_description:
Friendly neighbourhood with independent shops, cool cafes and restaurants, vegan pub, sauna, parks and Marshes all on the doorstep.  Accessible to swimming pools and lido.  7 min walk to the nearest tube, connecting you to central London within 30 mins.

We live in London's greenest borough, meaning we have the largest area of green spaces to enjoy.  Great for picnics, nature walks - a river runs through the Hackney Marshes which is nearby and you can see lots of wildlife there.  If you like to
listings.other_notes: Walking distance to parks, transport, cafés, restaurants, shops, bars, playgrounds, sauna.  Short distance from swimming pools, lidos, City Farms.  Well placed for transport into central London and all that this vibrant and cultural city has to offer; museums, art galleries, music venues, events, hi

listings.title: Cozy and luminous three bedroom flat in the heart of East London.
listings.description: We live in Hackney a very nice neighbourhood of London. There are so many things to do and also is very easy to go to central London. Our flat is very easy to clean up, comfortable and bright, we love it!.
listings.area_description:
Hackney is a vibrant and culturally diverse neighborhood in London, and is a popular area of the capital to reside, attracting young families, affluent couples, and working professionals alike.

Formerly a quaint Tudor town with beautiful 16th-century brick buildings, stunning evidence of Hackney’s previous life is interspersed amongst new builds, cutting-edge apartments, and quirky restaurants that make this East London borough one of the capital’s best places to live.

A hive of creativity, Ha
listings.other_notes: We are in a vibrant area of London. We are close to lovely parks: Victoria Park, London Fields, Hackney Downs, and the Hackney Marshes. We recommend the latter for long and calm walks. In terms of coffee shops and markets, we are near the famous Broadway Market, which is open every Saturday. On the

listings.title: Stunning apartment in Hackney
listings.description: Our home is a very spacious city spartment with floor to ceiling windows and a south west facing balcony. Perfect spot to return to after a busy day and night enjoying the sights of London
listings.area_description:
We live Hackney which is perfectly located for a stay in London, surrounded by good restaurants and bars but also only a short walk from 2 of Londons best parks, London fields and Victoria Park. 
With excellent transport links into the city, the Overground service from Hackney Central  a 10 minute walk from our flat and Bethnal Green Tube station a short bus ride. We also have a number of bus routes that run 24 hours  

- Buzzy restaurants: Any restaurant on Broadway Market

Two bedrooms, each with double bed. The master bedroom includes access to a bright terrace. The second bedroom includes a day bed (that can be made into a double bed) and a cot.

Fully equipped kitchen, including dishwasher and washing machine/dryer, opens on to a large living and dining area.

This clean and well equipped apartment is perfect for your stay in London. If you have any questions, just
listings.area_description: The area is a charming, well connected, hotspot in East London. The village itself has everything you need from great restaurants and cosy pubs. We are less than two minute walk to the wonderful Victoria Park, which hosts a great market every Sunday.

listings.title: Vibrant London Fields home
listings.description: Our home is a split level maisonette with ground floor access. Downstairs is an open plan living area, upstairs we have three bedrooms, a bathroom and separate WC. We have a main bedroom with a king sized bed, a childrens room with a bunk bed and a smaller room with single bed. Please note that if you need all three bedrooms then we will struggle to make any wardrobe space for you and children’s toys etc will still be dotted around the flat. If you only need two bedrooms we can make more space f
listings.area_description: Our family home is in Hackney (zone 2), a central location with loads to see and do! We live in a wonderful estate with lovely neighbours. Next to London Fields park with tennis courts, two children's playgrounds, a brilliant (heated) lido and Broadway Market full of excellent restaurants and a bustling weekend market with produce, goods and cooked food.  The famous Columbia Road flower market is a walk away as are three overground train lines taking you all over London, we are also on several e
listings.other_notes: Non smokers only please

listings.title: 4 Bedroom modern maisonette in Dalston
listings.description:
Welcome to our home! We have a four-bedroom flat located in a modern development right in the vibrant heart of Dalston. At over 1,300 square feet, there's plenty of space to relax and spread out.

The interiors are functional and really bright, and we recently put down beautiful new parquet flooring throughout. The kitchen is well-equipped and centred around a sleek island—it's a great spot for cooking and socializing.

You'll also get to enjoy the generous balcony that extends from the bedrooms
listings.area_description:
We love being so close to Dalston with its restaurants, bars and things to do. The Dalston Curve Garden is a favourite and it's only a few minutes away. Having both Dalston Junction and Dalston Kingsland nearby makes getting around really easy.

The street itself is a quiet pedestrian road and you don't hear any noise from Dalston Lane. There's a great community feel too. In the summer the kids rollerblade and play football outside

listings.title: Cosy, single-bedroom flat in Hackney, north-east London, with access to back garden
listings.description: My home is a comfortable flat in one of the most vibrant areas of London's inner city. Sliding doors between the kitchen and lounge enable the creation of a spacious area, with glazing at each end - one facing a typical urban side road on which the council  has installed traffic-calming measures, and the other onto a 33m-long, lovely shaded garden to which I have access. The bedroom, though small, has immediate access to a well-equiped bathroom. The flat, within a Victorian terrace, is on a corn
listings.area_description: My flat is in one of the most vibrant areas of London's inner city, close to good transport links, supermarkets, good restaurants, bars and clubs. The flat is on a quiet urban street, near the corner of a lovely urban park with a heated outdoor swimming pool.
listings.other_notes:
It is beside a lovely urban park and a 50m open-air, heated lido
It is close to good, quick transport links into central London
It has a lovely garden with mature trees.

listings.title: Beautiful two bedroom flat in quiet estate, close to Victoria Park and all of Hackney's wonders.

RECIPROCAL ONLY, including at different times. I have enough Guest Points, thanks, so please do not contact me if your place is never available.

Sunny, spacious split level flat with wonderful views onto communal gardens and the south-facing sky and trees of Victorial Park. We had tenants for a couple of months while I was away working who described the flat as 'full of soul'. 

Upstairs is a large, master bedroom with a comfortable double bed and workspace with desk and monitor. There is also
listings.area_description:
I know I'm biased but I think I live in the best neighbourhood in London.

3 minutes walk to the amazing, famous Victoria Park with a Sunday foodmarket. From there, access to Hackney Wick, the Olympic Park and Westfield and the beautiful peaceful canal towpaths of East London.

10 minutes walk  East to the shops and cafes of Lauriston Villabe. 10 minutes West to trendy Broadway Market and London Fields - another 15 minutes to bars of Shoreditch or Dalston.

12 minute walk to Bethnal Green tube S
listings.other_notes:
We will need £60 or EURO80  for the cleaner for each stay, payable when you arrive in cash.

Charming and bright 1-bed, 1-bath apartment in London’s Hackney, fully furbished, on the upper
ground floor with high ceilings and view of the lush garden.
Close to Hackney Central and Homerton stations, big offering of cafes, bars, restaurants, food
markets, yoga studios and gyms, and a nature reserve all in walking distance.
listings.area_description: The neighbourhood has a great mix of independent cafés, bars and restaurants, plus local food markets, yoga studios and gyms. You’re also moments from the beautiful Hackney Marshes, perfect for walks, runs or a bit of nature in the city.

listings.title: A cozy family home in Hackney
listings.description:
This family house has an open plan lounge-diner with doors and windows opening into a garden & separate fully equipped kitchen. 

On the first floor, there are 3 bedrooms: a double bed in the main bedroom, kids bunk beds in another room & a kids daybed in the 3rd small room. The kids beds are not suitable for adults. You also have a full bathroom on this floor. 

The house has plenty of light, it's very warm in winter and breezy in summertime. We are located at the heart of a very quiet close. O
listings.area_description:
It's a cosy oasis in the middle of buzzing Hackney, a stones throw from trendy Victoria Park Village & a 15 minute walk from the super hip London Fields & Broadway Market. 

We have all sort of public transports nearby that connects you with Central London,  with Shoreditch and Stratford. Our nearest tube station is Bethnal Green (13m walk - Central Line). The nearest Overground Station is Cambridge Heath (7 min Walk). 

If you like Hackney's parks this place is ideal for you and your family. A

listings.title: First-floor Edwardian (1909) apartment in fashionable Hackney
listings.description: Elegant, spacious first-floor Edwardian (1909) apartment in fashionable Hackney, NE London. (There is no lift.) Quiet street yet only a few minutes' walk from a number of Parkside pubs and walks, restaurants and shops and to lively Hackney, a transport centre to all London. (see home swap offer in Londo

It is very peaceful as most of my windows face the garden and during the day I get a lot of light into the home. During the weekend, only 7 minutes walk, the famous Broadway market opens for an array of different food/artistic/music vendors. Many restaurants, bars and pubs are very close to home (walking distance) and a 30 minute bus/train or even walking journey to one of London's most famous club/bar strip

listings.description: While our home is in the heart of Hackney, close to one of its most interesting markets, the overground and all the restaurants, it’s also a quiet haven tucked away from the madness.
listings.area_description: I live in Dalston Hackney two minutes from the train which takes you into King’s Cross in 20 minutes. Lots of shops, pubs and great local amenities around.
listings.other_notes: We charge £50 for cleaning.

listings.title: Leora and Sam's 2-bed apartment with stunning City views
listings.description:
Welcome to our cozy and modern 2-bedroom apartment on the 6th floor of a secure building in vibrant Homerton. 

Spanning approximately 50m², our home features a bright and airy living space, perfect for a couple or small family. One of the highlights is the incredible view of London’s skyline and the city lights - an experience that’s truly breathtaking, day or night. The open-plan kitchen and living area are thoughtfully designed, with all the essentials for a comfortable stay. The building is
listings.area_description: Homerton is a dynamic and diverse neighborhood in East London, offering a mix of trendy cafes, restaurants, and green spaces. You’ll find the charming Chatsworth Road Market nearby, known for its independent shops and weekend stalls. For nature lovers, Victoria Park and Hackney Marshes are just a short walk away, perfect for a stroll or picnic. Excellent transport links make it easy to reach central London and other iconic attractions, while the local area retains a relaxed, community-focused vi

Absolutely no smoking or vaping and while we love animals, none allowed in our home.

Located in vibrant and trendy Clapton in Hackney, East London, with transport links close by and lots of trendy cafes and restaurants a short walk away.

listings.area_description:

If going further afield it's very well connected, only one stop from Liverpool street station on the underground and also overground and bus options to get around.

In a very quiet location you can sleep well and relax.

listings.title: Yoko and Ian's private room

Enjoy our child friendly and child-full (!) flat in Haggerston, East London. The ideal location for exploring London, close to 2 overground stations, many key bus routes and multiple local parks and play grounds.

Haggerston is a quiet residential area between Shoreditch and Dalston, central London is 20 mins away and easily accessible by bus. Many local shops close by, and our favourite play ground just a few minutes away. 
The balcony looks out over an old bath house with stunning weather vane.

listings.title: Anna-Joy & Guy’s house

1 x double bed in master bedroom

listings.description:
Welcome to my flat and home!
I moved into this flat about two years ago, and since then it has become my most cherished place to relax and unwind. I rent it out when I go for long research trips out of the country, and hope you will enjoy it as much as I do.
listings.area_description: The flat's location in Hackney really offers the best of both worlds – a peaceful residential neighbourhood and easy access to London's bustling attractions. Just a few steps from the flat you will find so many cute and trendy cafes, vibrant street art, and unique boutiques. It's located right beside the canal which is perfect for a walk, and is 10 minutes away from Victoria Park, one of the most beautiful parks in London. During the weekend you can check out the market on Broadway Market and fi

details.full_description: We have lived in this part of London for 20 years and we have renovated the house ourselves over the years. It is a family home but could comfortably accommodate 3 couples. Original wooden floors and Victorian sash windows, working fireplace with wood in living room, beautiful secluded garden, lovely and quiet neighbours, beautiful morning sunshine in the master bedroom.
details.full_area_description: Hackney is in East London with the City of London to the west and the new Olympic site to the east. Homerton Tube station is a short walk from the house from which access to the West End is quick and easy. There are some lovely cafes, a deli and other shops just a short five minute walk from the house. Located in transport zone 2, the area is central enough to access all the classic London tourist sites but is also on the doorstep of the beautiful Lea Valley for canalside walks with a country feel. Culturally, Hackney is at the heart of the vibrant East end with more artists per square mile than anywhere else in Europe. All the latest trends in London's nightlife are to be found in nearby Shoreditch and Dalston. Local cuisine spans Turkish delights in Stoke Newington, through Caribbean flavours to good old fashioned Eel, Pie and Mash; and all three can be found in the wonderful Broadway Market. For the bargain hunter, all the best of London's market life is on offer:  Brick Lane bric-a-brac, Spittalfields' fashions, Columbia Road flower market, Broadway Farmers market,  and the real heart of multicultural Hackney at Ridley Road Market.
details.full_other:
London is the best place to live and Hackney is a fantastic neighbourhood. 
The house is light and our garden is peaceful

listings.area_description: Broadway Market in Hackney, East London, is a lively street market known for its mix of gourmet food, vintage fashion, and artisanal goods. My favorite part is the food section, where you can find everything from freshly baked pastries to international street food like banh mi and paella. The market’s vibrant atmosphere, combined with excellent coffee and unique stalls, makes it a perfect weekend destination.

details.full_description:
Escape to a serene, plant-filled oasis in the heart of vibrant East London. This large two-bedroom flat combines mid-century style with modern conveniences, including underfloor heating, Scandinavian windows, and smart appliances. Relax in the living room on a plush velvet sofa surrounded by lush greenery, original art, and treasures from our travels. Enjoy meals on a mid-century teak table with handmade dishes and eclectic glassware.
The flat is perfectly set up for work, with high-speed Wi-Fi and two dedicated workspaces. The master bedroom features a luxurious super king-sized bed with a deep green bedframe, plus a cleverly concealed workstation complete with a sit-stand desk, a walking pad, and a comfortable chair. The second bedroom offers a Japanese-style king-sized bed and another workspace with a desk and office chair.
For those who love to cook, the kitchen is fully equipped with everything you need, from modern appliances to cookware and utensils. The sleek bathroom includes a deep bath, shower, and heated towel rail for a touch of luxury.
Perfectly located in Zone 2, you'll have Michelin-starred dining, hole-in-the-wall eateries, markets, artists workshops, and parks on your doorstep, with Central London just 15-35 minutes away.
details.full_area_description:
Hackney is where East London's creativity and authenticity shine. Walk or bike to vibrant markets, world-renowned restaurants, and expansive parks, all while soaking in the area's unique energy.
Highlights include Michelin Guide resturant Angelina Dalton's Japanese-Italian fusion, Broadway Markets weekend stalls, and the iconic Columbia Road Flower Market.
For greenery, Hackney Downs Park is just 5 minutes away, with tennis courts, while London Fields offers picnic spots, and boutique studios like Disco Barre and BLOK for fitness enthusiasts. Insider favourites include Violets Bakery for indulgent treats and rooftop bars like Night Tales Loft for stunning views.
Should you want to explore more, unbeatable transport links (Hackney Downs, Hackney Central, and Dalston Junction) take you to Liverpool Street, Oxford Street, and South London with ease. And with Alysha's restaurant guide and insider tips, your Hackney adventure will be unforgettable.
details.full_other:
We kindly ask you to pay £45 for the cleaning.

We only have one last availability in our current home: reciprocal exchange - 10 days between 16-31st July - we’re looking for a 2+ bedroom home with outdoor space, fast wifi and near cultural attractions and food. (Not available for guest points, sorry!)

We are selling the flat and will likely be moving to a new home in September. We will be moving to a similar neighbourhood in a 2+ bedroom home with our same style. If you’d like to stay in touch for exchanges later in the year please do, although we won’t be able to confirm anything until later. 

Thank you!

details.full_description:
PLEASE NOTE:

1. MINIMUM STAY FOR GUEST POINTS IS SEVEN NIGHTS (WITH NO EXCEPTIONS).

2. FOR GUEST POINT STAYS THERE IS A CLEANING FEE OF £80

3. WHEN ENQUIRING, PLEASE CONFIRM THAT YOU HAVE READ THE INFORMATION RE NOISE, BOTH SURROUNDING THE FLAT AND ALSO THE NEED TO BE RESPECTFUL OF MY NEIGHBOURS. BECAUSE OF THE NOISE SENSIVITY ISSUES,  I AM RELUCTANT TO TAKE BOOKINGS FROM
PEOPLE WITH YOUNG CHILDREN. SORRY!

My relatively newly-renovated (January 2018) three-bedroom flat is at the heart of the trendy London Fields neighbourhood, with a good-sized fourth-floor balcony directly overlooking the park, very near the famous London Fields Lido (a 50 metre heated outdoor pool)  and also Broadway Market, with Columbia Road flower market and the city also very 'do-able' walks from here.

PLEASE NOTE BEFORE BOOKING: I was going to call this section "Reasons not to book" but actually since I also started letting the flat out on Airbnb everybody who has been to stay so far has really liked both the flat and the location, as reflected in the 50+ five-star reviews I've had. 

However, just as I like to be fully informed before I book anywhere, please bear in mind that this an ex-local authority flat. My neighbours tell me that the estate was originally built in the 1970s by a private developer but they ran out of money and so it was taken over as social housing by Hackney Council, the local authority. 

This perhaps explains why it’s not your typical ‘council estate’. It’s right next to the park, and with wide walkways and facing front doors which make for a great community spirit (we have regular street parties here and there’s a lot of friendly interaction between neighbours). 

Many (now more than half) of the flats are now privately owned. But it is still a council estate which is something I like to make clear before people book. 

Also, please note the following

1. CHILDREN. The flat has a 4th floor balcony overlooking the park. As you can see from the pictures, it has a proper wall and safety rail but if you have the kind of children with a tendency to explore/climb unsupervised, it might not be the place for you. 
2. GURGLING! A quirk of the plumbing means that water running through the pipes at night can produce a low gurgling sound which can be particularly annoying if you are sleeping in the small third bedroom on the sofa bed. 
3. NOISE. The wonderful location of the flat (directly overlooking London Fields park) means that it is, for the most part, very peaceful and relaxing. However, especially in summer, you sometime get people walking back from the pubs on Broadway Market and making a bit of a racket. And on very hot nights, when people have been on the park all day, you might hear chatting, singing and even drumming etc before they disperse. 
Also, the rubbish/recycling collections for this estate are often as early as 6.30am on weekdays, and this can also be very noisy, since the flat is right next to the bin area. And my neighbour's children often play noisily out on the walkway below the flat during the summer.

So.. that all gives an impression of a constant cacophony. It's really not - most of the time it's very restful here - but just want potential guests to be fully informed! 

And despite all of what I've just written, I have neighbours to one side and below me and this is most certainly not a party flat. Because of the acoustics in the block any noise made in my flat can sound louder than you might expect to my neighbours. Please only make a booking enquiry if you're confident that you are a quiet and respectful small groups who wil be sensitive to my neighbours' need for peace and quiet. 

If all of that hasn't put you off.. here's a bit more information. The flat is arranged over three floors: 1. It has its entrance (the front door is on a raised walkway, with a single flight of stairs leading up to it from the ground level). Stairs to... 2. first floor with open-plan living room and kitchen, toilet, Stairs to.. 3. Second floor, with three bedrooms (two double, one single, and a bathroom. Guests have the entire flat to themselves.
details.full_area_description:
This part of the East London Borough of Hackney, where every railway arch has been turned into a brewery, cafe or even a cinema, is well-known as the hipster heart of London and it's never boring. Some things I love about living here:
1. In medieval times, London Fields was the last grazing ground for sheep being driven into the city's abattoirs. From the park, they went down nearby Broadway Market (just a few minutes walk from my flat), now home to a bustling Saturday market visited by people from far and wide. To quote their website: "Broadway Market has been home to market traders since the 1890s, and provides a unique kaleidoscope of tastes and cultures. Visit us and you will find independent shops, pubs, restaurants and cafes and of course stalls offering amazing fresh produce, authentic street food, the most original clothing, arts and crafts in London. All crammed into a little East End street between the Regent’s Canal and London Fields.
2. The canal at the bottom of Broadway Market. Cuts through a wonderful urban landscape and takes you to nearby Victoria Park, another huge green urban space built by Queen Victoria as 'the lungs of London' Along the way, you'll see colourful barges converted into cafes and even bookshops and you'll be in the company of runners, walkers, cyclists... everyone loves the canal!
3. London Fields Lido. My flat is just two minutes walk from this amazing heated outdoor pool, a breathtaking (literally!) 50 metres long.
4. Restaurants. The area has everything from F Cooke, an authentic East End 'pie and mash' shop (not my thing, but each to his own) to the Michelin-starred Ellory (five minutes walk). My favourites include the fish and chip shop on Broadway Market, Sunday lunch at the Dove, and pizza/pasta at the incredibly noisy but always fun Bella Vita. Or further afield, about 25 minutes walk, the strip of excellent Vietnamese restaurants at the bottom of Kingsland Road. All are staggering distance back to the flat. Or, if you want to just set out and see what takes your eye, Broadway Market never disappoints: everything from Argentinian to Japanese.
5. Star quality. You'll often see film crews on location in this part of Hackney, frequently in London Fields itself. 
6. The public transport (see getting around).

- Buzzy restaurants: From the Michelin-starred Ellory, to the cool hipster rooftop bar at Neil House, to great pizzerias on Broadway Market, and pop-up restaurants opening all the time, there's never a shortage of interesting things to eat and drink.
details.full_other:
Apart from being at the heart of the very trendy and cool London Fields area (a destination in its own right) the flat is within easy reach of many favourite places for visitors. For example, public transport travel times to:

St Paul’s Cathedral (29 mins)
Tower of London (35 mins)
Oxford Circus (34 mins)
Buckingham Palace: (49 mins)
Theatre district (Shaftesbury Avenue) (36 mins)
Soho (36 mins)

details.full_description:
My home is my wonderful sanctuary, its peaceful and calm, yet surrounded by East London life and the design echoing my joy of colour and texture. Built in late 2020 and located on the ground floor, it's a very spacious brand new duplex apartment, with wheelchair access on the top floor. My home is maintained in immaculate condition. I own a small adult road bike, which is welcome for cycling. 

All main rooms boast double patio door access to allow for great ventilation and light. The apartment is well insulated and very rarely (really never) requires the use of central heating. 

There is no private parking, but there is on-street paid parking all around, which you can purchase Hackney Council residents parking tickets in advance, if desired. 

----------------------------------------------------

Located on the TOP FLOOR, although still accessed from street level within the complex courtyard, which cars can drive within meters of the front door: 

LIVING ROOM: 3 seater red velvet sofa (can be used as sofa bed), gorgeous green loveseat, flatscreen TV, my Mum's old record player and record collection, my little library of books and disco ball. Move the coffee table to the side and it makes a great space for a couple of people to practice morning yoga as the light comes through the patio doors. 

PRIVATE PATIO: The property is accessed by its private patio area which overlooks the communal courtyard, offering a grassed area for children to play safely in sight, with comfy conversation seating area, which is perfect for morning coffee. 

KITCHEN: Marble work tops and all the mod cons, great for entertaining and well equipped to host small dinners. Includes nutribullet and portable bluetooth speaker. 

BEDROOM 1: Queen bed, Juliet balcony and lots of space. 

BATHROOM 1: Communal access from upstairs hallway boasting full-size bath with shower. 

----------------------------------------------------

Located on the LOWER FLOOR: 

BEDROOM 2: Master with King size bed, lots of space and 3 patio doors leading on to private back patio. 

BATHROOM 2: Ensuite boasting full-size bath with shower. 

UTILITY ROOM: Ample space for storage and washer/dryer. 

I love my home and to welcome people into it - I hope you love it too.

Cleaning Fee - £150 for non-reciprocal exchanges
details.full_area_description:
I live in the heart of vibrant and wonderful Hackney, located in East London. Creativity, community, colour and life ooze from this much loved area of London, both day and night. Home to many young creatives and families the area boasts a mass of cultural and social offerings, with tons of green space and the canal located within a short walking distance. The eclectic culture within the area has given rise to a plethora of independent businesses, very popular with music lovers, art enthusiasts and foodies. The area has brilliant transport links and all essential amenities a stone's throw away. There's loads to explore and be entertained by in the local area, with easy links to venture wider London. If you wish to lay low, the area and my home doesn't impose the fast London city pace on you either, it can be your sanctuary too. 

There are incredible restaurants aplenty from Michelin starred to 24 hr fast-food, great east-end boozers (pubs) to top world ranked cocktails. 

Hackney Central is a 7 min walk offering a large Tesco supermarket, Marks & Spencer, cinema, cafes, bars, world food supermarkets and organic stores. 

Much desired Chatsworth Road is a 7 min walk offering a lovely Sunday Market, with the marshes, canal and Wetlands located behind it. London Fields is a 10 min walk in the other direction linking to Broadway Market and Columbia Road Flower Market. 

Homerton Overground - 6 min walk 
Hackney Central Overground - 7 min walk 
Victoria Park - 20 min walk / 10 min cycle 
Kings Cross - 30 min tube / 30 min cycle 
Oxford Circus - 36 min tube / 35 min cycle 
Liverpool Street Station - 25 min tube / 55 min walk / 22 min cycle 
Olympic Village Stadium / Stratford - 45 min walk / 20 min cycle 
London City Airport - 30 min cab / drive 
Homerton Hospital - 7 min walk
details.full_other:
Children are welcome, but I would be mindful of the staircase and suggest it's not ideal for small children. 

Please note the sofa bed is a vintage 70's pull out, so I would suggest it would be preferred for small children or minimise to one nights sleep for adults or best just one adult!

The house sleeps 7, has 4 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms. The front room has a wood burner and TV and there is a family kitchen opening up to the garden
There are fun cafes/pubs/shops around the corner and it is 30 mins on the train to Central London.
listings.area_description: We live just off Chatsworth Road. It is a fun and cosmopolitan area in London with nice cafes, pubs and restaurants. Hackney marshes are a 5 mins walk away - great for nice walks along the canal or letting your dog off lead
listings.other_notes:
We are looking for a dog friendly exchange for Easter 2024.

Somewhere with beautiful nature and maybe a pool

listings.title: Oliver & Ella's house
listings.description:
Our home is over two floors, with two very good sized bedrooms, one of which also has desk space and we use as our office,  but it also has a double bed.

The apartment benefits from a lot of natural light, which our (many) plants love. Both bedrooms, and our downstairs living spaces get great sunlight as you can see from our photos. 

We also have a large balcony that somehow always feels much warmer than at street level - great for an evening glass of wine.

There is a king size bed in the mas
listings.area_description:
The location is fantastic: we are a few minutes walk from both London fields and Victoria park, and about 12 mins walk from Broadway Market.  

There are LOTS of bars and restaurants in the local area, many of them very trendy and highly recommended. 

Ella and I are both vegan, and can recommend options for plant based and non vegan options.

listings.title: Elise's house
listings.description:
Bright and peaceful flat on the upper floors of period conversion with pretty balcony, ideal for families, couples or friends, close to London Fields Park and Victoria Park, Broadway Market, Victoria Park Village, the canal, endless restaurants, coffee shops, pubs, bars and markets, with excellent transport links to all parts of London.

May be able to accommodate up to 4 people plus a small child with 2 on single sofa beds, a crib and 2 in double bedroom. 

The space

Our lovely flat is i
listings.area_description: The flat is located in the heart of Hackney, close to London Fields Park and Victoria Park, Broadway Market, Victoria Park Village, the canal, endless restaurants, coffee shops, pubs, bars and markets, with excellent transport links to all parts of London.

listings.title: Raquel's house
listings.description:
Spacious 1-Bedroom Flat - Ideal for Families

Our roomy 1-bedroom flat comfortably accommodates up to 4 guests. Perfect for families with children, it features a bedroom with a double bed and a kids' bed that I can disassemble if needed . Enjoy direct access to the terrace from both the living room and the bedroom.
listings.area_description:
Located on a quiet street in the heart of the unique and diverse Dalston neighborhood, this flat offers the perfect blend of tranquility and vibrant city life. 
Known for its lively music scene, events, the popular Art Deco Rio Cinema and nightlife, Dalston is packed with restaurants, supermarkets, street markets, and many stores, including the newly reopened luxury design concept store LN-CC—all within a 5-minute walk from the flat. 
Additionally, you can easily walk to London Fields, Broadway

listings.title: East London Gem - Private bedroom with own bathroom and city views!
listings.description: Stunning double room with en-suite.  You will be sharing this penthouse apartment with two friendly roommates.  You will have your own private en-suite (bathroom), with access to all shared living areas (spacious living/dining areas with 2 balconies, plus separate kitchen with all the amenities).
listings.area_description:
Dalston is a vibrant community with trendy restaurants, cafes and access to a number of parks and markets. An extremely well connected location, with transport hubs Dalston Junction and Dalston Kingsland at your doorstop - as well as an abundance of lime bikes.

Broadway market is a famous weekend market which is a 10minute walk away, and famous parks nearby include London Fields, Clissold Park and Victoria Park.

listings.title: Spacious 3-bedroom flat in East London
listings.description:
Welcome to our 3-bedroom flat in the heart of one of London's most vibrant neighbourhoods, Dalston. 

Just a stone's throw away from fantastic bars, restaurants and nightlife, while still being on a relatively quiet street, with the overground a 3 minute walk away that can take you anywhere in London.

We've decorated our place with love and feel free to enjoy a cozy living room with a 120 inch projector for movie nights, a lovely dining area and the best equipped kitchen you'll find around (we
listings.area_description:
Dalston is one of London's trendiest neighborhoods, with fantastic restaurants, bars, nightlife, vintage shops and areas to explore. We're also a five minute walk from one of London's best bakeries, where you can enjoy delicious baked goods. 

Just a fifteen minute walk is London Fields, a great green space with a heated lido you can swim in, and Broadway Marker, where you can sample delicious street food.

The rest of London is also at your fingertips, with the overground which is 3 minutes fro

listings.title: Elegant East End Apartment
listings.description: Ours is a brand new build elegant apartment in the heart of happening Hackney - 5 mins walk to leafy London Fields and bustling Broadway market.  This intimate yet roomy 110 sq/m apartment has been furnished in a comfortably modern mid-century manner with 1 bedroom en-suite, 1 additional bathroom,  1 bedroom with a comfortable sofa and workstation a 3rd bedroom for 2.  The living room is light, airy and open plan and opens to a balcony with 2 sets of table & chairs. A great base to explore all t
listings.area_description:
Located in tranquil and intimate Hackney Square our flat is in the heart of Hackney - a great place to explore all that this diverse and comprehensively complex urban quarter has to offer 

- Buzzy restaurants: Lardo, Raw Duck, Institute of Light, Ellory, Pidgin, Bistrotheque

- Bit of culture: Viner Street Galleries
