listings.title: Home in the heart of Hackney
listings.description:
Currently only looking for a two-week to 1-month reciprocal exchange to New York City in November.

Two-floor airy family home with a sunken front garden. We have a lovely lodger Cody who has to enter through our home to reach his flat in the loft conversion. We are publishers who have lived here for over two decades and we've poured a lot of love into our home, we hope you'll find it warm and inviting.

There is an office that you are more than welcome to use and plenty of kitchen utensils for
listings.area_description:
Hackney is a fun, creative and buzzing neighbourhood - you'll never get bored here. What's more it's really easy to access central London so you can experience both aspects of the city. 

The thing we love most about our neighbourhood is the green spaces, just five minutes walk away you'll find Victoria Park (east London's best park, we promise we're not biased) and London Fields (which has an outdoor swimming pool). It is a very lively area and you'll find a lot of fun nightlife with a diversit

listings.title: Three bed home in trendy Clapton, East London
listings.description: Welcome to our casa! We're a combo of a Mexican + British marraige and our house reflects that. Proper Victorian house with Mexican colour and fun! It's a lovely three bedroom house (master bedroom, one of the bedrooms is a kids room, the other can have a king size blow up mattress in it or stay as an office). Huge modern kitchen, two front rooms and a lovely backyard with a fire pit.
listings.area_description:
We're steps away from Chatsworth Rd with it's indie bars and resteraunts (and great market on a Sunday), with a 7 minute walk to the Mildmay Line that connects to all London has to offer. 

Also a walk away from the canal that will lead you to the Olypmic Village and all the new shops and sports venues in Stratford. 

It's quiet and green, but plugged into so much great East London life and then all of London from there.

listings.title: NO MORE EXCHANGES FOR 2024!!! Modern stylish one bedroom apartment in trendy East London
listings.description:
My place is a brand new one bedroom apartment on the first floor of a small private block situated in a very quite little road in Dalston. 

It's an excellent location as it's less than 2 mins walk from two stations and several bus stops, you can be in the heart of central London and all the tourist spots in just 20 mins. The Olympic park is also only 15 mins away.

There's a shopping mall just at the end of my road and the high street with all its shops, pubs and restaurant is also a 2 mins wal
listings.area_description:
Dalston is part of the borough of Hackney, in East London (zone 2, considered central), and it's the place where all the artists, musicians, designers and generally the whole of London's creative industries are based. Even the Guardian has described it as "the coolest place in Britain".
It's been undergoing a huge regeneration in the past few years (especially due to its proximity to the Olympic area) and it now boasts lots of trendy cafes, restaurants, music venues, art galleries, theatres and
listings.other_notes:
it's in the most exciting cultural neighbourhood in London
there are great transport links to everywhere
it's next to fantastic restaurants, pubs and music venues

listings.title: Rory's house
listings.description: Light filled apartment on the 6th floor of a modern building. Well set up for a city break to explore London or to remote work. A super king bed and rainfall shower head make this a comfy space. Large terrace with views of the London skyline. Very well connected to East London, the City Centre and lots of nearby green spaces. The building has a lift, concierge and requires a fob for entry.
listings.area_description: I live in the middle of East London - very close to the buzzy neighbourhoods of Clapton, London Fields/Broadway Market, Newington Green and Stoke Newington. Victoria Park and Hackney Downs park are nearby. Many of London's best restaurants are between 10-20 mins walk away. Two Overground lines  can take you into the city or over to West London in 30 mins or so.

listings.title: Abi's house
listings.description: My home is very important to me as this is my first one. I would like to think I've designed it down to my style and the interior design I like whilst it still being livable and social.
listings.area_description: I live in the heart of east london, next to the best restaurants and pubs, and walks away from london fields and victoria parks. On the weekends theres plenty of stuff to do close by including markets and bar hopping/gigs. Its got great transport links, with transport able to take you to west london in 30 minutes and south west in 45.

listings.title: Kyla's house - not available in 2024
listings.description:
This is our comfortable relaxed family home that guests will share with our 3 cats, Mr Binx, Tiger and Simba.

A typical Victorian house in quiet residential area, the house  comfortably sleeps 6.  We also have a large basement play room that is perfect for children to hang out in - they have their own TV and dining table down there.  The playroom has a double sofa bed that can be used to sleep an additional 2 guests.  Please note that it doesn't have a chest of drawer or wardrobe - it is not ad
listings.area_description: Our  house is in the heart of a vibrant and friendly East London community.   You will have street markets, parks, cafes, canal walks, museums, restaurants and events to explore in the local area, and can also be in Central London within 35 minutes.
listings.other_notes:
Local Area:

2 min walk from Chatsworth Road, the local high street with independent food shops, delis, restaurants, cafes and weekly food and craft Sunday Street Market

2–5 min walk to favourite local pubs

10 min walk from large supermarket

10 min walk to Lee Valley park and waterway and Hackney

listings.title: Private room in 3-bedroom terraced house (looking for reciprocal exchange from 16-21 October in Amsterdam)
listings.description: My room is the main bedroom in a 3-bedroom Victorian terrace house in the heart of Hackney, east London. It is a lovely big room that is inundated with morning sunlight, and there is a lot of space to store clothes and other items.
listings.area_description: I live on the Lower Clapton/Homerton border, close to lots of shopping amenities, Hackney Marshes and the canal, Victoria Park and excellent transport connections, with lots of buses and a train station within a 5-10 min walk. Hackney is considered one of the most vibrant and exciting neighbourhoods in London, with a plethora of pubs, bars, clubs, restaurants and cafes right on the doorstep.

listings.title: Modern and cosy flat with amazing view of London
listings.description:
This beautiful and very luminous double-bedroom flat benefits from a magnificent view of London, thanks to its location on the 10th floor. There is a lovely wraparound balcony, offering panoramic views and stunning sunsets that never disappoint.

I love plants and may have more than one person really needs. :) The flat has a washing machine, dishwasher, and all the essentials you'd expect to find in a modern kitchen. The building also features its own gym (with free access) and a bike garage.
listings.area_description:
Hackney is a vibrant, multicultural, and trendy area with excellent transport links. You can reach Liverpool Street in just 10 minutes, where you'll find all the underground and train connections you need to explore the city further.

There are so many super cute cafes and small restaurants nearby (I'd be more than happy to share my recommendations). I love this neighborhood so much, I couldn't imagine living anywhere else.

You'll have everything you need right at your doorstep. One of my favor

listings.title: Zaisha Hackney Sanctuary
listings.description:
My 1-bed garden flat is perfect for plant lovers and has a real peaceful vibe about it. 

Guests will have full private access to the living room/kitchen with a beautiful bay window (great for people watching), one double bedroom with plenty of clothes storage, bathroom and a nice sized private garden.
listings.area_description:
The flat is located in the trendy Lower Clapton just 2 minutes walk to Chatsworth Road  with many cute cafes, coffee shops, concept stores & restaurants. On Sundays they have a lovely street food and crafts market which is very buzzy. Lower Clapton Road is also 2 minutes walk away with great restaurants & a wonderful sourdough bread bakery.

Within 10 minutes walk you can get to Millfields Park, Hackney Downs, River Lee and Hackney Marshes (which has some of the most beautiful nature in London)

listings.title: Light-Filled Hackney Maisonette with Rooftop Access
listings.description: Nestled in the vibrant heart of Hackney, this delightful two-storey stacked maisonette offers the perfect blend of authentic East London living and thoughtfully curated comfort. The ground floor features two cosy bedrooms and a full bathroom, whilst the second floor opens into a stunning open-plan kitchen and living area crowned by a dramatic skylight that floods the entire house with natural light throughout the day. A unique foldable staircase provides access to the roof, creating the perfect
listings.area_description: The location is absolutely perfect, situated right by the historic Hackney Church with lovely local shops on your doorstep, and just a few minutes' walk from Chatsworth Road where you'll discover a beautiful market brimming with fresh produce, artisanal goods, and local treasures, plus an independent cinema for cosy evening entertainment. It's ideal for travellers who appreciate character, culture, and the authentic neighbourhood feel of one of London's most exciting areas.
listings.other_notes: The house sleeps four comfortably and includes fresh linens, towels, and a well-equipped kitchen with everything needed for proper home cooking. Fast WiFi throughout means you can easily stay connected, whilst the quiet residential street ensures peaceful nights despite being in the heart of bustlin

listings.title: Floriane's house
listings.description:
Our home is the perfect blend of comfort and space, offering a warm and inviting atmosphere where you’ll feel right at home from the moment you walk through the door! 

Our home offers generous living space, with large rooms that are cozy yet airy. Whether you’re enjoying a quiet evening in the living room or cooking up a meal in the well-equipped kitchen, you'll find it easy to unwind and feel at ease.

We’re confident that you’ll enjoy your time here and feel right at home, just as we do.
listings.area_description: We’re lucky to live in a neighborhood that’s both vibrant and peaceful. You’ll love the friendly, welcoming atmosphere here, with plenty of parks, cafes, and local shops nearby to explore. It's the kind of neighborhood that feels like a community. The best London vibes !

listings.title: Family friendly home in London Fields
listings.description:
Our home is a classic Victorian terrace nestled in the heart of London Fields, Hackney — one of London’s most vibrant and creative neighbourhoods. The house combines original period charm with modern comforts, featuring high ceilings, wooden floors, and plenty of natural light.

Downstairs, there’s a spacious living room  that leads onto a leafy garden. The kitchen is simple but functional, with everything you need for easy home cooking. Upstairs you’ll find comfortable bedrooms and modern bathr
listings.area_description:
London Fields is one of East London’s most sought-after neighbourhoods — a lively, green, and creative pocket of Hackney with a real community feel. The area centres around the beautiful London Fields Park, complete with tennis courts, a lido for year-round outdoor swimming, and plenty of space for picnics or weekend strolls.

Just around the corner is Broadway Market, a bustling weekend market lined with independent cafes, bakeries, and vintage shops — perfect for brunch or browsing local produ

listings.title: Bright 2 bed flat in a quiet residential area of central London
listings.description:
Cosy and well distributed apartment in central London Zone 2.
65 sqm
3rd floor with elevator.
Pretty and vibrant part of East London, with many bars, restaurants and cool spots.

The flat is very practical , cosy and silent,. Facing west so flooded with daylight. 
- living room with a very practical and well equipped open kitchen, and balcony facing west
- one master bedroom with a comfy queen size bed and ensuite bathroom with shower
- a separate room that we use as our office
- a separate bath
listings.area_description: It's on a leafy quiet street, very close to several green spaces. We are on the trendy side of London Zone 2, full of interesting restaurants and bars, and very close to the centre: 20 mins to Oxford Circus, 15 minutes to Liverpool street and the Financial City. Regent's canal is next door: a beautiful relaxing stroll that connects some very exciting neighbourhoods. Columbia road Flower Market, Brick Lane and Shoreditch are a short walk away.

listings.title: Nicole's house
listings.description: Lovely three-bedroom family room in the heart of Hackney. Suitable for a couple or a young family. Not suitable for more than two adults. Downstairs is huge - sliding doors connecting living room to open plan, marble worktop kitchen and airy dining room leading out into large private garden. Upstairs has a large double bedroom with private balcony, plus two kids bedrooms with bunkbeds.
listings.area_description: Seconds from bars, restaurants, food markets, parks, art galleries, cinemas and the overland - we're in the heart of Hackney, east London.

listings.title: Bright and Spacious Modern Haven in the heart of London
listings.description:
In the sought after Navarino Grove, our flat is a peaceful place in Hackney, London. It offers modern lateral living, with a large open plan reception, floor to ceiling windows and wooden flooring. The development is gated and surrounded by greenery and sunlight. It has quality finishes throughout such as underfloor heating, quartz countertop and a lovely South-West exposed balcony. There is plenty of storage, all the amenities you may need and a bike storage in the courtyard.


Please note that
listings.area_description: Navarino Grove is ideally located between the green spaces and Overground stations. It is a 10 minutes walk to join Hackney Down, Hackney Central and Dalston. Only 3min away from the park London Fields, the local neighbourhood is vibrant and welcoming with its independent deli, bakery coffee shop and home to one of the best food market in London. Walking further along the canal, we may enjoy the greenery of Victoria Park, the street art in Shoreditch or cosy areas in Angel.

listings.title: Liza's house
listings.description: Our home is a beautiful traditional Victorian house. Set over three floors with a large garden it’s a house with many features that are over 150 years old. The large bathroom with a traditional bath is a real winner and the kitchen area leads onto a big garden.
listings.area_description: We live in Victoria Park area in London. The area the Sunday Times voted the best area to live in in London. It’s a really beautiful area with access to the great nightlife and restaurants of Hackney but also incredibly peaceful. We are about 100 meters from the park and we are there most days. It’s got two very big playgrounds, tennis courts, cricket batting cage, a boating pond and one of the best park cafes in London. Our road is a cul-de-sac leading to the park so it’s very quite

listings.title: apartment in London, United Kingdom
listings.description: My home has beautiful views over a park and the skyline and is south facing with lots of sunlight
listings.area_description:
The park with its markets and festivals

Surrounded by fantastic restaurants and bars and cafés

Great connections to every part of the city and completely central
listings.other_notes: This is one of London’s most vibrant, eclectic and artistic neighbourhoods with lots of history. Food, fashion, art, music and wine - it’s all going on in Hackney!

listings.title: Large 3 bedroom family home in east London
listings.description: Our spacious Victorian family home is in the vibrant neighbourhood of Dalston, East London. It has a large kitchen diner, a separate living room, 2 bathrooms and 3 large double bedrooms all simply decorated with lots of original features. Given the large communal kitchen dinner there is no shortage of space should you wish to use the living room & sofa bed as a fourth bedroom. This is an ideal place for families with children but also smaller groups of extended family members or friends.
listings.area_description:
Dalston is a vibrant multi-cultural neighbourhood with lots of cafes, bars & restaurants. There's a bustling market nearby for fruit and veg as well as an arthouse cinema, The RiO. Transport into Central London is close by as well as some of East London's other main attractions such as Broadway Farmer's Market, Brick Lane & Columbia Flower Market


It is a short walk to the Dalston Kingsland Overground station that connects to the Underground at Highbury & Islington. Central London, therefore
listings.other_notes:
It is very comfortable & spacious
It has a nice big garden
There's a nice big kitchen for spending time together

listings.title: Spacious maisonette flat in the heart of Hackney with private garden

My flat is ideal for anyone keen to explore East London.
The flat is very spacious. It has a large bedroom with a king size bed, two living rooms, a small office/guest room and a separate kitchen as well.
listings.area_description:
Hackney is one of the most vibrant areas of London.
It boasts a seemingly unlimited offering of great restaurants, bars, pubs and coffee shops
Victoria Park is just a stones throw away from my flat.
In addition, the flat is very well connected to the transport system via overground, bus and tube.

listings.title: EMMA's beautifully appointed flat, in the heart of vibrant Hackney is a great spot to explore London
listings.description: Welcome to our home. The flat is on the ground floor of an Edwardian building so is full of charm with plenty of period features while having all the creature comforts you might need during your stay.

We are only a few minutes walk to local parks and green spaces (Hackney Downs, Clapton Park, Hackney Marshes etc). The rest of Hackney, London Fields and Stoke Newington are all no more than a 20 or so min

listings.title: Bright & happy Hackney home
listings.description:
Our East London home is a place where people feel good. It's charming, spacious, filled with natural light and plants. We've got an awesome original 50s kitchen, loads of happy green plants and a massive balcony. The flat is full of beautiful things to feed your eyes. We've got big sofa and projector for movies too (bring you own laptop). The kitchen/ living room is open plan, then there are 2 bedrooms, a playroom with double sofa bed, & 1 bathroom over 2 floors. 

Our home is an apartment in a
listings.area_description: Just walk 2 mins to the train station to get to London's tourist sites. It's 30 mins on public trans or bike to all central London has to offer. Or stay local in London's best neighbourhood! Hackney is packed with unique restaurants, bars, clubs, cultural and family facilities. There's a park opposite our block with an awesome new playground and more big parks 10 & 20 mins away, an outdoor lido 10 mins away and a beautiful swimming reservoir 20 mins away. We're 10 mins walk to Broadway market, 2
listings.other_notes:
The flat is on the first floor with stairs and no lift. 

NB 2 of our beds are small kids beds - suitable up to age 7 I think. The sofa bed is more comfortable for 1 adult than 2. You can sleep 2 but it's quite small. So we'd recommend the flat for max 2 adults and 3 kids or 3 adults and 2 small kid

listings.title: 2 bed flat in the heart of Hackney, East London.
listings.description:
2 large bedroom flat recently renovated (and always being tweaked). Flat come with separate toilet room, shower room, kitchen, living room and a small balcony.
Well connected to Weaver and Mildmay overground lines with many buses connecting to central London.
listings.area_description: Hackney has everything you need, lots of nice bars and restaurants a few minutes walk from the flat. East London has a great nightlife and activities on your doorstep. I have two big supermarkets within a 10 min walk, small kids playground beside the flat, Victoria Park and London Fields both a few minutes away. Broadway Market has a weekend market every week as does Victoria Park on a Sunday. Hop into central for all of London's classic sight seeing.
listings.other_notes: I have two bicycles, one older bike that can be left unattended for long periods of time and one that will need to be secure parked for long periods of time. Either can be made available for guests on request.

listings.title: Dalston Townhouse
listings.description:
Our home is a spacious, recently renovated four bedroom, four storey townhouse in the heart of Dalston, East London.

Set on a quiet, leafy street, it’s just a four minute walk to Dalston Kingsland station and all the bars and restaurants that make the neighbourhood buzz. 

Inside, you’ll find a large kitchen living space that spans the entire first floor, a cozy TV room and a separate office. The real gem is our large garden - perfect for relaxing after a day of exploring London.
listings.area_description:
Our road is a quiet and peaceful retreat from the busy and vibrant streets of Dalston. 

From famous cheap eats to Michellin-star restaurants,  our neighbourhood has it all. 

We are within a short walk of Dalston Junction and Dalston Kingsland stations as well as multiple bus stops providing easy access to all of London.

listings.title: Linnea's house
listings.description: My home is a cute house that offers peace for the mind. It offers a quiet rooftop, big living room with sofa and dining table, also a big TV. Three big white bedrooms and 2 toilets, and one good sized fully equipped kitchen.
listings.area_description: My home is close reach to the very cute cafes and shops, that east London can offer.  Good transportation with both buses and tube minutes walk away. The flower market is just 20 minutes away and close by lovely parks can be discovered.

listings.title: Beautiful London family home
listings.description:
Our spacious & luxurious light-filled home in leafy Victoria Park Village, East London is spread over three floors and perfect for family groups.  We are next to one of London's nicest parks with cafes, boating lake, adventure playgrounds etc... with markets, coffee shops, boutiques, galleries and famous restaurants on the doorstep, and excellent transport links to anywhere in London - Oxford St is 15 minutes on the tube.

Downstairs is very open with wonderful wooden parquet floors throughout
listings.area_description:

listings.title: Sunny 2 bedroom house in trendy London neighbourhood
listings.description:
A warm, arty terraced hourse, decorated with a lot of love and care, on a quiet street in leafy London Fields

Upstairs there are two big bedrooms, with large windows overlooking the street and gardens, a jazzy bathroom with a large bath, and a smaller back office.  Downstairs is mainly open plan, with a large space leading down to a warm inviting kitchen and onto a back lawn and terrace. There is also a small well appointed utility room. 

Great for relaxing, working, and finding solace in the
listings.area_description: In the heart of the popular London Fields neighbourhood - very close to the outdoor lido, the parks and playgrounds, and the pubs and shops of Broadway Market, but situated on a peaceful residential street!

listings.title: Hackney Flat
listings.description:
Welcome to our flat! 

There are two bedrooms (one is used as a study), a fully equipped kitchen, and a spacious living room with loads of natural light.

Located on the 2nd floor of an Edwardian terrace in the heart of Hackney.
listings.area_description:
Located in the heart of Hackney. Great transport links all over the city, just a 5 minute walk to the nearest train station (Hackney Downs). A 2 minute walk to the park with cafes, restaurants and shops right on your doorstep.

Short walk to Dalston, Hackney Central and Stoke Newington.

listings.title: Victoria's house in London
listings.description:
PLEASE NOTE: only one bedroom is available if you’re using guest points, so a maximum of two guests. No last-minute additions. (There are reasonably priced hotels nearby.)
Hi! I’m Victoria, I work as an editor and I live in London Fields, in Hackney, in a spacious flat with roof terrace and garden, just a stone’s throw from the local lido and also very near loads of lovely delis, bars, restaurants and pubs. There are also very good transport connections to get to central London or wherever else.
listings.area_description: Fun and vibrant neighbourhood with plenty of exciting people around. Nice neighbours. Lots of trees and light. Excellent restaurants, bakeries, pubs and cafes nearby and groovy London Fields park right on the doorstep. Broadway Market a five-minute walk across the park. Plenty of travel links - overground and buses. A 7-minute train hop from Liverpool St Station. Open-air heated swimming pool two minutes’ walk away, two good playgrounds within five minutes’ walk.
listings.other_notes: I live with Toby, an affectionate neutered male tabby who was a rescue cat from Cyprus. He arrived with me in late 2020 and is an outdoor type but he likes someone staying here when I go away. He is very low maintenance and comes in and out as he likes but he will need feeding every day and a bit of

listings.title: Anna's private room SORRY NOT CURRENTLY AVAILABLE
listings.description:
Our home is full of character, built in the 1860s so it is a little fragile. Our available spare bedroom is in the basement, a shared bathroom
is on the first floor. Shared use of living room with 150cm TV. In the city but a residential neighbourhood.
listings.area_description: Our neighbourhood is very mixed, families, park, trendy cafes and markets.
listings.other_notes: Minimum 5 nights. Please do not contact us outside available dates.

listings.title: East London Flat with Park views (exchange with modern flats ONLY -  looking for Europe Summer 2026)

details.full_description:
We live in a two-bedroom garden maisonette in a quiet area of vibrant Hackney in East London, just a step away from London Fields Park. The house is decorated with some antique and Scandinavian 1960s furniture, and plenty of plants, books and art. The floor across the house is marble and solid oak. There is a sunny and well-established south-facing garden.

Both bedrooms and shower rooms are upstairs. The main bedroom has a king-sized bed, a window onto the garden and an en-suite shower room. The other bedroom has a window onto the street side but is very quiet. It has a double bed with a heavy wooden frame, lots of storage space and a desk. 

There is a large sofa (not a sofa bed) in the living room on which one person could sleep comfortably.

The large eat-in kitchen has been renovated quite recently and is fully fitted with a washing machine, dishwasher, large fridge-freezer, microwave, gas cooker and electric oven. Complimentary tea and coffee will be provided. The kitchen has everything you will need including all sorts of utensils (we love cooking!), pots, pans and glasses. Towels will be provided. There is good wifi but we haven't got a television.

There are USB ports in all rooms, and we can provide adapters for EU/US plugs.

It’s fine to smoke in the garden or outside the front door, where it’s covered.
details.full_area_description:
Hackney is a very lively area with lots of bars and restaurants, in transport zone 2 - so not far from the city centre. London Fields park has become very fashionable and is just around the corner. You can swim at the Olympic-sized London Fields Lido which is heated all year round, play tennis or enjoy a drink with park views at the Pub on the Park. Victoria Park, one of the biggest green spaces in London, is a 10-minute walk away. 

Broadway Market is just a 5 mins walk and comes alive on a Saturday with food, vintage clothing and all sorts of other stalls. Broadway Market is lined with restaurants, cafes, bookshops and boutiques and is good for coffee and window-shopping any day of the week. From the end of the Market, you can access the picturesque Regents Canal. Columbia Road Flower Market is a 20-minute walk, a lovely way to start your Sunday. And we’re close but not too close to the restaurants, shops and nightlife in Dalston, Hoxton and Shoreditch.

Getting around: 10 mins walk to London Fields Station from here there are regular trains into Liverpool Street Station, just a 7-minute journey. Liverpool Street has good connections to the Central, Circle, District and Hammersmith & City lines. The Stansted Express train arrives to and departs from Liverpool St. too.

Approximately 15mins walk to Haggerston and Hackney Central overground stations, making it easy to access to both Stratford (Westfield Shopping mall and the Olympic Park) and Highbury & Islington (the latter connects to the Victoria Line for a quick journey into central London). 

Bus lines 55, 48, 26 take you straight into central London (Oxford Circus, London Bridge and Waterloo/Southbank respectively).
details.full_other: Please note we are currently only looking for exchanges within the UK.

Our beautiful one bedroom apartment is a tranquil oasis in London. It is uniquely situated in the heart of east London, overlooking the canal and beautiful Victoria Park.

From here you can soak up all of the London atmosphere, with lots of great bars and restaurants in walking distance. Not to mention Shoreditch (15 minute busride), the Olympic Park (20 min walk) and the art-hub of Hackney Wick (15 min walk) in close proximity. You can even reach the west-end within 30 minutes.

listings.title: Carlotta's house

My flat is a one bedroom apartment in Hackney, the most vibrant area in London.

It is modern, clean and comfortable, with plenty of light and space.

It's perfect for single travellers or couples who are visiting London.

listings.other_notes: There's a £30 cleaning fee.

We have two large bedrooms with 1 double bed and one large sofa bed, a fully equipped kitchen, as well as a warm and incredibly bright living room thanks to the large windows overlooking the city.

Enjoy the comfort

Within an easy walk you have the green spaces of Hackney Marsh, Millfields Park, Mabley Green and a little further Victoria Park, Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park and Stratford Westfield.

Behind the marsh is the River Lea and Lea Valley where you can walk or cycle for miles up and down the waterways away from the busyness of London roads.

Within the immediate neighbourhood there is Chatsworth Road with its cafes and local shops, Hackney Wick with its canalside bars and eateries and Hackney with it

5 mins walk from Homerton overground where you can reach in 10 minutes both Highbury and islington (Victoria line) & Stratford ( Central & Jubilee line)

Josephine's house

With everything from parks and canals to murals, street food and a host of great pubs and bars, plus fantastic parks and a plethora of cultural venues. The reopening of 50 meter London Fields Lido is a must visit - less than 10 minutes walk away, plus a Picturehouse cinema and a revived theatre and bar at Hackney Empire all within 1
listings.other_notes: We have two friendly cats.

listings.area_description:

The kitchen/living room area is pretty large, with huge windows that look out onto Shacklewell Lane, providing you with constant entertainment.


You will see the living room looking different. The daytime pictures have been taken a while ago, whilst the late
listings.area_description:
It's right in the heart of Dalston, THE PLACE TO BE in London! Very dense with restaurants, bars, pubs, cafes, music venues....you won't get bored here. In fact, people flock to Dalston from outside London.

There are plenty of buses (236; 488; 76 ;67;243; 149). Two minutes walk from the flat, as well as Dalston Kingsland (5 mins) and Dalston Junction (10 mins).

The City can be reached in about 20 minutes via public transport and the West End in about 35. I often walk to Covent Garden and o

The apartment has an open-plan layout with double-height ceilings and opens onto a patio with an ivy-covered living wall. The mezzanine has a snug bedroom and a second flexible room with a desk. There is ample storage, including a small walk-in closet upstairs.

The apartment is perfect for individuals or couples and can also accommodate a baby or toddler. We have a trav

This is a lively and trendy part of Hackney in East London. Broadway Market, London Fields, and Victoria Park are just a few minutes away on foot. Hackney Wick and Shoreditch are about ten minutes on a bike.

There is a bustling weekend street market on Broadway Market as well as a Sunday flower market on Columbia Road.

We are also within walking distance of a heated open-air swimming pool.

If you’re exploring London in general, please note that some tourist spots in the west can take up to an

If there’s a dry spell (which sometimes happens during summer), we’d appreciate it if you could water the pat

 Our house is a spacious, light, victorian house in Hackney East London with a sunny garden.

Leafy pockets such as London Fields and Victoria Park soften the urban edge, while a reputation for live music, galleries and nightlife keeps the area firmly on the trending map.

Saturday means Broadway Market, a Victorian street lined with vintage shopfronts where awa

There is a close by Overground station with easy and quick connection to Soho, Oxford Street, Olympic Park etc.
If you like outdoor swimming London fields Lido is an iconic spot. The is a Cinema in walking dist

A large window looks out onto greenery, making the space feel calm and welcoming, with lovely light throughout the day.

The room has a distinctly creative, lived-in feel, with original artworks and thoughtful details, while still being practical and comfortable for a lon

The Roding Road area has a quiet, residential, village-like feel while still being firmly rooted in East London.

It’s calm and green, with tree-lined streets and easy access to Hackney Marshes, the River Lea and canals, making it ideal if you enjoy walks, cycling and time outdoors. The area feels friendly and authentic rather than polished or touristy, with a strong local community and a relaxed pace of life.

You’re well connected without the chaos — close to Homerton and Hackney Wick, so you

A south-facing sun trap balcony accessed through a glass wall from the living space, and a newly landscaped secret garden greets you into the kitchen/diner. It's the perfect space for dinner parties with a table that extends from a circle housing 4 guests to a long oval that can hug 10 around it. Get cosy on the sofa and enjoy the Samsung Frame TV, or enjoy a soak in our deep japanese style bath.

The

details.full_description:
Our home is a stylish but comfortable terraced house on a residential street in Stoke Newington, just a short bus journey from Central London and all its incredible attractions. 

We live in one of London's TOP 5 neighbourhoods:
http://www.theguardian.com/travel/2012/jul/27/london-top-five-neighbourhoods

As such it provides a rare opportunity to live like a local in a four bedroom house centrally located in the the greatest city in the world: LONDON! From the Tate Modern to Buckingham Palace, St. Paul's Cathedral to the Tower of London - you will never be short of things to do.  We live on multiple bus routes that will get you wherever you want to go in London in around 30 minutes. Our own neighbourhood (Stoke Newington - see your neighbourhood section) also has its own unique character; full of families, fashionistas and fun.

Our house itself is pretty cool (even if we do say so ourselves). We have a double lounge and a very big kitchen with an island unit/breakfast bar, a twelve-seater table, and high quality cooking kit, including a SMEG oven and hob.  The kitchen opens out through cascading glass doors onto a paved garden with table & chairs and a big Weber gas barbecue. We're really into cooking so you'll find everything you need to whip up a nice meal with ingredients bought from the amazing local shops.

We're big fans of contemporary art so you'll find a few surprises around the house including some Tracey Emins, Damien Hirsts, and a big neon sign in the front room. Also feel free to dig into the DVDs, board games and books and we'll leave a laptop out for general internet browsing, etc.

Upstairs, there's two big double bedrooms and a nursery. The master bedroom has a super-comfy king sized bed. The second bedroom has a standard double and plenty of wardrobe and drawer space. There's a family bathroom with separate shower and bathtub. The third bedroom on this floor is a nursery and currently has a cot but we could convert this into a toddler's bed if needed.

We have also extended into the loft and there is a fourth bedroom here with en suite shower facilities.  This is currently set up as an office but if you're a big family and really need the space then we can swap it over to be used as a makeshift bedroom for your stay with two foldaway beds. 

If a family comes to stay, we can leave toys out, etc. but if you're a couple and don't want to be bothered by baby stuff, we can pack it all away so it feels 100% like a grown-up house. 

It's permit parking on our street, but we can provide you with temporary parking vouchers for each day of your stay.

We hope that if you come to stay in our house, you'll make yourselves at home and enjoy all that our house, our neighbourhood, and London, have to offer. If you have any questions about anything that you've read, please don't hesitate to ask.  Please note there is a cleaning fee of £100 for guest point only exchanges.
details.full_area_description:
https://www.themodernhouse.com/journal/the-best-of-stoke-newington-resident-guide/#see

You know all about London and how much there is to do, but we wanted to talk about our local neighbourhood Stoke Newington so that you realise why you want to come and stay in our house!

Stoke Newington is a neighbourhood loved by families with great parks, cafes and shops. Clissold Park is fantastic, with duck ponds, dog free areas, a paddling pool, a running track, tennis courts, deer, goats, a butterfly enclosure and free table tennis tables. You'll find lots of picnic stuff in our house so if you want to make full use of the park if it's a sunny day then you absolutely can.

In Stokey (as the locals call Stoke Newington), Nook and Search & Rescue are our two current favourite shops and The Green Room is my current favourite cafe (in the back of a beautiful florists'). If you're looking for bars and pubs, Stoke Newington has quite a few - The White Hart is a great pub with a huge beer garden and yummy American-style food. The Londesborough (we had our wedding reception in there!) is a gorgeous pub with cool art on the walls and the best Sunday roasts - fancy and a bit pricey but totally worth it. And The Prince would be your local - less than a two minute's walk away with very fab interiors, delicious beers and a tasty food menu. Some other top eating spots are Rasa (in Time Out's Top 100 restaurants in London), Numara Bos Cirrik (also in the top 100), and Il Bacio (a great local Italian with lots of homemade dishes). 

If you want to cook for yourself, you've got amazing delis (Gallo Nero on the High Street does incredible sliced Italian meats and cheeses and the best Bolognese sauce ever), great meat and fish shops (Meat N16 stocks delicious Scotch Eggs and has a great selection of wines and chutneys to go with anything you buy), and there's the best Greengrocer on Stoke Newington Church Street who pack your bags for you and stock both the best standard ingredients and some pretty weird and wonderful fruits and veggies, too. There's the only totally organic farmer's market in the UK in Stoke Newington - it's on a Saturday from 11 to 2. They have very yummy cakes and the soda bread is particularly good.

Stoke Newington is also only a stone's throw away from Dalston, Shoreditch, and Islington - all of which are neighbourhoods packed with bars, restaurants and amazing shops. This means that there are loads of places for you to explore very close to the house, particularly if you want to hang out with London's hipsters (of all ages). More galleries and shops are opening all the time and it's worth an afternoon's walk around one of these neighbourhoods to see all the great places. 

In Shoreditch, Cafe Kick is an amazing table football bar and all around there are other bars. There is the amazing House of Hackney interiors shop in Shoreditch, too, and on a Sunday there's Columbia Road flower market. The Shoreditch bar scene changes all the time so we can update you as to where is good if that's what you're into just before you come to stay.

Dalston is full of bars and restaurants. Their Ocakbasi (Turkish grill) restaurants are the best - world renowned, in fact. And for bars, you have to try Birthdays (their margaritas are yum), Dalston Superstore, and Alibi (apparently, Harry Styles' favourite bar-club in London). Very popular right now is the Russet Cafe, which is just off the lovely Hackney Downs park - good for walking off one of the Russet's legendary brunches. In the week, it's a mum and baby hangout during the day and does music by night. At the weekend, they do a brunch which is gaining legendary status. Lots of places in Dalston are cafes by day, bars by night, and The Russet is just one of them. 

Dalston's also got a great theatre which puts on some really cutting edge stuff, called The Arcola. It's worth checking out. If you're into world music or jazz, then Cafe Oto is making waves in that area, having had lots of write-ups in the press for being excellent. They do good coffee in the daytime, too.

Islington has some fabulous bars and restaurants - we have been going to the yummy Gallipoli on Upper Street for years for Turkish Food. And Upper Street is a shopper's heaven, with Whistles, Hobbs, Space NK, Cop Copine and heaps of other amazing lifestyle brands as well as destination interior design shop, Aria.

Then, when you're ready for proper sightseeing and getting out of our local neighbourhoods, the City of London is a 30 minute bus ride or 15 minute train ride away and the West End is a 30 - 45 minute bus ride. We are a 15 minute walk from Dalston Junction, Dalston Kingsland and Canonbury overground stations, which will take you to the Olympic Park (& Westfield shopping centre) and will also connect you to the London Underground via Highbury & Islington or Stratford station. Basically, there are heaps of transport options if you decide to come and stay at our place, so we can give you loads more info on the easiest ways to get to various places, and you'll feel like a local in no time at all! We have loads of London reference and guide books in the house, too, so you can make use of those while you're here to find out more sights and things to do and see and to get general inspiration. 

There is so much going on it's hard to come up with a definitive list, but once we're directly in touch with you and know what you're into, we can give you some more specific suggestions and help you plan an amazing trip (if you want us to!) 

And, if you're still reading, a little bit of history about Stoke Newington: 
Stoke Newington is pretty well known for being creative, independently minded and family-friendly. In the late 1800s/early 1900s, this would have been seen as being very much the outskirts of London and all sorts of Victorian rebels moved here to escape mainstream society. Many of the Suffragettes lived around here, as did Daniel Defoe. Edgar Allan Poe was born in a flat on the High Street. 

Even today, it's retained a political, rebellious edge - it's full of liberal free-thinkers who want to shop in independent places, know their neighbours and feel a part of their community. It's the kind of place which, despite being in London, people still smile at you in the shops and get chatting in the park. 

http://www.timeout.com/london/things-to-do/stoke-newington-area-guide

details.full_description:
Our clean and light, high-ceilinged three storey terraced Victorian house is on a very quiet terraced street in Stoke Newington in North London. This is a well loved family home to 5 people and hopefully the pictures reflect the fact this is lived in - if you are looking for a minimalist hotel feel we're not the house for you! It is a comfortable and relaxing place to be with everything a family would want. There is a living room and a large kitchen with dining table for family meals which opens onto the private back garden. We have two separate bathrooms (one is en suite to the main bedroom) and separate guest toilet. In the garden there is a small office with computer/internet which can be available for anyone needing to do any work remotely whilst on holiday. The property is more than big enough for children, teenagers and adults to happily coexist. The main bedroom has a superking bed, two of the children's rooms have double beds and the other has a single bed. We have Apple TV/Disney+/Netflix/Amazon Prime and superfast broadband all available to any visitors to use.  

Our neighbourhood is very friendly, with fantastic cafés, shops and park, and is centrally located in London (Zone 2). It is convenient for day trips outside London as well as all the amenities and sites of London.
Stoke Newington has lots to occupy you but it is also perfectly placed and in walking distance to Dalston, Shoreditch and the other interesting areas of East London. 

Great transport links nearby mean you can get to all areas of London (and beyond) easily. We travel within London almost exclusively by bike and can provide access to some bikes from teenagers to adults (depending on how many we have away with us!). London has a fantastic network of signed cycling Quietways taking you to Bloomsbury, the City or the river in under half an hour via back streets, parks and waterways or out to Epping Forest or the Hertfordshire countryside in an hour along the canal. 

This is an ideal base for a family holiday or a work visit, combining a child and teenager friendly local area with easy access to activities around London.
details.full_area_description:
Our house is a minutes walk from Stoke Newington Church street with its brilliant restaurants, cafes, pubs and a range of shops. We are a few minutes walk from the amazing Abney Cemetery (one of the 'Magnificent Seven' Garden Cemeteries of London) and Clissold Park (playground, water splash pad, lakes, deer park, picnics, tennis courts. Clissold Leisure Centre is 5 minutes away and has two swimming pools, a toddler’s pool, gym, and squash courts, and London Fields has a heated lido that is open year-round a short distance away as well as the West Reservoir for your outdoor swimming fix.
 
Stoke Newington Church Street has cafes, independent shops, an antique market and some great pubs. In the other direction, Green Lanes has the best Turkish restaurants in London. The Almeida Theatre and Little Angel Puppet Theatre are a short bus ride away in neighbouring Islington. Head east to Dalston for the Vortex Jazz Club and the wonderful art deco Rio Cinema. We will happily leave recommendations for great places to eat around the local area.

The Tube network gives you very fast access to the whole of London. Our closest stations are Stoke Newington, Arsenal, Highbury and Islington and Manor House (a few minutes by bus or a pleasant 15-20 minute walk). Hampstead Heath, with its beautiful swimming ponds and walking trails, is 13 minutes by Overground train from Canonbury. Cambridge, Brighton, the South Downs National Park and Whitstable are nice day trips and easily achieved from a base in Stoke Newington.

We've included a few pictures from Abney Park Cemetary, Clissold Park and Church all minutes from our house.

details.full_description:
OUR HOME

Ours is a very cosy and stylish four bedroom family home in Stoke Newington, North-east London. 

The house is Victorian so has lots of period features high ceilings, large sash windows, and has been updated with a modern kitchen, bathroom, fittings and furniture. We've lived in the area for almost 25 years. It's a great place to live and has much to offer for visitors to London.

The master bedroom is a large, light double with high ceilings and two windows looking out over the quiet street. The other two rooms at the top of the house are our children's rooms and both are a good size, each with a double bed. 

On the ground floor is the living and dining rooms, with large windows, high ceilings and lots of light. The living room has seating for six with the Cable TV/DVD etc in the corner, while the dining room has a table that can seat eight, but six more comfortably. The bathroom is on a middle level and has bath/shower, basin and toilet. There is also a separate shower room.

Downstairs is the dining kitchen, with doors leading out onto our patio garden. The bright, modern kitchen has all the equipment you need (dishwasher, electric oven, gas hobs, air-fryer, coffee machine etc) and is where we have most of our family meals. The garden catches plenty of sun and is a pleasant place to have meals on a summer afternoon. If your kids need more space to play then the local Clissold Park might be a better option. Our tumble-dryer and washing machine are down in the cellar. 

The house has quite a few different levels and so has plenty of stairs, which may be a factor for you to consider.
details.full_area_description:
The area - affectionately known as 'Stokey' - has great facilities and a really local community atmosphere which you don't always get in London. Church Street (the local main street) has lots of small independent shops and deli's, lots of bars, traditional English pubs, and many restaurants - Turkish, Indian, Italian, Spanish, Thai, Japanese, Mexican, European - all within walking distance. It has an indoor climbing centre, a sailing centre on a local reservoir, a leisure centre with several pools and gym, and the much-loved Clissold Park. The park is a real hub of local life, with sports facilities (Tennis, Football/Soccer, jogging, cycling, skate park) a children's adventure playground and a lovely cafe in the park's recently restored Georgian house. Also near by is Abney Park Cemetery. The no-longer-used cemetery may sound spooky, but it's wonderfully atmospheric, very overgrown Victorian gothic. Most people use it as a place to stroll, take photos, walk the dogs etc. Every Saturday morning there is a Farmer's Market in Stoke Newington selling all manner of organic and home made food.

Just south of Stoke Newington is Dalston which has become very trendy in recent years. It has a young crowd, popular with artists, media workers, digital start-ups etc. New bars seem to pop-up all the time. Further south is Shoreditch / Hoxton, the centre of London's digital economy district.

TRAVEL

The house is in Zone 2 for travel - there are 6 zones in total for London, with zone 1 being the centre.

There are plenty of transport options - we're two minutes walk from Stoke Newington train station. Trains run to Liverpool Street Station in the City (financial district). From there you can get the Elizabeth Line or Central Line underground into the West End (shopping district, theatres etc). Liverpool Street is also the starting point for journeys out to Cambridge, Norwich, Ipswich, Essex and East Anglia.

The 106 bus at the top of our road takes you to Finsbury Park underground station (15 mins), from where you can take the Victoria and Piccadilly underground lines into central London/West End (15 mins). From Finsbury Park you can also get overground trains out to Cambridge for a day trip (50 mins) which is well worth doing. 

The Victoria line also takes you to Kings Cross St Pancras and Euston stations. Those are both big mainline stations that will connect you to pretty much anywhere in the midlands and north of the UK, plus to Europe via the Eurostar channel tunnel train. The Piccadilly line takes you to Holborn (British Museum), Covent Garden, Leicester Square, Piccadilly, Green Park and, eventually, Heathrow airport. The Elizabeth Line from Liverpool Street Station is now a really good way to get to Heathrow.

A five minute bus ride south of Stoke Newington gets you to Dalston, which has two stations. One line takes you west and east so is handy to get to the huge Westfield shopping mall in Stratford on the 2012 Olympics site (20 mins), but also to head out to the beautiful Thames-side areas in far west London - RIchmond, Kew (60 mins) and also Hampstead Heath (30 mins). Dalston's other station heads south and takes you into the City and Docklands areas, and it's also part of a new circular overground line that takes you in a (very wide) loop all around the city.

A 15-minute ride on the 73 or 476 buses from Stoke Newington gets you to Islington, which is one of North London's nicest spots to visit. Lots of good shops, bars, cinemas, restaurants etc.
details.full_other:
It has character, charm and space for eight people

Great location. Lots of cafes, restaurants, shops and bars. People we have exchanged with have found it a good base to enjoy London's major attractions , but also to enjoy local life too.

From our house, it's easy to travel to central London.

We travel usually as a party of four: Paul, Charlotte, Tess (21) and Archie (18)

listings.description:
My home is a spacious family house with 4 good-sized bedrooms and 2 bathrooms on a popular and peaceful residential street in London's fashionable Stoke Newington.

Set over four floors the house has a double reception room with four-seater sofa and spacious modern fitted kitchen with a large dining table and concertina doors leading to a patio garden. Upstairs there are two large double bedrooms, one with an ensuite bathroom, and two single bedrooms (plus we also have an extra single mattress t
listings.area_description:
Stoke Newington is a place where many young families choose to live because it's safe, clean and very easy to access other parts of London, but at the same time there is great nightlife on the doorstep in Dalston and Shoreditch.

It is a very special and desirable part of London and has a local, independent atmosphere unique in a city this size. Nearby Church Street is famous for it's independent vintage boutiques as well as great restaurants and cafes.

We are a short walk from Dalston, well se
listings.other_notes:
It is spacious, calming and also great for kids
It is in one of London's most desirable areas
It's near a big park and lots of great restaurants
It's got great access to central London

listings.title: Charming 5 bedroom family home in lovely local area with village feel
listings.description: Our home is a classic Victorian London terrace, but with a loft conversion at the top. We have been here for 20 years and feel it is both an exciting, but also warm and diverse place in which to bring up our children. We love living in Stoke Newington, which is like a village with lovely shops, cafes, restaurants and parks, yet within half an hour of central London, and in the borough of Hackney, an area with so much to offer in terms of culture, food / drink, parks and sports.
listings.area_description:
Stoke Newington is the best of both worlds - convenient to the City of London, historic London and the West End, yet really peaceful, interesting, vibrant and family oriented in itself. 
You can take a train or bus into town in about 30 minutes and / or take in all that Stoke Newington and the borough Hackney (widest and best range of green spaces in all of London) has to offer. Stoke Newington has developed into a much-sought after (villagey) neighbourhood with excellent cafes, bars and wider
listings.other_notes:
Our home is traditional yet modern, homely and exciting
There are so many lovely parks, cafes, restaurants etc within walking distance
Our neighbourhood is warm and friendly, with the feel of a village within London

listings.title: Bright and comfortable 2 bedroom apt
listings.description:
Come and experience one of London's best neighbourhoods, Stoke Newington, where you can enjoy a lovely village atmosphere really close to the buzz of central London. https://www.timeout.com/london/things-to-do/stoke-newington-area-guide

Our home is a contemporary, family friendly apartment in the centre of Stoke Newington. It's really bright, with dual aspect and great views to the city. We have two large bedrooms, a family bathroom, an open plan living / kitchen / dining and a front and rear b
listings.area_description: As well as exploring the centre of London, the borough of Hackney has so much to offer - the hipster heaven Dalston with its great nightlife, London Fields, Broadway Market, St Regent's canal, the Victoria & Albert Children's museum in Cambridge Heath and the Olympic Park in Stratford are all within easy reach. There are several great parks within walking distance from the apartment, and we are really spoiled for choice with speciality food shops, as well places to eat out with terraces and beer
listings.other_notes:
A well designed apartment in an award winning block.
A vibrant, creative and family friendly neighbourhood with lots of parks, restaurants and great independent shops.
A few minutes walk to the overground and a short ride into the centre of London.

listings.title: Creative family home not far from the city
listings.description: Creative 4 bedroomed Victorian terraced  family house in a vibrant and creative area.. There are lots of shops bars and restaurants nearby. Our house is on a quiet residential street with a local park 5 minutes and local pub 2 minutes away, It is a short bus ride into the city/ West End of London .
listings.area_description:
Our house is in a quiet residential street within a short walk to a beautiful park, an historic cemetery and lots of bars shops and restaurants. We have one of London’s best jazz bars and a  unique cinema 10 minutes walk away. Central London is a 30 minute bus ride away or there are train stations nearby to access the rest of London including Kew Gardens. All other London sights are within easy access . 

- Cozy cafés: There are several amazing cafes near by, The Good Egg does a fantastic breakf
listings.other_notes: There’s toys galore. Netflix and Apple TV for your use.

listings.title: Gerard and Maria's 1 bedroom flat in North London
listings.description:
Welcome to our one-bedroom flat on the top floor a terrace house, located between Highbury Fields and Holloway Road.

The flat has a large lounge with a sofa and a dining table and many lounge chairs. The kitchen includes a large fridge/freezer and is suitable for everyday cooking. The spacious bedroom has a queen-size bed and there is a bathroom with a bath/shower combination. We also have a projector with a chromecast, a record player with a burgeoning collection of records and Wifi.
listings.area_description:
Our flat is conveniently located for both green space and public transport. We're only one stop away from King's Cross on the Victoria Line. 

Highbury Fields is a 3-minute walk 
Drayton Park Station is a 5-minute walk
Highbury & Islington Station (Victoria Line and Overgroun) and Holloway Road Station (Picadilly Line) are only a 10-minute walk
Clissold Park is 20-minute walk

The area has plenty of cafés, pubs, restaurants, and everyday shops within walking distance, and fast, simple transport

listings.title: 2 bedroom flat in a residential area of north London, 8 min walk to tube.
listings.description:
Very conveniently located, fully equipped ground floor flat in a buzzy area of north London. We have a spacious back yard and are right on the edge of three lovely parks.

We are surrounded by lovely coffee shops, delis, wine bars and restaurants and only a short 8 min walk to the nearest tube station. 

We have two bedrooms. The main king bed is upstairs and the second one is in our office come spare bedroom with double doors out to the back garden. It is a very comfy double IKEA pull out bed a
listings.area_description: Located in a quiet residential neighborhood, our home is in a great location, near lovely local shops, cafes and Highbury Fields park. The streets are pretty and lined with trees with easy access to public transport, just a short walk to the tube and 20 minutes to central London.
listings.other_notes: We kindly ask for £70 cleaning fee for each stay.

listings.title: Francisco's house
listings.description:
My home is on the 4th floor and has an amazing balcony with a front line view to the Emirates stadium. Very comfortable areas and a well equipped kitchen.
Make sure you enjoy the sunset from the balcony.
listings.area_description:
Islington is one of the nicest neighborhoods in London, with lots of restaurants, parks, bars and other nice common areas within a 20 minutes walk.
The tube is 5 mins walking, and you can be in any part of London within 30 minutes.

listings.title: Beautiful Georgian Flat in the heart of London  with a "village " feel.
listings.description:
The flat is in a converted house in a Georgian Terrace built in 1789 and forming part of the Highbury Fields conservation area. It has a double bedroom, lounge with sofa bed, bathroom (with bath & rain shower) and study on the lower level. While on the upper level there is a fully fitted kitchen/dining room. We have wifi throughout the flat.

Parking is available immediately outside but requires permits costing about £15 per day. However it’s quicker and easier to use public transport so we do
listings.area_description:
We are situated close to the heart of the city in the borough of Islington. Immediately opposite there are green fields and tennis courts at the end of our road. We are lucky enough to have an area 5 minutes walk away where there are a number of independent shops - I buy all my organic food there. There is also a very traditional fish and chips shop which cooks everything while you wait and is conveniently located next to the pub.

We are walking distance from The Arsenal Football Stadium - fo
listings.other_notes:
It has a real village atmosphere with lots of independent shops nearby which cover all of our needs.
It is very peaceful (at times all you can hear is birdsong) and yet is close the one of the largest concentrations of bars and restaurants outside the West End.
It has great transport links so much o

listings.title: Matthew & Claire’s flat - no availability until 2026
listings.description: This is a basement flat in a lovely part of London close to Highbury Fields. The main bedroom has a large double bed and two wardrobes. The  lounge has a sofa which converts to a small double bed - ok for 2 adults and good for 2 children. The lounge connects to a kitchen and bathroom and looks out to a small garden.  We also own the house upstairs which is self contained and has a separate entrance.  The flat is ideal for a couple and can work for a family of up to four people.  There is a washe
listings.area_description: We live in Highbury, very close to a nice park and to the Emirates Stadium (home of Arsenal Football Club). Quiet street but lots of shops 2 minutes’ walk away. 12 minute walk to the nearest Tube station. Lots of bus connections and great restaurants within easy reach.
listings.other_notes: We can only travel during the school holiday periods.

listings.title: Family home in leafy, central area of London
listings.description: We have a lovely, informal, family home - very well equipped for kids. The kitchen was designed by chefs and is beautiful, big and incredibly well equipped. We have a big basement with washing machine / dryer and a bathroom. Main floor is a open plan sitting room and playroom, and upstairs another bathroom, office, main bedroom and the kids' bedroom (which can either have two small beds or a double sofa bed). If you need to sleep more than four people, we do also have a comfy sofabed in the sitt
listings.area_description: One of London's loveliest areas - Highbury. Very central, leafy, full of parks, great shops, amazing transport links into London and for trains to take you around the rest of the UK. We are near Arsenal Football Club's stadium if you're football fans!

listings.title: Beautiful 2 bedroom flat in Highbury and Islington
listings.description: Our flat is a large and light two bedroom apartment on the top two floors of a Georgian townhouse. We love the views over the gardens to the back of the building, the large proportions of the rooms and its excellent location. There is a large open plan living room, separate kitchen, one double bedroom and one kids room with bunk beds.
listings.area_description: Our flat is ideally located in one of the nicest neighbourhoods in London. We are a few minutes walk from Upper Street with it’s excellent restaurants and shops, a 7 minute walk to Highbury and Islington tube station which takes you directly into the centre of town, and near many lovely green squares and parks. It’s a perfect area for families, with plenty of playgrounds nearby, or foodies, with an array of incredible restaurants and gastropubs in walking distance.
listings.other_notes: We also have access to a large shared terrace which is perfect for a glass of wine in the evening, or for kids to run and scoot around.

listings.title: Modern build 1 flat bedroom in Highbury
listings.description:
Hello future visitors and friends! Our flat is a modern built 1 bedroom in Highbury that will be the perfect base for your London stay, either if you're coming to visit or to work.

The living room is where we usually spend most of our time while we're home. We got a new sofa recently which is incredibly comfortable and you can make use of the TV while you're here. The dining table can be converted into a boardgames table which can be used for all the games in the house. There's also a desk and
listings.area_description:
It's 8 minutes away from the Piccadilly line and 15 minutes from the Victoria line, which means that you can be in the heart of London in less than 25 minutes after leaving the house.

There's a lot of green space nearby, with Highbury Fields, Clissold Park (check out the animals and the shoe tree) and Finsbury Park all within a 20 minute walk.

Highbury Park next door has a lot of nice brunch places and cafés, and Upper Street is just a 10 minute bus ride for all the restaurants, bars, and danc
listings.other_notes: There is a very fluffy cat living in the house that would need to be cared for - give him food and water, and if he feels brave to come say hi also give him a few pets :)

listings.title: Immaculate 3 bed flat in Central London
listings.description:
Experience all that London has to offer from our 3-bedroom pied-a-terre featuring high ceilings, huge windows, and original Victorian architectural details. Spread across the first floors of two large Victorian houses, our flat is full of charm, owned by artists and designers and adorned with art and designer furniture. Loved for its calm, comfort, and cleanliness, it offers a confortable retreat from the busy city.

Centrally located in Islington, it provides easy access to all of London and is
listings.area_description: Islington is a vibrant and much loved neighborhood in North London, known for its lively arts scene, diverse dining options, and charming streets. Visitors can enjoy world-class performances at Sadler's Wells, a leading venue for contemporary dance. Food enthusiasts will love Westerns Laundry, a highly acclaimed local restaurant moments from the flat. Highbury Fields provides a peaceful Victorian Park for relaxation and outdoor activities, with fantastic children’s playground, while The Yoga Qua
listings.other_notes:
We don't accept more than 6 guests, although the flat is large. We are not currently able to offer stays in peak seasons, but can accommodate occasional or short notice stays. 

Cleaning and laundry fee of £100 per stay. With thanks!

details.full_description:
Experience all that London has to offer from our 3-bedroom pied-a-terre featuring high ceilings, huge windows, and original Victorian architectural details. Spread across the first floors of two large Victorian houses, our flat is full of charm, owned by artists and designers and adorned with art and designer furniture. Loved for its calm, comfort, and cleanliness, it offers a confortable retreat from the busy city.

Centrally located in Islington, it provides easy access to all of London and is situated just across the road from Arsenal Stadium. Enjoy flexible sleeping arrangements with clean duvets and pillows, and crisp cotton and linen bedding for a comfortable stay.

As our flat is located in London the cleaning and laundry costs are significant. We do not live in the flat and cannot do it ourselves. Therefore we charge a flat £100 for GP exchanges, to pay our cleaners. This enables us to offer GP stays in central London which would otherwise not be possible, and your understanding is appreciated. Further we have gratefully accepted similar arrangements in stays in Spain, France, and Italy.
details.full_area_description: Islington is a vibrant and much loved neighborhood in North London, known for its lively arts scene, diverse dining options, and charming streets. Visitors can enjoy world-class performances at Sadler's Wells, a leading venue for contemporary dance. Food enthusiasts will love Westerns Laundry, a highly acclaimed local restaurant moments from the flat. Highbury Fields provides a peaceful Victorian Park for relaxation and outdoor activities, with fantastic children’s playground, while The Yoga Quarter offers a range of yoga classes for all levels. Football fans can visit the iconic Arsenal Stadium, home to Arsenal FC. With excellent transport links, Islington offers easy access to central London, making it a perfect base for enjoying an authentic London neighbourhood and exploring city.
details.full_other:
We don't accept more than 6 guests, although the flat is large. We are not currently able to offer stays in peak seasons, but can accommodate occasional or short notice stays. 

Cleaning and laundry fee of £100 per stay. With thanks!

listings.title: spacious 3 bedroom garden apartment  in central london
listings.description: Large Open plan apartment arranged over two floors living space on lower ground floor and bedrooms on upper ground floor. Own separate entrance on lower ground floor and separate communal entrance with communal hall on upper ground.  Two flats above occupied by very friendly neighbours.  Own garden adjacent to lovely friendly neighbours.  Any difficulties then they will be able to help - we will provide details in the visitors book.  Wifi available and lots of children's toys (can be removed).
listings.area_description:
The neighbourhood is vibrant and is a short walk away from bars restaurants.  There are several turkish restaurants and patisseries close by as well as a michelin star restaurant on the same street.  

You do not need a car everything you need is within walking distance and all the main London tourist attractions are within easy reach due to  excellent transport links.
listings.other_notes:
Located on a lovely tree lined street spacious open plan apartment with garden
Short walk  to transport links to all London tourist attractions
Short walk to excellent cafes and restaurants parks - vibrant area in its own right

listings.title: Peter's house
listings.description: My home is a modern, high spec apartment with all the necessary amenities. It is open plan, airy and bright. It is a 2 bedroom flat. The second bedroom has a very comfortable pull out double sofa bed with an additional mattress topper for comfort. The bedroom can be easily interchanged between an office and a bedroom. My apartment is less than a five minute walk to the underground station which takes you into the heart of london in less than 10 minutes.
listings.area_description: I live in zone 2 central London in leafy Islington. Islington is one of the most desirable neighbourhoods in London. It is less than 10 minutes away from all the heart of London by tube but has a beautiful suburban feel, with old Georgian buildings, public parks, public pools and tennis courts that can be used by visitors and amazing restaurants and bars.
listings.other_notes: I accept a maximum of 3 people, two adults and one child. Beyond a basic general cleaning when you leave the apartment, £35 pounds  must be left for a deep cleaning. The double cleaning is required to ensure the home is maintained in the great conditions you see in the pictures !

listings.title: Paulina's house
listings.description: My 2 bed flat is cosy and clean . I have a small balcony.
listings.area_description: It’s is near the beautiful park and very close to Arsenal Stadium. Nearest tube station is Highbury and Arsenal. Good public transport and close to central of  London.

listings.title: Richard and Sarah's Central London home
listings.description:
Nestled in a charming, historic neighborhood, this extraordinary two-bedroom apartment offers an unparalleled retreat from the bustling city life. The tranquility of its location belies its central London location. The peaceful ambiance makes it easy to forget you're in the heart of the city. 

One of its most remarkable features is the private garden – a hidden gem that provides a sanctuary for relaxation, alfresco dining (when weather permits!). With abundant space, this apartment is unique in
listings.area_description:
The friendly neighborhood is steeped in history, with charming streets, local shops, and cultural treasures just a stone's throw away. Your daily life will be enriched by the vibrant community that surrounds you, all while enjoying the peaceful seclusion of your spacious haven.

Highbury, where history meets modernity, is the epitome of London charm. This vibrant neighborhood seamlessly blends the elegance of its historic architecture with the buzz of contemporary life. Stroll along tree-lined s

listings.title: Joscar's house
listings.description: Our home is a listed Victorian Coach house built in the 1800's. We live on the first floor up some stairs and is a fully kitted out home ready for your stay. We have a small private balcony and a large shared garden where you can sit and eat meals or enjoy the sun and birds. with one king size bed in our master bedroom. We have a kitchen, bathroom and lounge.
listings.area_description: Our home is in a very central location, in Zone 2 London in a desirable conservation area. We are a 5 minutes away from the train station and 15 minutes walk from the Victoria line which is said to get you anywhere in London in 20 minutes.
listings.other_notes:
If you want to use our car parking space, there are restrictions on the road which you need to follow otherwise you will get a ticket. Please park in a considerate way so our neighbours can also fit their car.

Restrictions: 8.30am to 9.30am and 3.15pm to 4.15pm, during term-time, Monday to Friday

listings.title: Marie and Romain’s house - looking for 1 week minimum in the mountains or in NY, dates open
listings.description: Our home is located across the church on Highbury Fields, accessible from a gated lane. It feels very homey with lots of plants which greatly enjoy the bright lights of our living room. The private back terrace ensures a secluded feel.
listings.area_description: Located just across the greens of Highbury Fields and 50 meters from the strand of local shops on Highbury Grove with nice cafes, organic grocery shopping and a nice pub. Clissold park is also close, and access to most touristic areas such as Covent Garden, Soho, Mayfair or Oxford Street is easy either with the Victoria Line (10mns walk through the park to the Highbury and Islington tube station) or Piccadilly Line (12mns walk to the Arsenal tube station). listings.title: Charming Victorian Home in Family-Friendly Highbury
listings.description:
Our charming Victorian home spans three floors, offering two reception rooms, including one with a Smart TV and a selection of toys for babies and toddlers. The fully equipped kitchen is on the ground floor, along with a cozy living room featuring a sofa bed. 

Upstairs, the first floor includes a double and a single bedroom, two bathrooms (one ensuite), and a utility room with a washing machine and dryer. The top floor features a spacious double bedroom with a desk, perfect for work or relaxati

You are also 15min away from Oxford street (Central London) via Highbury & Islington Station, Drayton Park station or Arsenal stadium.

The apartment is seven minutes walk from Arsenal tube station, on the Piccadilly line, which takes you to the centre in 10-15 minutes. It's also the same tube line as Heathrow airport and St Pancras train station (for Eurostar) so very well connected for transport.   The area is also home to the world-famous Arsenal football team, and fans will enjoy the view of the stadium from my apartment!

F

Our place is a two-bedroom apartment in Newington Green, Islington, in north London.

The flat consists of:

- The main bedroom with a queen-size bed (150x200cm)

- A small bedroom with a single bed and a desk

- Kitchen - with fridge freezer, electric cooker with a double oven, washing machine and dishwasher, and a dining table. The
