listings.title: Newly refurbished 4 bedroom home in friendly neighbourhood, 4 tube stops from Oxford street
listings.description:
Large home, with 4 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, 1 living room plus open plan kitchen, diner and living area and a large south facing garden. We modernised the whole house in 2009 and everything still works perfectly. Big patio doors onto south facing patio with dining table. Table tennis table in the garden.

****We only want to exchange with houses with 3 or more bedrooms and 2 or more bathrooms near the sea. Please do not enquire otherwise, thank you! *****
listings.area_description: We have lots of lovely restaurants, cafes and local shops including La Fromagerie - the best cheese shop in London!  Amazing selection of wines at Highbury Vintners.  Sobell Centre, Clissold Leisure Centre and Highbury Pool for sport as well as tennis and football in Clissold Park or Higbhury Fields.  The Park Theatre and The Almeida provide a mix of theatrical styles. Great transport links to all areas of London just 20 minutes on the tube to Oxford Circus and 40 minutes to Harrods.
listings.other_notes: We request a cleaning fee of £85.00. We are only looking for houses with swimming pools.

listings.title: Milly's house
listings.description:
As a designer, I’ve loved renovating my flat and making it a beautiful, cosy, sanctuary that I love coming home to. 

Comprised of an open-planned kitchen with an island dividing the space; there is a separate desk with an ergonomic office chair; a sitting nook with a large sofa for you to cozy up by the window; there are bar stools to perch at.

The bedroom comes with a double-bed line with fresh linens and a storage cupboard to keep your belongings.

The bathroom comes with a high-walled
listings.area_description:
I live in Highbury, north London. I love it because it's a peaceful and green neighborhood, just outside the city centre. 

My flat is on the edge of a park, with tennis courts, and delicious coffee and pastry stands. And yet just I'm just a short walk to amazing restaurants, boutique shops and buzzing night life. 

For when I want to get to different parts of London, Highbury and Islington station is 10 minutes away, making it easy to explore all that London has to offer.

listings.title: Milly’s flat
listings.description: As a designer, I’ve loved renovating my flat and making it a beautiful, cosy, sanctuary that I love coming home to. When I’m away, I rent my space for others to enjoy.
listings.area_description: Centrally located, my flat is on a peaceful and green street, yet just a short walk to amazing restaurants, boutique shops and buzzing night life. Highbury and Islington station is 10 minutes away, making it easy for you to explore all that London has to offer.

listings.title: 3-bed Victorian terrace in Islington, London
listings.description: Inner city location, good transport to central attractions, quiet desirable neighbourhood. South facing garden with sundecks.

listings.title: Lovely secluded room in the heart of beautiful Canonbury, London

The neighbourhood is quiet and with beautiful houses and gardens with splendid flowers. You will enjoy the chirping of the birds, a lovely walk by a river with a duck pond close by. In my view, one of the most beautiful neighbourhoods London has to offer.

listings.title: Ian's house

Dalston, Canonbury, and Hoxton are all nearby on foot, and I love heading to Highbury Fields or Clissold Park for a stroll. listings.description: Large house near public transport, 20 minutes from central London, ideal to visit the city.
listings.area_description: Plenty of shops and restaurants nearby

listings.title: Steven’s house
listings.description: Welcome to our family home! This lovely, spacious family friendly home with a secluded garden is the perfect base for visiting London.

listings.title: Bright 1 bed flat right on green space of Clapham Common, London

Clapham is a lovely area of the city and there are many places to shop and eat without the hassle or high prices associated with more touristy parts of London. There are hundreds of restaurants, bars and shops in the area.

- Buzzy restaurants: The Dairy, Alro's, Trinity, Soho Society

- Retail therapy: The whole High Street
- Bit of culture: all of London! 
- Walks in the park: Clapham Common, Brockwell Park, Dulwich Park
- Spot of exercise: you can cycle

listings.title: Family-friendly Victorian home in Balham, well connected to central London

It is also very well connected, with both Underground (Northern Line) and National Rail trains providing quick and easy access to central London.

listings.area_description:

Every single day except Sunday you have the veggie and fruits market 1’ away.
You can access the heart of London in 10’ by tube (Waterloo) or train (Victoria), tube direct to king cross, London Bridge, Charing Cross, Leicester Square, Battersea power station etc..

A public swimming pool is two mins walk away and the wonderful Tooting Bec Lido (open-air fresh pool) is on Tooting Common.

listings.area_description: We love the diversity of the area. You can walk down streets and find beautiful artisan cafes and one-off design shops alongside Caribbean food stalls and lots of second hand shops. The architecture of the area is unique with the Ritzy, library and city hall all showing remnants of an older Brixton. You have access to some of the most exciting areas in London like Brockwell Park with its amazing views of London or Herne Hill and its lovely shops and cafes. Any walk through the area shows you som

listings.title: Boho 3-bedroom family house in Herne Hill/Brixton, central London
listings.description:
Our Edwardian terrace house (built around 1900) is full of character, comfortable, and in one of the coolest neighbourhoods in central London.  

On the ground floor there is a double reception room, kitchen and bathroom.  On the first floor there are 3 double bedrooms. There are many books, Netflix, video games (too many!) and a lovely little black cat to make you feel at home.  As our guests, you are welcome to use bath toiletries and everything in the kitchen like oil, tea, coffee. 
We have a
listings.area_description:
Although we're in central London, our  leafy Herne Hill neighbourhood is friendly and  feels like a village, with a Sunday market as well as small, local shops, restaurants, pubs and cafes.  At the end of our street is Brockwell park with an outdoor pool, walled garden, duck ponds, and playgrounds.  

A 10 minute walk in the other direction, takes you to Brixton, one of the most popular areas of London - it's vibrant, trendy and multicultural, with markets, cinema, shops, live music, cafes and a
listings.other_notes:
As this is our primary residence, it is only available when we are all away, for a minimum stay of 5 nights.  We will prioritise guests who can stay for longer, while we are away.

Check-in is usually 3pm, with checkout 11am, unless we have made different arrangements. There is a £50 cleaning fee.

listings.title: Peaceful bright flat in dynamic Brixton,  minutes from Tube
listings.description:
Comfortable, light filled and modern 2 bed flat, surprisingly quiet despite being less than a 5 minute walk from Brixton tube. Sandwiched between the energetic high street and a beautiful Georgian square with an idyllic pub, my flat is an ideal base to recover between exploring all the best London has to offer.

Brixton is a dynamic and fun area with lots of restaurants and bars (but the flat is quiet and the balcony overlooks greenery) and I'm also only a few minutes walk from the Tube, where t
listings.area_description: Brixton is a dynamic area in south London with heaps of good bars, restaurants and music venues. For a quieter day, nearby Herne hill has a quiet village feel, a large park and a sunday farmers market

listings.title: Stuart's house
listings.description: A modern and fully equipped 2 bed flat on the third floor (with lift) of a modern apartment building. Private balcony with views over London skyline (can see the Shard and Canary Wharf). South facing so gets lots of morning and daytime sun. 2 bedroom (1 king size and 1 3/4 bed). 2 bathrooms, 1 with walk in shower and 1 with full size bath.
listings.area_description: Less than 10 minute walk from both Brixton (Victoria line), Clapham North (Northern Line) and Clapham High St (overground) stations so excellent public transport links. Walkable to both Brockwell Park and Clapham Common parks. Brixton and Clapham are both well stocked with lots of cafes, pubs, bars and restaurants. Happy to give recommendations
listings.other_notes: Fully stocked home with everything needed to live/stay/work here. Excellent WiFi (I regularly work from home on lots of zoom calls).

listings.title: Exceptional 5-Bedroom House with Private Garden in heart of vibrant Brixton, London
listings.description: An absolutely stunning split-level five bedroom rare property that benefits from a private garden in this quiet residential road in central Brixton. The property has been refurbished to the highest of standards and benefits from solid oak flooring and marble tiles throughout.The accommodation extends over 1600 sq.ft. has 3 bathrooms, each with showers and a beautiful balcony and garden in a very peacefully quiet location very close to the heart of Brixton and excellent London transport links. Th
listings.area_description:
Brixton and the Surrounding Area

Brixton, in South London, is one of the city’s most vibrant and culturally rich neighbourhoods. Known for its  heritage buildings, colourful markets, and dynamic music scene, it’s a hub of creativity and community spirit. Electric Avenue and Brixton Market are alive with food, art, and independent traders, while nearby Brockwell Park offers green space and skyline views. The area’s nightlife is legendary — from live gigs at the O2 Academy Brixton to intimate ven

listings.title: Charming Home in the Heart of Brixton with cat
listings.description: Welcome to my home in the vibrant neighbourhood of Brixton! I think you will love the cosy and homely feel of the flat, along with the opportunity to experience local life. The home offers speedy fibre broadband internet, a gas stove in the kitchen, and a comfortable living space for relaxation. There is also a small garden at the back of the house. For entertainment, I provide a projector with Apple TV and the typical streaming subscriptions and a BOSE bluetooth speaker. The home also comes wit
listings.area_description: Brixton, located in the heart of London, offers a vibrant mix of cultures, food, and entertainment for visitors. Known for its rich history, Brixton has played a significant role as a multicultural hub, primarily shaped by the Afro-Caribbean community since the 1940s. Today, its spirit is captured in the colourful Brixton Market, a bustling spot bursting with a variety of food stalls and shops. The indoor market of Brixton Village, in particular, is a haven for foodies, offering a plethora of cu

listings.title: Lovely Bright and cosy family home close to central London with garden
listings.description: We live in Brixton - a residential area in zone2 and very close to central London with great transport links into the centre of London and to the Olympic Park, Buckingham palace, Victoria, Regents Park…most central London attractions within 30 minutes doors to doors. We have 3 train stations and 1 tube within a ten-minute walk. Blackfriars (for the Tate Modern) and Victoria are both 10 minutes away by train. There are lots of buses going into town and if you cycle, we are a 20-minute cycle ride
listings.area_description:
Very cosy and central to London, Brixton has a great hype with working families and young professionals.
Life is very vibrant with tones of cuisines to try from, family friendly restaurants, 24hours shops and supermarkets opening very late (10-11pm) even on Bank Holidays.
listings.other_notes:
It's only few minutes away from the best city attractions with amazing transport links. (Victoria line and buses 24/7)
We're in a quiet street next to a lovely park so our place is ideal for couples or families alike.
At the en of the day, you can escape and gain some "ME or US time" in a separate d

listings.title: Casa Juan
listings.description:
Relax in this stylish 1-bedroom flat in vibrant Brixton!
Enjoy easy access to the city with just a 6-minute walk to Stockwell/Brixton station (Victoria & Northern lines). You'll be in the heart of London in under 20 minutes.

The flat features a spacious double bedroom with plenty of space to work or unwind.

The recently renovated open-plan kitchen and living room offer a calm and inviting atmosphere.
listings.area_description:
The neighborhood is a true hidden gem! We have the relaxed, village feel of Clapham North with its charming cafes and green spaces, while being minutes from the electric energy of Brixton. This puts you at the heart of South London's culture. 
You also get fantastic connectivity—a key feature of London—meaning you can be exploring the West End theatres or visiting the historic museums within 20 minutes, then retreat back to a quiet, authentic London neighborhood.

listings.title: Brixton, cats, close to Tube and Central!
listings.description: Flat is big enough for three adults and I have two friendly cats who will need to be fed.
listings.area_description:
Brixton is a vibrant, diverse community known primarily for its Afro-Caribbean roots. There is a market with green grocers and butchers but also a range of independent bars and restaurants. While the area is gentrifying, it hasn’t lost its edginess. 

There are great transport links to central London (10-15 minute Tube ride) so you can easily get around.

listings.title: Cristian's home
listings.description: Our lovely ground floor flat is warm, clean and airy and has a beautiful garden, which is rare for London and great to relax in. The flat is just a 9 minute walk from Brixton underground station. Brixton itself is just 11 mins tuberide to Victoria in central London.
listings.area_description: Brixton is a destination in itself with plenty of bars and pubs and especially known for its good, experimental and cheap food particularly in Brixton Village and Pop Brixton markets. It also has many live music venues such as the O2 academy and the Hootananny. Spectacular Brockwell Park is just 10 mins walk away.Brixton is probably the best destination south of the river.
listings.other_notes: Please take a look at our calendar for availability as this is our primary residence and we only host guests when we are on holiday ourselves.

listings.title: 1 bed flat in quiet corner of trendy Brixton
listings.description:
A cosy but more spacious than it looks 1-bedroom flat in a quiet road off vibrant, busy, trendy Brixton.

The flat is on the first floor of a 3-floor building, there is one double bed, one sofa bed and one inflatable bed that can be laid out in the living room, so it comfortably sleeps 2-5.
The flat can be pretty bright when London decides to show some sun and there's a nice breeze in spring and summer, while in winter it honestly gets hot pretty easily. The fast boiler also means hot showers an
listings.area_description:
Brixton ranks amongst the top coolest areas of London, and one often missed by visitors who spend their stay only within zone 1, so this is the perfect opportunity to enjoy some incredible gems away from the madness (and queues..) of central London.

Brixton Market and Brixton Village are full of eateries and bars, there is one craft beer place, one brewery and one wine bar in walking distance, and also my favourite brunch cafe which I can confidently vouch for. Plenty of live music venues too,
listings.other_notes: As this is my main residence it's available for reciprocal exchanges mainly. Unless specifically marked on the calendar I CANNOT DO GP EXCHANGES.

listings.title: Bright Brixton home
listings.description:
The house is an Edwardian (1910) property with large bay windows to the front and a courtyard garden. The rooms are generously sized for this part of London and we are a short walk from central Brixton.  

The bedrooms are generous with superking and king beds in the main 3 bedrooms and a double Murphy bed in the study bedroom 4. 

The Bathrooms are stylish with heated floors and anti-steam mirrors.

The kitchen is eat-in and spacious with plenty of appliances and plates etc if you are a family
listings.area_description: Brixton is full of foody options.  you have to check out Brixton Village, Pop Brixton and Market Row the three main foody hubs but there are eateries and restaurants everywhere.  Don't miss out on some great craft beer options too.  Transport links are great and we are only 30 mins from all the main museums, art galleries and theatres.  There's  a bus stop at the end of the road and its under 10mins to the tube and train stations in Brixton. There is a large park a few mins walk away and a green
listings.other_notes: This is our home and we

listings.title: Cozy flat with garden in London
listings.description:
My home is my sanctuary. It is in quite road not far from busy central London. It
The apartment is in a converted Victorian building so has a lot of character. You can enjoy chilling in the comfortable seating room or in the Garden. The garden gets quite a lot of sun shine but there are several mature trees so you can also get respite from the sun in hot days
listings.area_description:
Herne hill is a quite neighborhood at the heart of London. It has a village feel to it.  It's 12 minutes walk to trendy Brixton, so I there are plenty of trendy bars and restaurants in walking distance. All attractions are easily accessible via undergrad network and buses. It's quite central ( zone two). 
Very well connected to public transport. 12 minutes walk to underground and 6 minutes walk to the overground train line which connect directly to both Victoria and London Saint pancras.
listings.other_notes: There is a lido and massive park just across the road. You appreciate this if you are into jogging or swimming!

listings.title: Christian's  flat
listings.description:
Single dad's home. You will have everything you need in a safe and warm environment. Sunny in the mornings due to windows and small balcony (too small to sit on, just good for plants and opening the door really), friendly neighbours and helpful staff in the house. Internet, bathtub. Perfect way to stay cheaply in London.

Will add more pictures soon. These are just what I could find on my phone just now.
listings.area_description:
I live in North Brixton/Stockwell. Zone 2. Lots of public transport to anywhere in London. You can easily walk to Vauxhall and even central London, Buckingham Palace, Victoria, London Bridge, etc. Otherwise there are plenty of buses, trains and the tube to take you anywhere very quickly. You will have access to the Victoria and Northern line and railway trains. 
Lovely small and bigger local parks very close to the house. Playgrounds, too. 
It's a friendly area, known for it's music, market, b

Two cinemas within walking distance. 
Morrisons, Lidl and Tesco supermarkets on main road. Many local fast food places and cafes on same road. 
Swimming pools and gym in Brixton and Clapham High Street. Day entran

Kitchen has two larder fridges and a small free

listings.title: Ilena's house
listings.description: My home is a nice newly decorated modern house with lots of light and space. With an open spaced lovely kitchen  and a nice living aera that opens up to the garden. A fairly large and well kept house for a London home, with four bedroom, two bathroom and a study room.
listings.area_description: My house is situated on one of the most vibrant and popular, multiethnic area of south London. It is very close to an open market and the popular Brixton Village, known for all its multiethnic restaurants with visitors from all around the world. Situated at only 5 minutes from Brixton underground station, ou can find all the shops and amenities you are looking for. At on 7 minutes walking you have a teather, cinema, clothes shops etc. From Brixton station you can get the Victoria line which take

listings.title: Cheryl's house
listings.description: Good sized family home with all modern necessities. Mid century decor. Very comfortable.
listings.area_description: Our home is a 3 minute walk away from a train station that can transport you to central London in 20 minutes. Our local area has everything you need including an array of coffee shops and cafes, convenience store, park, library and bars.

listings.title: Una's house (unavailable until August)
listings.description: A comfortable 1-bedroom flat on the 6th floor of a modern building with easy access to Central London by bus or tube (~20-25 minutes to all the main tourist attractions). Great internet connection and a quit place for remote work. Local shops and supermarkets are within 10 minutes walk.
listings.area_description: Easy access to central London; plenty of pubs, restaurants and supermarkets within 10-20 walk; Quit neighbourhood and great internet connection for remote work
listings.other_notes: The flat is located in Brixton, one of our beloved capital’s most vibrant areas. Full to the brim with all kinds of culture, it’s a buzzing neighbourhood for drinks, gigs, market shopping and much more besides.

listings.title: Light & green 2-bed apartment in a hip London neighbourhood.
listings.description:
Welcome to our cool apartment in the heart of Brixton, only 5 minutes from the underground station - which itself is a quick 9 minute ride to Oxford Street and Buckingham Palace. 
It's set just away from the hustle and bustle on a quiet leafy square, with a great pub - the Trinity Arms - opposite that does a great Sunday roast. 
We have two large double bedrooms, both with standing desks for any remote working and superfast WiFi, and plenty of storage for your clothes. It has a large bathroom, w
listings.area_description: Located in the heart of Brixton and only 5 minutes from the underground station - which itself is a quick 10 minute ride to Oxford Street and Buckingham Palace. It's a short walk to Brixton market with loads of great places to eat and shop, the O2 Academy and legendary Phonox music venues are down the road, and it's a 15 minute walk to Brockwell Park. The apartment itself is set just away from the hustle and bustle on a quiet leafy square, with a great pub - the Trinity Arms - opposite that does

listings.title: Ilena's house
listings.description: My home is very spacious and has 4 bedroom a studio and 2 bathrooms. It has recently been refurbished. It is situated only 6 minutes walking from Brixton town centre and tube station (zone 2) . Centrally located from my house you can get in Victoria Buckingham palace in only 10 minutes and same for Wesmnister Abbey and big Ben. You can reach Oxford circus and Covent carne in only 20 minutes or less.
listings.area_description: I live in Brixton zone 2. Situated smack bang in the centre of south London, Brixton has become a go-to haunt for foodies, clubbers, artists and rockers alike. This cultural mecca is filled with a sense of community, which is evident for anyone who has shopped in Brixton Village or enjoyed a drink with local residents outside the Ritzy cinema. And for those in search of a party, Brixton’s famous live venues, including the Brixton Academy and Hootananny, draw in the late night crowds.

listings.title: Em’s Home
listings.description:
Welcome to our charming Victorian home, where classic architecture meets modern English countryside style. The house is filled with natural light thanks to large windows and a striking bay window at the front. Inside, you’ll find a warm, elegant interior with a mix of period features and contemporary comfort.

The second bedroom doubles as a perfect WFH space, with an extra bed and an automatic standing desk equipped with two monitors. We also have a PS5 and a smart TV for cosy evenings in.

Our
listings.area_description: We’re nestled in the heart of Herne Hill, a leafy and vibrant neighbourhood in South London. Just a five-minute stroll brings you to the iconic Brockwell Lido and the stunning Brockwell Park — one of England’s most beautiful green spaces. The local area is full of character, with independent restaurants, a beloved local bakery, and top-notch delicatessens right on your doorstep. It’s a safe, family-friendly neighbourhood with a warm community feel, and on Sundays, the local market offers fresh p
listings.other_notes: There is a cleaning fee of £80 for stays

listings.title: Eva's cheerful Brixton flat – with lovely outdoor private terrace!
listings.description:
Additional cost of £80 for cleaning and all linens.

Welcome to my beautiful 1-bedroom Brixton flat! Discover a stylish haven with a bright living space, well-equipped kitchen, and a cozy bedroom. Immerse yourself in Brixton's vibrant culture, borrow a book from my collection, and explore all the local eateries. With Brixton Tube Station just a 6-minute walk away, central London is easily accessible. Book now for a memorable stay in this wonderful neighbourhood! We can't wait to welcome you for
listings.area_description:
Brixton has everything you could want during your stay; amazing restaurants with all different cuisines, pubs, bars and nightclubs, Brixton Village, Market Row and the Ritzy Cinema. It's a fun, vibrant area with lots going on and you won't be short on things to do!

There is a big Tesco 10 minutes away, a big Lidl 10 minutes away, two Sainsbury's Locals between 4-6 minutes away, M&S food 5 minutes away, and a Co-op 3 minutes away. You can also do your food shop at the market stalls on Electric A
listings.other_notes:
My home is listed on Airbnb, where you can see reviews: 

https://www.airbnb.com/slink/nf1kWFNw

The listing also has a thorough list of all amenities.

It's in a secure development with 1 parking space.

It has all the latest modern appliances, including bean to cup coffee machine, ice on demand, home Cinema and a

100s of quirky independent restaurants, bars and markets right in the heart of vibrant Brixton, but the tranquility of Brockwell and Windmill park nearby.

The house overlooks one of the last remaining windmills in London, 10 minutes walk from Brixton tube station, with only a few stops right into the heart of the City. This is a well connected location with 7 bus routes, tube and overground only minutes away.

The house is located opposite a light night music venue, at weekends it can get a lit

listings.title: Anna's house
listings.description: Cosy characterful two bedroom apartment in a converted Victorian terrace. On a lovely quiet street but a stone’s throw away form the heart of buzzy Brixton.
listings.area_description: Brixton is lively, with a wealth of places to eat, drink and shop. You can eat any type of cuisine you want here! The apartment is also only a 15 minute walk to the beautiful Brockwell park, great for running, walking or swimming in the outdoor lido. The apartment is perfectly located on a quiet street but only 5 minutes from the tube and Brixton’s amenities. Just 12 minutes on the tube to Oxford Circus means it’s the perfect base to explore London.

listings.title: Bright and airy centrally located 3BR London maisonette
listings.description: Bright and spacious maisonette (2 floor apartment) with a modest but sunny garden with a big olive tree. Our kitchen/living space is the heart of the house, with lots of natural light, and is a wonderful place to cook and entertain. The downstairs bedroom has a walk in closet and a super king bed and hotel-quality mattress. The second bedroom (upstairs) has ample wardrobe space and a King size bed. The third bedroom is currently a nursery with a toddler crib. There is a washer-dryer and storage
listings.area_description: Our flat is located on a quiet street, while being a stone's throw from London's buzzy Brixton area. It's amazingly well connected, just a short walk from Brixton and Stockwell tube stations, which will get you to the heart of London in 15 minutes. Good parks for running around, playgrounds for the kids, lovely local pubs, and some of the best food London has to offer.
listings.other_notes: We are currently looking for direct exchange in San Francisco from March until November 2024.

listings.title: My home
listings.description: We live in a four bedroom house which is very spacious and it has a lovely and big landscaped garden,  and a study area on the ground floor. We love the bright light coming from the garden and the living area with a really nice fireplace.
listings.area_description: We live in Brixton, 10 minutes from Victoria Buckingham palace, 15 minutes from Oxford circus, Leicester square and Piccadilly by tube. Brixton is  often described as 'the beating heart of South London', Brixton has an energy unlike any other neighbourhood in the capital. From excellent travel links to a thriving nightlife scene with plenty of restaurants, bars and clubs, the SW9 postcode has become one of the most sought-after areas in London

listings.title: Lovely light, stylish home in a funky area close to Central London
listings.description: Our home is a classic London end of terrace house situated between Brixton and Herne Hill in South London. The house is light, comfortable and quiet. It's full of character, art and stylish furniture yet not precious. We have a sweet, sunny garden accessible via big doors from the living room that open fully so you get an inside/outside feeling. Eating outside is a pleasure. We have 2 double bedrooms, 2 with en-suite shower rooms and one main bathroom with a bathtub. The master bedroom has a sup
listings.area_description:
The location is perfect. We're on the edge of the city and the start of the suburbs. We're situated halfway between Brixton and Herne Hill, both 8 mins walk. Brixton is fast paced and lively, Herne Hill is much slower and has a village feel. Classic London neighborhood 'town' contrast.
We have amazing public transport nearby. Central London (Green Park/Piccadilly) is 20 minutes from the house via the super fast Victoria Line. Herne Hill train station has two lines which serve Victoria Train Stat
listings.other_notes:
We have a cat in case you have allergies.
This is a city house and generally very quiet but as with urban dwellings there is occasionally noise.
We charge a £100 cleaning fee and ask you to leave the house as clean as you will find it.

There's a fantastic market, a superb cinema, loads of brilliant places to eat and drink, as well as great supermarkets all on your doorstop. 

Travelling with children.

listings.title: James's house in London
listings.description: Beautiful home away from home you will love to visit most of London from this house
listings.area_description: Lambeth is the best area in South London to explore and there is one of the best cinema's around as well as leafy parks and great connections to London

listings.title: 2-flat bedroom in the heart of London, close to transport links and the world-famous Brixton Market.
listings.description:
Welcome to my cosy apartment, ideal for relaxing while making the most of London!

I have two bedrooms with double beds, a fully equipped kitchen, as well as a warm and incredibly bright living room thanks to the large windows overlooking a residential street.

Enjoy the comfort of high-speed WiFi and a peaceful, quiet apartment – perfect for working or unwinding after a day of exploring – just steps away from a variety local restaurants and grocery stores.
listings.area_description: Located in a quiet residential neighbourhood, my home is just minutes from local shops, cafes and parks. I love the friendly and diverse atmosphere of our neighbourhood, with its vibrant Market and music venues, easy access to public transport, and above all our friendly neighbours. The city center is a 30-minute bus ride or a 25-minute tube ride, so you can enjoy the best of both worlds: peaceful surroundings and vibrant city life.

listings.title: Family Home in well-connected Brixton
listings.description:
This lovely Edwardian house is a home away from home, well-suited for guests who would like to spend time relaxing together as well as exploring London. It is situated on a quiet, tree-lined street, moments from all Brixton has to offer.

Features:

listings.area_description:
Brixton is an incredibly vibrant, popular area that has a lot to offer in terms of entertainment. The house is well positioned for good proximity to all the latest live acts, sounds, bands at the O2 Academy Brixton, a mixture of gastro and informal dining and a short distance to the open spaces of Brockwell Park and Clapham Common. Our road however is set back from the main street and offers a quiet residential experience, while being an easy walk to all the amenities you could desire.


- Buzzy

listings.title: Spacious 2-bedroom apartment in the heart of Brixton, London
listings.description:
It is spacious and modern, whilst also feeling cosy. It has lots of sunlight, especially in the afternoons and evenings with great sunsets because of the huge windows in the kitchen, and floor to ceiling windows in the bedrooms. 

All of our neighbours are lovely, and super quiet so we are never disturbed!

We have a super-friendly ginger cat called Percy who you would need to feed and look after whilst staying here. He is very affectionate.
listings.area_description:
Our home is right in the centre of Brixton, less than 10 minutes walk to the tube, and into central London within 15 minutes. 

There are many parks and green spaces (and an outdoor pool) nearby, as well as many bars, restaurants, and exciting things to explore in the neighbourhood.

listings.title: Louis and Gery's Home
listings.description: We have spent two years renovating our bright and sunny modern home to reflect our love of design, colour and mid century style. We live with our four year old son, Val and our fabulously friendly and gorgeous cat Dusty who is one year old and half Egyptian Mau, he is an outdoor cat who loves exploring but equally adores a lap stroke or a cuddle. Our cheerful yellow kitchen with island opens up fully onto a private suntrap garden, perfect for summer get togethers. Our open plan sitting room with
listings.area_description: We live in South London near Brixton tube (zone 2) with super fast transport links to Central and North london. Brixton is a vibrant hub, bustling with street life, top notch food markets, live music and culture. Our favourite park - Myatt's Fields is just a 7 min walk - a Victorian jewel of a park, with beautiful planting, green lawns, nature areas and a playground with summer water play.

listings.title: James's private room
listings.description: Charming bedroom in a Victorian home offers a cozy stay in vibrant Brixton, just minutes from Herne Hill. Recently renovated, the room has a comfortable double bed and desk, perfect for work or relaxation. You’ll share the rest of the house with my friends who are a couple and own the house, who create a warm, inviting atmosphere. The area is ideal for exploring local culture, with nearby markets, parks, and cafes, providing a true taste of Brixton’s lively community. 10 minute walk to the Victo
listings.area_description: Located on a quiet road in between Brixton and Herne Hill. Enjoy Brixton’s dynamic markets, street art, and music venues, then unwind in Herne Hill’s charming village atmosphere, with cozy cafes and the lush Brockwell Park. Perfect for a day of culture and relaxation.
listings.other_notes: Only open to singles or couples. No pets allowed.

listings.title: Fiona's house
listings.description:
We live in a 3 story terraced house a 5 minute walk to the heart of Brixton. We have a 3 bedroom house with an office. We have a garden that features an outdoor couch, fire pit and gas BBQ. 

We live next to a pub. You will hear some music through the wall on a Friday and Saturday night but we have been living here 3 years and it doesn’t bother us. The Pub shuts at 11pm during the week and 12am on Friday and Saturday night.  
There is also a train track nearby so you will often hear trains.
listings.area_description:
Brixton is a great place to stay if you are visiting London. It is a vibrant and diverse neighbourhood, it has 3 food markets, great pubs, nightlife and multiple breweries, retail shops and a cinema. It is only 10minutes to central London by tube or alternatively there are buses and bikes (to hire) at the end of street.  
We have two large parks nearby in neighbouring suburbs.
listings.other_notes: Only looking for properties in New York during the Christmas period.

listings.title: Jane & Sam's house
listings.description: A beautiful home in London's trendiest and most vibrant area. In a quiet residential street the home has the best of both worlds. Just 5 minutes walk away it is filled with shops, restaurants, bars and a tube and just 5 minutes walk in the other direction is beautiful Brockwell Park with its's large open spaces and cafes and market in Herne Hill. London's theatre district is a 10 minute tube away.
listings.area_description: It is full of life and fun. Also quiet and a strong neighbourhood at the same time.
listings.other_notes: NO GP EXCHANGES PLEASE

listings.title: London 1 bedroom flat
listings.description:
Beautiful, modern apartment 20minutes away in public transports from Oxford Circus. My flat benefits from great natural light and faces a small park with horses which means that it is really quiet and calm. The balcony is ideal to enjoy breakfast as it gets the morning sun. It is ideally located near many parks, has a lift and even a children’s play park in the residence’s garden. 
Although there is one bedroom, there is also a double sofa bed which sleeps two people.
In the heart of Brixton, yo
listings.area_description: The flat is located in the heart of Brixton (20 minutes away from Oxford Circus). Luckily, the flat is opposite a park which keeps it quiet and gives beautiful views from the balcony and windows. It is very close to pop brixton and Brixton village which are both buzzing, lively food halls with many cosmopolitan restaurants and bars.

listings.title: It’s a three double bedroom flat
listings.description: My room is a very nice room and the flat is on the third floor. Lift is available
listings.area_description: The neighbourhood is very located and popular. Gp ,school  ,dentist shopping places near to the address. Bus stop and station 4 and 8 minutes walk.

listings.title: Kora’s home
listings.description:
My home is small but mighty! 
I really take pride of my space, it’s my little city sanctuary :)
listings.area_description: I live in the heart of Brixton. Very vibrant neighborhood and I’m next to cafes, restaurants from around the world and a big produce market

listings.title: Anahita's house
listings.description: Our home is a hidden sanctuary in the heart of vibrant London—a newly renovated Victorian conversion designed by architects, with abundant natural light and a beautifully landscaped private garden.
listings.area_description: We live in Brixton, in London’s Zone 2, is a vibrant neighborhood known for its rich cultural heritage, eclectic market, and lively nightlife. A hub of creativity with strong Caribbean and African influences, it offers everything from global street food and independent shops to music venues and cozy pubs. Locals enjoy Brockwell Park’s open green spaces and views of the city, while the Victoria Line provides quick access to central London. Brixton’s unique blend of energy, community spirit, and c

listings.title: Amber's house
listings.description: My home feels fresh and clean. There is natural wood throughout, which gives a grounding feel even though it is a third floor flat. It has a lot of light. It feels uncluttered and in a friendly neibour hood. It has a shared garden area/ playground. It also feels secure and safe.
listings.area_description: The flat is two minuits walk to the heart of Brixton which is a multicultural area. There are many places to eat and drink, from all over the world. There are markets here where you can buy food and much more. It has many clubs and bars, a big music scene and night life. There are also two gallery’s. There are parks close by(in walking distance). Brixton has a tube station, train station and many buses, it has good links around the city.

listings.title: 2 Bedroom family flat, London (1 week minimum)
listings.description: We are a family with two boys aged 9 and 12 years old. The whole flat is very child friendly, usually with lots of toys all over the place, a massive kids-oriented DVD collection and a coloured playing-foam mats area in the living room. All these items can be moved to the kids room or the storage to make the apartment absolutely suitable for people without kids. We are in New Cross Gate area, in Deptford's district, London Zone 2. Tower Bridge is 15 minutes away by car, 30 minutes away with the
listings.area_description:
The house is in a quiet residential area, five minutes walk from New Cross Gate Overground Station, trains connect with London Bridge, Shoreditch and the East End, Islington, Clapham and other areas in roughly fifteen minutes. Direct trains run to and from Gatwick Airport every half an hour. The nearby petrol station (1 minute walk) has a little supermarket and it is open 24h.

We are part of Deptford's district, home of the Goldsmith's University, and definitely a student-friendly area. The N
listings.other_notes:
A man, a woman, 2 little boys and a cat. The cat doesn't usually travel though.
Silvia is a senior project manager in the market research field, Stefano is a singer/songwriter, musician currently working mostly with children under '5.
Edoardo is 11 and Jordi is 9 and they go to school.

listings.title: Beautiful family home in buzzy Nunhead
listings.description:
Welcome to our home!

We have 3 bedrooms, a double master bedroom, one which is set up as an office with a double sofa bed, and a nursery. 

Downstairs is a large living room with a 40" TV, and a second office space. This follows into the centrepiece of the house, the open plan kitchen/diner.  Our dining table seats up to 8 and looks onto the garden. 

Got a car? We have space for 2 on our driveway.
listings.area_description:
Our home is ready for the ideal city trip. Just 20 minutes train ride from the centre of London, Nunhead is a little hub of activity - independent shops, cafes and restaurants, parks, great pubs, and only a stone's throw from other areas like Peckham with its food and nightlife scene. We are right in the middle of it. Step out of the house and within 30 seconds you have everything in front of you. 

- Buzzy restaurants: D4100, Peckham Bazaar, Kokum
- Cool bars: El Vermut
- Cozy cafés: Mosey, Goo

listings.title: Roland's house
listings.description: My mid of terrace Victorian townhouse was built in the 1870s. It's spread over two floors. On the ground floor are the living and dining rooms. At the back are kitchen and bathroom. Upstairs at the first landing is another small bathroom and a small bedroom, now used as office. At the second landing are two bedrooms with king size beds. There are many historical features, such as original fireplaces, original wooden floorboards, antique and vintage furniture. Behind the house is a seating area w
listings.area_description:
Nunhead is one of the London neighbourhoods closest to the centre and yet it kept its village character. Tree lined streets and many green spaces, among them the famous historical cemetery, a densely wooded nature reserve, make it a walkers’ paradise.
Local bars, restaurants, parks, playgrounds and shops are only a short walk away. The close proximity to central London makes my house an ideal base to discover the metropolis.
Two train stations in short walking distance connect to central London.
listings.other_notes: Although I have fast wifi in my house, please note the flat screen tv is not connected to wifi but has an HDMI cable to connect a laptop or tablet. The tv screen is connected to my stereo sound system.

listings.title: Ursula Melina's house
listings.description: Our house is an airy townhouse in Peckham a popular district in south east London. Our house is old but converted with a modern twist. It is comfortable and bright with large windows which let the light in. We love pretty tiles, which you will find everywhere in the house. We have one child, so you will find his room with plenty of toys to entertain any young visitors.
listings.area_description:
Peckham is very cool. Funky cafes, bakeries, cocktail bars, family friendly hipster pubs, pizzerias, Vermouth bars, Michelin star Southafrican, Indian restaurants, and everything in between. Its arty, its noisy and quiet all at once.  Uber friendly and fun. 
A 7 minute train ride to London bridge, a 15 minute ride to Victoria. 20 mins to Shoreditch. Served by overground and national rail. Endless busses. We love it here. I hope if you visit, you will love it too!

listings.title: Colourful three bedroom apartment in South London (with garden)
listings.description: This is a vibrant, colourful space well located in trendy South East London, surrounded by an array of coffee shops, bars and restaurants.  The house is a three minute walk from Peckham Rye, an expansive park with beautiful botanical gardens.  It is ten minutes walk from the train and has easy connections into central London (15 minutes on the train to Victoria).
listings.area_description: With its rooftop bars, quirky coffee shops, and art galleries, Peckham has fast become one of London's coolest neighbourhoods. The beating heart of Peckham – home to Del Boy, road rap hero Giggs, national acting treasures Olivia Colman and John Boyega, and a much-photographed pink staircase – is Rye Lane. It’s a throng of colours as stalls sell fruit, veg, clothes, handbags and trainers, jumbled up with the smells of saltfish and raw meat. Walk past the kiosks playing Nigerian pop and the Chines

listings.title: Richard's Home
listings.description:
My home is a 1 bedroom apartment close to central London, with a caravan in the garden for use as a home office or second bedroom. The caravan has electricity, WiFi and a desk, perfect if you need to work remotely.

The home is recently decorated, now with a projector home cinema set up with streaming services.

I have a garden with lots of space, a table, chairs and lights to enjoy the outside space into the evening.

The road is very quiet, it's a cul-de-sac, so there is no traffic noise. It i
listings.area_description: Peckham is a fun neighbourhood only 8 minutes by train to London Bridge station for access to central London (14 minutes into London from my sofa!).  There are lots of great places to eat and drink in Peckham and it has the best rooftop bars in town.

listings.title: Maxine & Phil's house
listings.description:
Welcome to our modern townhouse, ideal for relaxing as well as making the most of the surrounding area and great public transport links into central London.

One comfortable bedroom with a super kingsize bed. A well equipped kitchen leading through to a large open plan sitting/dining room over looking our small,  but beautiful garden, where you can dine outside in good weather. Enjoy the comfort of high-speed wifi and a large smart TV.
listings.area_description: Located in a quiet residential area, our home is about 5 minutes walk from excellent local  cafés and shops including a butchers, greengrocers and fishmongers and about 10 minutes from Peckham Rye Park. We're with in walking distance of Peckham which hosts many wonderful and diverse restaurants. The centre of London is accessible by train in 30 minutes door to door.
listings.other_notes: The house overlooks the trees of one of London's well know Victorian cemeteries where one can walk and admire a wonderful view of St Paul's cathedral

listings.title: Large one bedroom with office and back yard in South London
listings.description:
Our split-level, end of terrace flat has a large bedroom, light filled bathroom, patio office/dining room, and back yard with a dining table. Each space has its own light and feel, giving them a distinctness from each other and making the flat feel very spacious. It's also full of art, objects, and color and feels like staying at a friends house (which you are!).

Sleeping: 
The bedroom is at the top of flat and has a new mattress, down duvet, and down pillows. Light streams through the windows
listings.area_description:
We’re nestled between three much-loved neighbourhoods — Peckham, Nunhead and Dulwich — each wonderfully diverse and full of real London families, great food and lively local spots. There’s always something going on: Time Out named our local pub (3 minute walk away!) the best in London in 2026, Dulwich Hamlet FC boasts a passionate fan base, our local Peckham Rye Park has a weekly Park Run, and Peckham Rye itself is packed with vintage shops, record stores and bustling restaurants.

We also have
listings.other_notes: Accessibility: The entrance to our house is via four outdoor stairs which are somewhat steep; caution should be taken when going up and down. The garden is accessed via the kitchen by a small set of stairs as well, though these are less steep and have a hand rail. The different levels of the home ar

listings.title: Beautiful Victorian family home in the heart of Peckham. 7 mins by train to central london.
listings.description:
Beautiful Victorian family home in the heart of Peckham. 7 mins by train to central london.

We have three bedrooms. 1 x bedroom has king size bed. 1 x bedroom has double bed. 1 x bedroom has single bed with a pull out mattress underneath. 

Family bathroom upstairs and downstairs toilet. 

Large living area with loads of toys and space to sit and relax. 

Kitchen/ diner has just been renovated and has a range cooker everything you need. We can get 8 people around the table. 

Sit under the perg
listings.area_description:
Nunhead Village is within strolling distance with a wide range of independently run restaurants, pubs, cafes, shops, and local gems such as Ayre's bakery, Soper's fishmongers, The Old Nun's Head gastro pub, and other restaurants and cafes surrounding The Green: including the exclusive Kudu, El Vermut, and pizzeria Four Hundred Rabbits to name a few. A new Co-op also recently opened on Evelina Road. 

Vibrant Queens Road, Bellenden Road and East Dulwich are also close by and Peckham Rye Park is t

listings.title: South London design home - reciprocal exchanges only
listings.description:
Our home is design forward, having won various architecture awards and been featured in an exhibit on sustainable housing in London’s Design Museum. 

We love the unique interior of spruce wood, which gives a calming, nature-inspired feel. A real urban retreat. 

Our bedrooms, office, and bathrooms are on the lower floor. And our living, dining and kitchen space are on the top floor, off which there’s a roof terrace. We love the open-plan living this gives us, and the mega high vaulted ceiling m
listings.area_description:
Peckham’s often mentioned as London’s coolest neighbourhood. It’s creative, buzzy, multicultural and filled with independent restaurants, bars, cafes, shops, co-working spaces and cinemas. We’ve got a Google map ready for you with all of our favourite spots, including Frank’s - voted as Europe’s best rooftop bar. 

We live on the edge of Peckham Rye park, with great access to green spaces and right in the heart of Peckham’s restaurant and bar scene. The below links will give you a flavour of wha
listings.other_notes:
PLEASE DON’T SEND GP REQUESTS. Looking for Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Bangkok and New York exchanges for 2026. 

Our home is not child friendly and we prefer not to host young children, having not had great experiences with prior exchanges. But always happy to chat!

As we don’t drive, we’re only lookin

listings.title: Scandi-style cosy sanctuary in the heart of Peckham (RECIPROCAL EXCHANGES ONLY)
listings.description:
Please read before contacting me for an exchange. Thank you!

*SPAIN, ITALY, COPENHAGEN, SWEDEN, AUSTRIA REQUESTS ONLY CURRENTLY*

*My apartment is suitable for solo travellers or couples only*

A spacious one bed flat with a cosy Scandi mid-century design vibe. Comfy south facing balcony area, with views of Peckham. Minutes walk away from the heart of Peckham, with a huge selection of independent bars, restaurants and shops.

A 7 minute walk from Queens Road Peckham overground station and 12 mi
listings.area_description:
Peckham was voted one of the 50 coolest neighbourhoods in the world by Time Out - 

https://www.timeout.com/london/news/were-calling-it-peckham-is-still-londons-coolest-neighbourhood-091719

Nearby Copeland Park is a melting pot of different cultures, art, great independent eateries, roof top bars and cinemas. 

Peckham Rye Park is a great place to spend a Sunday walking followed by a traditional roast in one of the fabulous local pubs.

Here is a nice recent article which gives a feel of the pl

listings.title: 1 bedroom paradise of calm flat in trendy Peckham
listings.description:
PERFECT FOR SOLO/TWO FRIENDS/COUPLE + 1 SMALL CHILD

Spacious 1 bedroom flat Peckham - lovely sash windows, separate kitchen and massive living room with original floorboards and blazing fast wifi. Queens Road is a cool, foodie enclave and my flat has bars, pubs and fantastic restaurants within a 5 min walk.

Peckham’s star has been on the rise and it can now legitimately lay claim to the title of London’s coolest neighbourhood. With Goldsmiths on one side and Camberwell Art School on the other
listings.area_description:
Peckham is a fun and buzzy place to explore, with my flat being on Queens Road side, giving you a calm and serene spot from which to plan your next outing. Queens Road station is literally 3 mins walk away and then you’re up in town and the world is yours.

https://londontheinside.com/map/peckham/

Kudu and Peckham Cellars are 2 min

- Buzzy restaurants: Kudu or Peckham Cellars 1 min walk away, Naifs well reviewed vegan spot 3 mins away, Levan 15 mins away if you are celebrating.

- Cool bars: B
listings.other_notes: One small child max - not suitable for families of 4 or more

listings.title: Nicole's house
listings.other_notes: Habitación disponible para una persona solo

listings.title: Family home in friendly London area
listings.description: Our family home is a typical London Victorian Terrace house, it is situated on a quiet tree-lined street in Telegraph Hill which is one of London's historic conservation areas. You will find all the features of a typical Victorian house including ceiling roses and original floorboards. Telegraph Hill features two quaint London parks with spectacular views over London, a farmers market every Saturday and a fantastic community vibe. We are a short walk from the local shops, bars and restaurants of
listings.area_description:
Our immediate area of Telegraph Hill has two incredible parks with amazing views over London, free tennis courts and a small skate park. It is one of London's conservation areas so you will see very traditional, original Victorian housing and features throughout. On Saturdays there is a cute farmers market selling organic produce and delicacies. 

It is an area long associated with artists and musicians and this is reflected in the local food and drink options. At the top of the hill is a local
listings.other_notes:
--> Typically we are looking for summer and easter holiday destinations, ideally somewhere hot and sunny.

--> We have young kids so for reciprocal summer exchanges a pool is pretty much a must for us.

--> We have a cat (Salvador), who needs looking after. He is pretty independent but needs feeding

listings.title: Colourful, architect-designed family home in Peckham, London
listings.description:
Our house has been recently renovated by award-winning architects. It is colourful, spacious and bright. It’s great for a family or groups of guests that want to enjoy a big, beautiful kitchen and dining area and wide open access to the back garden.

We require a cleaning fee for non-reciprocal/GP exchanges.
listings.area_description: Our home is in the heart of trendy Peckham, London. We are close to great public transportation, lovely parks, playgrounds, cafes, restaurants and more.

listings.title: Cyrus's house
listings.description: My home is a large one-bedroom, in the heart of Peckham. The building is rumoured to be the oldest in Peckham (or second), but for sure you will get a sense of what the area would have been like when once a small village! It is super modern, and I keep it quite clutter free. A lovely living space looks into a 40sqm private terrace that is south facing, so if the sun is shining you will get sun and light all day. The large bedroom has a separate wardrobe and dressing area, and a king size bed. Go
listings.area_description: Peckham is one of the more popular areas on South East London, boasting some of the areas best bars and restaurants. It has quite a reputation for being "up and coming" and "hip", but honestly it has everything from nice coffee shops, great cocktails, lots of ethnic food stores for buying all the ingredients. The apartment is close to two stations and gets you into different parts of central of London in 15-30 minutes (depending on where you are going).

listings.title: 3 bedroom cottage with a garden in leafy Peckham Rye, London
listings.description:
Cosy house in leafy Peckham Rye, London.

5 minutes walk to Bellenden Road and Rye Lane with it's plethora of restaurants, cafes and bakeries. 

Walking distance to Peckham Rye Park and Dulwich Park. 

Less than 30 minutes to central London including London Bridge and London Victoria.
listings.area_description: Quiet and leafy

listings.title: Flavia's house
listings.description:
Welcome to my flat in London! It’s a very bright, light drenched first floor flat in a modern building. Ideal for a short stay in London, for a single person or a couple. 

There is one master bedroom, 1 bathroom with bathtub, 1 spacious living room (with sofabed!) and a fully equipped kitchen. 

Enjoy a very cosy home in the heart of Hackney. 5 min walk to overground station Rectory Road which connects to Liverpool street through to Central London. 

I have a smart projector which can be used t
listings.area_description:
The Flat is located in a quiet residential space but very close to main roads and public transport. 

There is a lovely park 5mins walk away, which also has tennis courts and basketball courts!

Lots of cafes nearby, my personal favourites are Bake Street and Bean Café. There are a gym and sauna 5 mins away, and you are only 10 mins walk from the incredible bakery Casey’s which is on the main road. If you keep walking down there are lots of cute shops and restaurants. 

If you’re into a longer w

listings.title: Victorian terraced house in hip and central south London area
listings.description: 3-storey Victorian House with the feel of a large apartment in an attractive crescent adjacent to a lovely small park, in a hip central south London area.  The house is recently refurbished with spacious lounge, large kitchen/dining-room, bath/shower-room, bath/wet room and mature garden. There are two double bedrooms and an office..  Decor is eclectic and reflects my travels. There is unrestricted car parking in the street. The 2nd bedroom can be arranged as a double or a twin.
listings.area_description:
Hip central south London area. Just around the corner is a long, buzzy high street ( becoming a 'little France') with cafes, bars, delis, independent shops, a Saturday street market, local cinema. Close to 3 parks. 

Inner London (Zone 2) - the station is 2 minutes' walk away (10 mins' ride to London Bridge) and regular buses from the end of the street take you to all parts of the west end, the city, Shoreditch, Brixton within half an hour (as little as 15 mins in a car).
listings.other_notes:
Beautiful original features and character
Lively, fashionable and cool area, close to parks
Great transport links to all parts of London

listings.title: Emma's house
listings.description:
Our home is a ground floor maisonette with two bedrooms and a study. It is very quiet and also has a private garden which is lovely in summer! 

Bedrooms: There are two equally sized bedrooms, one with a small double bed and one with a king size bed. There is also a cot for a baby. It is suitable for a couple or small family (it is not appropriate for large groups). 

Our comfortable living room has a TV (with Netflix etc.) and record player, and looks out onto the sunny garden. As we have a you
listings.area_description: We live in Nunhead which is a quiet residential area in south east London. It has a village-type feel, with lots of independent shops and cafes close by. It's also just a 15 minute walk from Peckham, which has some great restaurants as well as nightlife options! South east London is very green, with a few lovely parks within walking distance. If you want to go into central London, you can be there in approximately 30 minutes with public transport.
listings.other_notes: We have family and friends in Edinburgh and Berlin so always keen for reciprocal exchanges in either of those locations.

listings.title: Bright and quiet 1 Bed. 10m to London Bridge
listings.description:
Hi! My apartment is modern, light and very close to a tube station for travelling into/around London

I have lived here for 10 years and gradually redesigned and redecorated it to my own standards. It has a very light (English weather permitting!) living room with large patio windows facing due south. There are quite a few plants, and the apartment is mostly quite quiet and peaceful, being away from the main road.

All the mod cons really (except aircon): dishwasher, central heating, Sonos syste
listings.area_description:
The immediate area is Queens Road Peckham which is mostly residential with a small supermarket (Tesco), and a nice bakery. The tube station (Queens Road Peckham) has trains and overground connections going into London:
- London Bridge: 9 mins
- Shoreditch: 18 mins
- Westminster: 20-25 mins
- Charing Cross (Trafalgar Square): 25 mins

 Nearby (10m walk) you have Telegraph Hill,  Nunhead and Peckham which have lots of bars, restaurants, shops, pubs and green spaces

listings.title: Family-friendly period home in a fun London neighbourhood
listings.description:
Welcome to our South London family home! We love our classic Victorian terrace, lovingly renovated to keep its period features and add a spacious kitchen-diner looking onto the garden. 

We have four bedrooms – two ensuite doubles (one with shower), one office with sofa bed, and a kid's room with changing table and cot for our two-year-old son (plus an extra travel cot if needed). Downstairs, the double reception room is a good spot to relax with some TV, and adjoins a play area with lots of toy
listings.area_description:
We're in the heart of East Dulwich – a buzzy, family-friendly neighbourhood with lots of shops, pubs, parks and restaurants. We love a Saturday walk with coffee from Bon, a stroll through North Cross Road market and Eric's for the most incredible pastries! Goose Green Park is just round the corner with a great playground. Peckham (and lovely Peckham Rye park) is another foodie hipster neighbourhood about 15 minutes' walk away.

We're a few minutes' walk from East Dulwich station, with trains to

listings.title: Tanc & Izy's house
listings.description: Cosy mid-century modern home with a large open plan kitchen / living room with stunning views on private garden (mind the wild fox!). Three bedrooms upstairs with plenty of natural light. Downstairs toilet and upstairs bathroom.
listings.area_description:
Situated in a quiet conservation area in the heart of the Nunhead village. Nearby local amenities (fishmonger, butcher, pubs and bakery) and only a 10-15min walk from the train stations (Nunhead and Peckham Rye). Local trains take 15min-20min to reach the centre, alternatively it takes 20-25min by e-bike or 45min by double decker bus.

Nunhead is full of green spaces and strikes the perfect balance between hip Peckham (great restaurants, bars and rooftops) and more bougie East Dulwich (higher en

listings.title: Modern 3-bed, cool area, Zone 2 public transport, perfect London base.
listings.description:
- Our modern three-bedroom house is in a quiet, safe street of family houses – yet it is right in the centre of lively, multi-cultural Peckham.

- Our home would make an excellent base for exploring London! Peckham is a real public transport hub, and we’re just five minutes' walk from our station, with speedy journeys direct to Victoria, London Bridge, St Pancras International and the South Bank. 

- Don't feel like cooking? There are so many amazing restaurants and bars on our doorstep that it’
listings.area_description:
Recently listed as No. 11 in the 50 coolest districts in the world (according to Time Out), Peckham is lively, multi-cultural and an exciting part of London to be based – and it is a popular arty/foodie destination for young Londoners (and the young-at-heart).
 
We have amazing craft beer and cocktail bars, traditional pubs, an independent-producer food court, and a multitude of eclectic restaurants – Vietnamese, Thai, Italian, Kurdish, Persian, African, south Indian, modern European, traditiona
listings.other_notes: The two members of the household who won’t ever be travelling are our two part-Siamese nine-year-old cats. They are independent and house-trained, and go in and out as they please through their cat-flap. They’re not used to going to a cattery, and we’d ideally like to leave them with our home-exchan

listings.title: Spacious one bedroom flat in Peckham/ Nunhead with a large sunny garden.
listings.description:
Welcome to our spacious home, perfect for visiting London. We're minutes away from public transport into the heart of the city, as well as being close by to Telegraph Hill and Nunhead's charming green space.

We have a double bedroom, office, a fully equipped kitchen and an open plan kitchen and living room. 

Enjoy high-speed Wifi and a TV, alongside a large sunny garden fit with a BBQ.
listings.area_description: We're on the border of Nunhead and Peckham, with Queens Road Peckham as our closest station. Nunhead is known for its "village feel," Victorian architecture, local shops on Evelina Road, and green spaces like Nunhead Cemetery. Peckham is a larger, more diverse area with a reputation for its creative energy, markets, and a lively social scene, bordering Nunhead and sharing many of its green spaces like Peckham Rye Park

listings.title: 2 bed + garden apartment in trendy Peckham, London
listings.description: Two double bedroom, spacious and modern flat in a trendy part of London, Camberwell/Peckham. The flat is in a period, London building but recently refurbished and well equipped. Open plan living/kitchen, with bifold doors opening out to a private, quiet garden - a lovely space to relax after enjoying London life. There is a desk in each bedroom and excellent wifi (we work from home) if you are planning to work here. There is a bbq, garden seating, garden dining table and fire pit in the garden.
listings.area_description: Camberwell/Peckham is a great part of London - a nice mix of calm/residential, picturesque streets & lively parts with good pubs, bars, local cafes and restaurants. (The the variety and quality of local independent restaurants is amazing and we can recommend lots!). As well having several nearby parks and green spaces. Easy to get into central London (10 minute direct train to Victoria or London Bridge). Peckham Rye and Denmark Hill stations are both ~10minute walks and lots of bus routes from C

listings.title: London Flat - looking for LA
listings.description: Located on the 5th floor, with a balcony overlooking Canary Wharf. Our modern, clean and well equipped apartment is perfectly situated for many transport links accessing central London.
listings.area_description:
We live south of the river in the up and coming area of New Cross, close to trendy Peckham and its many restaurants, bars, cafes, yoga and exercise studios.

South Bank - 30 mins by bus
Covent Garden - 40 mins by bus
London Bridge - 25 mins by overground and bus
Shoreditch - 35 mins on overground
listings.other_notes: Two bedroom flat - but one of the bedrooms is used as an office, so we have one double bed. Their is room in the living room for a small children's mattress, if needed.

listings.title: The Blue House, Peckham
listings.description:
The Blue House has been a landmark in Peckham since the mural was painted in the 1990s by a local artist. This cosy but spacious 2 bedroom Victorian apartment is split across three levels in a quiet pocket of south London, right between Camberwell and Peckham and with views on every side of the house.

A 10 minute train ride directly into central London, and a short cycle ride to the river, the Blue House is a great base to explore London from. You will return to a 'home from home' setting in a
listings.area_description:
I live right in the middle of Peckham and Camberwell. The closest train station is either Denmark Hill or Peckham Rye. Each station is a 10 minute walk to the house. 

Both stations can be reached directly via Blackfriars, Victoria, London Bridge or King's Cross / St Pancras stations. 

This area of South London has long been a base for artists and designers due to its proximity to Camberwell College of Arts and Goldsmiths. As well as art galleries, independent shops and music venues such as
listings.other_notes:
We are looking for a reciprocal house exchange in either: 
October 2025 (Amsterdam or Marseille)
OR in either the first week of January or Easter holidays (Canary Islands or Skiing location in France)

listings.title: Wonderful 1 bed flat with garden
listings.description:
The perfect blend of comfort and convenience in my charming one-bedroom flat, nestled in the heart of South London's vibrant Queen's Road Peckham (Zone 2). 

Charming Interiors: This one-bedroom flat offers a warm and inviting atmosphere. The bedroom features a comfortable double bed, a spacious wardrobe, and a desk, perfect for both rest and productivity.

Well-Equipped Kitchen: The separate kitchen is fully stocked with modern appliances, including a fridge, washing machine, new hobs and oven,
listings.area_description:
Experience the best of both worlds with a peaceful residential vibe and exciting urban attractions just minutes away:

Prime Location: Situated in Zone 2, South London, my neighbourhood offers the tranquillity of a residential area while being just steps away from vibrant city life.

Excellent Transport Links: With several train stations and bus stops just a 5-minute walk away, you’ll have quick and easy access to the city centre, East London, and South London. Exploring the city couldn’t be mor

listings.title: Luxury modern London apartment in trendy Peckham
listings.description:
Welcome to the coolest part of London!

My flat is a 5 year-old high-spec apartment designed to a top level providing both a modern, yet peaceful oasis from busy London. I love the fact that being at the back of the block, the apart is so quiet - perfect for unwinding with a good book after a long day!

The flat is based in vibrant Peckham, full of great restaurants and bars with a beautiful park just a 20 minute walk away. If you’d like to get into central London, the station is less than 5 min
listings.area_description:
The apartment is set back from the road with a large balcony, providing peace and quiet away from the excitement that London has to offer. 

When you want to step out, Peckham - one of London's coolest areas - is just on your doorstep. Full of independent restaurants and bars, Peckham has everything to offer without the big, corporate feeling you might feel in more central locations. Why not take a walk up to the beautiful park before grabbing a drink at Frank's and then heading onto Peckham roo
listings.other_notes: There is a £80 cleaning fee payable on arrival to ensure all guests have a pleasant stay. This can be paid for by cash/bank transfer on arrival.

listings.title: Zone 2 Spacious Victorian House with Large Garden in East Dulwich
listings.description: Our home is a large family home in South East London with easy access to the main attractions of the city, with the villages of East Dulwich and Bellenden Road on the doorstep. It has a large garden with two seating areas, so you can catch the morning or the evening light. There are monkey bars, a Wendy house and plenty of toys to play with for younger guests. There are 3 generous doubles, a nursery with a crib/ toddler bed and a 5th room where we can put up a cot as needed.
listings.area_description: Our home is situated between East Dulwich and Peckham, both of which are transport hubs and have great restaurants and bars. The Bellenden road is on the doorstep for cafes, delis, shops and cosy pubs. Goose Green has a children's playground just a few minutes walk away and Peckham Rye park offers lots of green space just 10 minutes walk away.

listings.title: Light & airy home in arty & foody Peckham
listings.description:
I would like to welcome you to my home for the duration of your stay in London. My home is located in Peckham which is an arty and leafy area of south-east London. It is close to the bustling market street (Rye Lane), but is just far enough away to be quiet with no traffic noise (located on a cul-de-sac).

The home has three double bedrooms - 1 x king-size, 1 x queen and 1 x small (i.e. 120cm). The master (king double) bedroom is usually my room. If we do a swap on the same dates, then this room
listings.area_description: Peckham is a particularly ethnically diverse part of London and that is reflected in the many restaurant and market options in the area. There are several Michelin rated restaurants within walking distance of my home. If you are a pub lover you are in for a treat, as there are many nearby, as well as two breweries within throwing distance and many more just a short journey away in Bermondsey. You could even visit the pub I part-own in New Cross - The Shirker's Rest - where you are guaranteed a w

listings.title: Spacious 3 bedroom Victorian house with lovely garden in South East London
listings.description:
Welcome to our spacious and charming victorian mid-terrace family home, ideally located in the heart of Nunhead (Zone 2)!

Our home blends the best of modern living with the timeless charm of a period property. Situated between Queens Road Peckham and Nunhead stations, it’s just an 8-minute train ride to London Bridge and 20 minutes to London Victoria, perfect for travelers and tourists looking to explore the city.

Key features of our beloved home:

Ground Floor:
Step into our bright and spacio
listings.area_description:
Nunhead is a vibrant and characterful neighbourhood in South East London, offering a perfect blend of urban convenience and local charm. Just a few minutes from the high street, you'll find everything you need: supermarkets, a butcher, greengrocer, fishmonger, and a selection of independent stores, cafes, pubs and restaurants to explore.

In nearby Peckham, you'll find 3 rooftop bars overlooking London (Frank's, Bussey and Forza Wine), a cinema, 
For green spaces, you're spoilt for choice. Enjoy

listings.title: Top Floor Apartment with Great Views Over London
listings.description: Our home is a lovely modern top-floor (4th) apartment with two balconies, two bedrooms, and two bathrooms. The second bedroom has a fold out double sofa bed, desk and en-suite bathroom. One of the balconies wraps around the building, with access from the main bedroom and lounge, and has great views of London. We love sitting out with a glass of wine and watching the sunset over the Shard and the central London skyline.
listings.area_description: We live in Nunhead - recently ranked as one of the best suburbs to live in London. We love Nunhead for its relaxed village-like atmosphere with great local pubs, cafes and independent shops. Two minutes from our doorstep is the expansive Peckham Rye park, which has beautiful gardens and playgrounds and is a great place to exercise or just have a picnic. Our apartment is also only 10 minutes’ walk from bustling Peckham Rye Lane, with its cultural diversity, and bars and restaurants that frequentl
listings.other_notes:
Our flat is well connected to central London - with several buses into the city within a 3-5 minute walk, and direct trains to London Victoria and London Blackfriars within a short (13 minute) walk. You can be in central London within 30-40 minutes.

Please note that for July 2023 we are ideally loo

listings.title: Victorian property in lovely part of London.

Plus the overground that takes you up to North London. We have a beautiful local cemetery, 10mins walk from the house, which is so lovely to walk around. There is a big park with a large pl

There is a downstairs toilet as well as the upstairs bathroom.
No smoking, no pets.

listings.title: Becca's house- peckham, London. Colourful Victorian terrace with small garden in zone 2

Quick links to centre of London by train bus and overground as well as other notable areas of south east London - like Greenwich, Brixton, east dulwich.
