listings.title: Sophie's house
listings.description:
This is our beautiful and much-loved family home and garden, in the heart of the popular Peckham Rye conservation area. Central London only 12 minutes away by train.  

Ideal for family stays. The four-storey, three-bedroomed house, though just a few minutes away from the station, is situated on a peaceful, no-through road, with a park directly opposite. Private off-street parking. 

No  pets or smoking please.
listings.area_description: Peckham is South London's liveliest corner. Within a 5-minute walk:  a rooftop cafe and sculpture park with amazing views of the centre, London's cheapest cinema, two dozen fabulous restaurants, art galleries, boutique shops and delis. 10 minutes: a dozen more restaurants. 20 minutes: beautiful Peckham Rye Park and its big kids' playground.
listings.other_notes:
We would ask guests to pay a modest cleaning fee to cover the costs of their stay, agreeable prior to booking. 

There is a pond in the garden with child-safety grill on top. Please do feed the fish.

listings.title: Tal's house
listings.description:
Our family home is comfortable and cosy for the 6 of us and our 2 cats. The house is on a terraced street just off Bellenden Road with all the amenities. 

The house can accommodate 5-7 people. Upstairs there are two double rooms and a single room along with a shower room and toilet.
Downstairs there is a front room occupied by a lodger (not available for visitors) and a kitchen diner looking out onto the long garden and an insulated garden room with a sofa and music system to hang out in. 

Th
listings.area_description: Our house is right in the heart of Peckham , a vibrant neighbourhood in zone 2, 15 minutes from London Bridge and well connected on the overland trains to the city’s many central locations. Close to Bellenden Road and Rye Lane, a visitor can enjoy a wide variety of restaurants, shops and art and music venues. With children there are several parks in close proximity.

listings.title: Harry’s Flat
listings.description: First floor flat in a new development. Large balcony and brand new kitchen and bathroom facilities. Decorated in a tasteful way with nice quality sofa, bed, and dinner table.
listings.area_description: Located on a quiet residential street in Peckham — a neighbourhood celebrated for its vibrant and diverse community. The area is known for its avant-garde bars and clubs, as well as a well-established food scene featuring cuisine from around the world. Peckham also holds a strong place within London’s arts landscape, home to renowned galleries and institutions, alongside independent project spaces and artist-run initiatives.

listings.title: Scott's house
listings.description: A light quiet sanctuary in the heart of vibrant area of South London, With great links to East London and Central
listings.area_description: Peckham is a mixed area and Caribbean area, with many Cafes, eating options, and a vibrant Pub and Bar Scene. Markets and Highstreet. Plenty of Green Spaces around for lovely walks in Parks.

listings.title: Donald's House

listings.area_description: On the border of Nunhead and Peckham (named the world's eleventh coolest neighbourhood in the 2019 Time Out Index). Twelve minutes by train to London Bridge station (Borough Market. Tate Modern) and fifteen minutes by bus to Greenwich World Heritage Site and National Maritime Museum.

listings.title: Family house with garden
listings.description: A spacious Victorian house with a cozy, family-friendly vibe and a garden that’s perfect for chilling or letting the kids run wild. It's got that old-school charm, but with all the modern comforts you need. Just a quick 10-minute train ride to London Bridge, so you can enjoy the peace and quiet of the neighborhood but still be close to everything London has to offer.
listings.area_description: Nestled in the relaxed, leafy streets of Nunhead, this spot is all about good vibes and easy living. It’s a quiet neighborhood, but you’re only a stone’s throw from Peckham, where things are a little more electric. Think cool cafes, street art, indie shops, and a nightlife scene that’s definitely got a bit of a buzz. You’ve got the best of both worlds—chill at home, but step out and dive into the heart of South London. Plus, the train’s only 10 minutes to London Bridge, so you're never far from

listings.title: Laurène’s home
listings.description: Small attic flat located in Nunhead, near Peckham.
listings.area_description: Very quiet residential area, the closest shops are on Evelina Road. Peckham Rye is 15-20 min walk and has a lot of cool bars and restaurants

listings.title: Scott's house
listings.description:
My apartment is nestled in the heart of Peckham, offering an oasis of calm in this vibrant London neighborhood.

It features a comfortable bedroom with a cozy double bed, a compact but fully equipped kitchen, a modern bathroom, and a medium-sized living area. The layout is thoughtfully planned to maximize space and comfort.

Amenities:  The apartment offers all the e

Cultural Hub: 
Peckham thrives as an arts and cultural center, attracting artists and enthusiasts alike. The iconic Peckham Levels stands as a testament to the neighborhood's creative dynamism.

Foodie Paradise: 
Peckham caters to food lovers with its bustling Rye Lane Market, featuring international cuisines, fresh produce, and artisanal goods. Trendy
listings.other_notes: It's a quiet flat. Looking to exchange with people who respect the neighbors. Thats not to say you can't have fun.

listings.title: Peckham Pad - 3 bed London home
listings.description:
Our home is a stylish recently renovated three bedroom Victorian terraced house in vibrant Peckham, London. 

Downstairs features an open plan kitchen looking out onto the garden, a double lounge with fireplace, comfy sofa and smart TV, and small WC. 

The main bedroom has a king sized bed, second bedroom a double bed and the third bedroom is set up as an office/nursery with a cot/toddler bed and electronic seated/standing desk so perfect if you're looking to work from "home".

The private (and
listings.area_description:
Peckham is a great place to live! We love it as there is an abundance of amazing restaurants right on your doorstep, coffee shops galore and so many brilliant pubs. It is  a vibrant part of London and we have lots of recommendations we can share!

A short walk away from the house is Peckham Rye - a large leafy park perfect if you're a keen runner or just fancy a walk.

From Peckham Rye station (6 min walk away) you can train into London Bridge/Victoria/Blackfriars in under 15 minutes, and there

listings.title: Rebecca’s House
listings.description: Beautiful house with lots of space and a lovely small garden.
listings.area_description: Very well located close to everything you could need. Parks, shops and everything London has to offer!

listings.title: Beautiful 4 bedroom Victorian house, 15 mins to central London
listings.description:
Now booked for July / August 2018. Open to offers for long weekends / school holidays during the rest of 2018/2019. 

Beautiful Victorian 4 bed / 2 bathroom, 1600 square foot, terraced house in residential street between leafy Dulwich and fashionable Peckham Rye. The house is on three storeys. The ground floor consists of a large high-ceilinged open-plan double living room and dining room, with separate kitchen diner backing out onto a garden split between a paved patio area and a small lawn.
listings.area_description:
Peckham Rye is 'the best place to live in London' (Time Out) 
https://www.timeout.com/london/blog/peckham-has-been-named-the-best-place-to-live-in-london-032017

There are lots of buzzy restaurants and cafes. Check out Begging bowl (Thai), Artusi (Italian), Franco Manca (pizza) all within 10 minutes walk from our front door. We've also got a wide range of great family pubs and cool cocktail bars equally close (eg Bar Story, Peckham Levels, Franks, The Victoria, The Montpellier, EDT, Great Exh
listings.other_notes:
Chic and comfortable four bedroom family house
Non touristy area, with some of London's best local cafes, restaurants, bars and parks on your doorstep. Peckham Rye is 'the best place to live in London', according to Time Out

listings.title: Cosy and Colourful Family Home
listings.description: Our warm, art-filled family home is a Regency-era house with a beautiful and mature front and back garden with sauna.  The cosy family room is the perfect place for chillaxing together during a quiet night in, while the open plan dining room/ kitchen/ sitting room is filled with lush houseplants, books, a vinyl record setup and all the amenities needed to cook up a gourmet meal.  There is a ground floor double-bed guest room with en suite toilet and shower, while upstairs the master bedrooms (al
listings.area_description: We live in buzzy, diverse Peckham, a funky and beloved Southeast London landmark that is chock full of restaurants, bars, and shops to buy groceries and goods from all over the world.  Two large, beautiful parks are less than 15 minutes' walk away, while a smaller dog-friendly park is just 5 minutes around the corner.  Excellent transportation connections including Peckham Rye station, with easy connections to Victoria station (15 minutes, 2 stops), London Bridge (15 minutes, 3 stops), Blackfria

You can reach anywhere in central London between 15-60 minutes, or stay local and enjoy the restaurants, shops and bars of Peckham and Camberwell, or nearby Dulwich and Brixton.  W

listings.title: Beautiful South London Townhouse
listings.description: Our spacious home has recently been lovingly refurbished so it’s now a space we truly love to share with others. It benefits from high ceilings and a wonderfully large garden that’s now brimming with flowers. We have two double bedrooms & a large living area with a double Sofabed. We enjoy an unusually large bathroom with both a bath and shower. Downstairs we have a double lounge and kitchen with all the modern conveniences.
listings.area_description: Our Zone 2 home is situated on one of the most desirable roads in Peckham. The end is pedestrianised with a communal garden lined with blossom trees. At the Queens road end of the road are several great restaurants and wine bars including Peckham Cellars, Beer Rebellion and Kudu (be sure to book ahead for Kudu). Queens road Peckham station is less than a five minute walk from the house - with trains taking just 7 minutes to get to London Bridge. For a village vibe take a walk up to leafy Nunhead
listings.other_notes: We have two friendly and well behaved cats who’d need to stay during your trip. Meg and Ted are home bodies who never stray far from the garden. We hope their presence will make you feel at home.

listings.title: Emma's house
listings.description:
A modern 4 bedroom terraced house in a great location in SE London. We live a 10 minute walk from 2 train stations that will take you to central London in 30 minutes.

We have 4 spacious bedrooms bedrooms, 2 have large comfortable double beds and 2 have single beds. There are 2 bathrooms - one with a walk in shower and a bathtub. Another bathroom has a walk in shower.

The large kitchen has a table and chairs so you can easily eat and cook together. Oven, grill, large fridge & freezer. No microw
listings.area_description: We live on a beautiful street in South East London in Zone 2. A 10 minute walk to 2 train stations taking you direct to town and many tourist attractions. Locally you will find our lovely high street with all the shops you will need such as baker, butcher, fruit and veg shop. There are several beautiful parks and open spaces within walking distance to enjoy.

listings.title: Spacious family home in London.
listings.description: Our home is a beautiful house in a block of red brick 1950s houses. It has a huge completely safe shared garden at the front and a big size private garden at the back. On the ground floor, there are a big living room, a live-in kitchen and a toilet. On the first floor there are 4 double bedrooms with a family bathroom. You only need to cross the street to find Dulwich Park and Dulwich Village, one of the quaintest and most picturesque parts of London. Ten minutes walk and you'll be at the Hornim
listings.area_description: Our neighbourhood is safe and 100% family friendly, dotted with woods, parks, adventure playgrounds, museums, restaurants and pubs with open space. And at the same time trendy and with a village like atmosphere. A true oasis of quiet in a busy town.
listings.other_notes:
It is quiet yet central
Good outdoor space, close to parks, shops and restaurants
It is spacious and light.
Please note that for non reciprocal exchanges there is a £50 cleaning fee.

listings.title: Valentina's private room - one person only
listings.description: It's a private bedroom in the residential area near Dulwich village. The nearest station is Forest hill or jump on any bus to get to the city centre. We usually travel by bike (25 mins to Trafalgar Square), we have a bike you can use. we're a friendly family of 5, my eldest son studies out of town and my husband is often away.
listings.area_description: Quiet residential neighborhood with amazing parks, woods and two golf courses nearby. Lots of places where to grab a drink and independent shops and eateries. Farmers market every Saturday and the famous horniman museum and Dulwich college a short walk away
listings.other_notes: You will be sharing with me and the kids or my husband or with both when one of the kids is away

listings.area_description: Dulwich Village is a beautiful part if London. One of the few urban villages. Check out these websites to give you a flavour of this amazing area: https://earthsmagicalplaces.com/guide-to-dulwich-village-london/  and https://www.aladyinlondon.com/2019/04/dulwich-village.html                         We love the convenience of nearby family friendly restaurants like Rocca di Papa. There is a pub in Dulwich Village and plenty more on Lordship Lane in East Dulwich. https://www.aladyinlondon.com/2018
listings.other_notes:
Family friendly home in Dulwich Village - a stunning part of London.
We are close to bus stops and trains to central London.
We have a beautiful sunny garden to chill out in after the hustle and bustle of London life.

details.full_description:
We do not have any availability for exchanges in 2025 and are only looking for a short exchange in October 2026 and then again for multipe exchanges from 2027
 
We are looking for Exchanges for 2026: 
October - 5-7 days city break: Athens, Stockholm, Istanbul

Our home is set in a beautiful part of London called Dulwich Village and it is popular with families. It is a very leafy suburb of London and within easy reach of the city centre by bus or train. It is Travel Zone 2 (the city centre is Zone 1 and Zones go up to 6 and beyond). The nearest bus stop is a five minute walk away and the nearest train station (North Dulwich) less than 10 minutes - you can take trains to Victoria station from here - takes about 12 minutes. The nearest tube station for the Victoria Line is in Brixton - to get to it you need to take a bus.

Our home is a stunning  and spacious semi-detached double fronted property located in the heart of Dulwich Village. It has a pretty front garden with secured bike shed and parking space for two cars. There is a very large and attractive large reception room with wood burning fireplace and an upright piano at the front of the house. To the rear of the property is a fabulous kitchen/dining/family room with stunning views over the large well maintained secluded garden.  

We have four bedrooms, the master bedroom has a superking size bed and an ensuite bathroom, one double bedroom with toys, one bedroom with a single bed and one double bedroom on the ground floor (no stairs to access). In addition we have a number of single mattresses for additional guests and a travel cot (all need to be requested in advance).

There is a large kitchen diner, a utility room with shower facilities, a lovely living room and a family bathroom as well as a suntrap of a garden with garden office space which also has a small bathroom. If you are coming in the summer we ask you to water the plants during hot weather. We have a TV as well as WIFI. 

Kitchen Dining Room - equipped with induction cooker, two ovens (one doubles as a microwave), fridge freezer, dishwasher, Nespresso coffee maker, toaster, air fryer, kettle tap and Amazon Alexa for entertainment. Large dining table, sofa and coffee table. This space is large and airy with patio doors opening to our secluded garden which is home to a large variety of birds as well as squirrels.

Utility room - large sink, washing machine, tumble drier, toilet and shower. Direct access to garden.
Living Room - with TV. Another large and light room with fireplace.
Two Offices
Family Bathroom - bathtub with shower over
Master bedroom - super kingsize bed and ensuite bathroom
Bedroom 2 - Single bed, desk, wardrobe
Bedroom 3 - Children's bedroom with a double bed, wardrobe, sink and lots of toys and books
Bedroom 4 - Double bed with desk, wardrobe, chest of drawers
Garden - Large and secluded. Two tables with chairs, a hammock, swing chair, BBQ and ping pong table.
Garden House with sofabed and additional toilet facilities.

The house swap includes our cat Ava who is very sociable and will love your care and attention. She is used to home exchangers.

For reciprocal exchanges we are able to offer car exchange - you will need to arrange your own insurance prior to arrival as our insurance does not cover additional drivers. We offer our VW id.3, an electric car which is excellent for London driving, very economical, there are lots of EV chargers and you do not have to pay the congestion charge to drive into central London. Obviously the car can also leave London and is a great runner on the motorway. There are rapid chargers at service stations if you want to take a very long drive!
details.full_area_description:
Dulwich Village is a beautiful part if London. One of the few urban villages. Check out these websites to give you a flavour of this amazing area: https://earthsmagicalplaces.com/guide-to-dulwich-village-london/  and https://www.aladyinlondon.com/2019/04/dulwich-village.html                         We love the convenience of nearby family friendly restaurants like Rocca di Papa. There is a pub in Dulwich Village and plenty more on Lordship Lane in East Dulwich. https://www.aladyinlondon.com/2018/12/east-dulwich.html Or a short bus ride away you find trendy Brixton! This area also has lots of wonderful cafes. Highly recommend Gails in Dulwich Village. Enjoy the local shops on Lordship Lane or alternatively hit central London!

Dulwich Picture Gallery is within walking distance as well as the Horniman Museum. We are just a short stroll to Dulwich Park and a slightly longer walk to Brockwell Park and Peckham Rye Park
 
We are in Zone 2 so you are within easy reach of all the fantastic sights of London. Several buses and a train take you into the heart of London. Nearest train stations are North Dulwich and Herne Hill. Nearest bus stops are Dulwich Village School and Turney Road. Nearest tube station is Brixton which is accessible by bus (P4, 3, 37)
details.full_other:
Family friendly home in Dulwich Village - a stunning part of London.
We are close to bus stops and trains to central London.
We have a beautiful sunny garden to chill out in after the hustle and bustle of London life.

3 bedrooms

double reception

186 m^2

listings.area_description:

We have 3 massive parks within walking distance, and we’re adjacent to Dulwich village, which feels like a country village, but still within zone 2. Independent shops and great restaurants, check out Lulu's, Llewelyn's, Naughty Piglets, Theo's, Rocca di Papa to get some inspiration.

excellent pubs within a 10 minute walk as well (the half moon pub, and the crown and greyhound).

listings.title: Elektra and Brad's house
listings.description:
We have a quirky and characterful two-bedroom garden flat in leafy West Dulwich, South East London. 
The flat has high ceilings and big windows which make it light and spacious. I describe it as quirky because we are on the first floor and access to our private garden is via a hidden staircase under a trapdoor in the kitchen. We have lots of house plants, including a beautiful golden pothos vine which I have trained to grow along the ceiling over our bed. The building is from 1900 and there is n
listings.area_description: West Dulwich is a fantastic neighbourhood because it looks like a beautiful leafy English village but is actually in the midst of London. We get the best of both! There is a real sense of community with our excellent local independent shops: West Dulwich bakery, Scotch butchery, organic green grocers and several authentic Italian delis. We also have a fantastic local pub called the Rosendale at the end of the road, it does great food as well as a nice cold pint. Opposite the house there is a fam

listings.title: Beautiful home in East Dulwich
listings.description: Our home is in a quiet leafy area of South London called East Dulwich. We love our local area as it has many options for going out. Our house is a haven of peace with a small and very peaceful back garden. We have decorated our house throughout with colourful schemes and we find it very cosy. Our bedrooms are very comfortable and the top one in the loft has a breathtaking view of London's skyline.
listings.area_description:
Our home is in a quiet leafy area of South London called East Dulwich. We love our local area as it has many options for going out, pubs, restaurants, cafés but also a cinema and many lovely shops. It's very quaint and boho. There are also many gyms, an art gallery, a village with pretty shops and pubs, and several beautiful parks.
Travelling to central London is very straightforward as we have a bus stop at the front door taking you to Charing Cross, Trafalgar Square, Leicester Square, Piccadil

listings.title: Fabulous 5-bedroom house in London Zone 2
listings.description:
We are delighted to welcome visitors to share our home in Dulwich. It’s a lovely 5-bedroom Victorian house, recently extended and renovated to provide a great family space.

We have an outstanding large and light kitchen / family room with a glass roof casting incredible shadows and floods the room with light until sunset.  This opens up to the patio outside, with steps up to a large (for London!) garden, ideal for BBQs and entertaining.

There is a separate utility room and WC, with a washer-dr
listings.area_description:
East Dulwich is one of South London's hidden gems, close to the centre of London but offering a lovely village charm. We are 1-minute walk from Lordship Lane, which is packed with independent shops, cosy cafés, gastropubs, and a popular Saturday market.  There is also a indoor swimming pool within 10 minutes walk and even closer a cinema showing new and old favourites.

We love the area for its green parks and woods, including Dulwich and Peckham Rye Parks and Sydenham Woods . 
 Dulwich Picture
listings.other_notes:

* Brockwell Lido, a 50-metre open air pool and sauna

listings.description:
Our home is a recently extended and renovated 3/4 bedroom flat in a beautiful Victorian house with a large 40 metre garden.  It is the best of both worlds as we are 15 minutes to Central London by train but surrounded by green space with a lovely village feel. It is perfect for enjoying the excitement of Central London whilst also getting the opportunity to relax. 

It is located just 3 minutes walk to West Dulwich Station and also in walking distance of Herne Hill and the beautiful Dulwich vill
listings.area_description:
Dulwich is perfectly located to access Central London and all the fantastic sights and activities it has to offer just a short train journey (15 minutes) to London Victoria and London Blackfriars (20 minutes).

London Victoria is walking distance to Buckingham Palace and the exclusive neighbourhoods of Mayfair and Belgravia. London Blackfriars is located a short walk from the Tate Modern and South Bank.

However, the area itself is surrounded by parks and green spaces and beautiful Victorian arc
listings.other_notes:
If required, we have a highchair, pram and travel cot for babies.

We have a cleaner that comes once a week for who will change the bed linen and tidy at the cost of £50.

listings.title: Victorian 4 bedroom  house in London (zone 2)

Lots of independent shops on the high street, Lordship Lane, with cafes, ice-cream shops, restaurants offering lots to do if you fancy a day away from sightseeing in central London. We have some beautiful parks nearby with Dulwich Park and Brockwell Park. listings.title: Lovely house in Lovely area of London

East Dulwich is a trendy and cosmopolitan area at zone 2 within easy reach of central London. The house is on a quiet road with free parking.
Nearby are cafes, restaurants and some of London great parks.

The lounge is in the front of the house and is a sociable space, cosy in the winter with a log burner and bright and sunny in the summer.

Dulwich Park is a really lovely park and is a 3 min walk from our front door. There you can find jogging tracks, lakes, nature spaces, football space, tennis courts, pedaloes, a children's playground, a cafe and more.

listings.title: Annabel's house
listings.description:
This is our much loved 4 bed family home, right in the middle of vibrant East Dulwich, 

The house is light, spacious and beautifully decorated - perfect for couples wanting space and families with kids. We have a large garden with a trampoline, table tennis table, garden games etc. We also have 4 bikes, a playhouse, and a BBQ.

It's an easy walk to Peckham with its trendy restaurants and bars, and an equally short walk in the opposite direction to beautiful Dulwich Village, with it's lovely park, college, and Dulwich Picture Gallery.  Lots of lovely green spaces are a short walk away - Dulwich Park, Peckham Rye, and Dulwich and Sydenham woods.

listings.title: London Penthouse in beautiful green surroundings
listings.description: We have the best view of the London skyline you will ever see in a private home!
listings.area_description: Dulwich feels like a village much further out of London than its Zone 2 location would indicate. We have beautiful parks, woodlands, and the most attractive high street in London as voted by The Times newspaper in 2025. We are surrounded by trees and greenery. You will feel what it's like to live as a Londoner in one of the most attractive neighbourhoods and only be a 15 minute train ride from central.
listings.other_notes: The living room and balcony boast an astonishing panoramic scene across the whole of London, no photos can ever do it justice. listings.title: Edwardian house in leafy southeast London .
listings.description:
Comfortable  family home in Dulwich with period features but a contemporary vibe with flexible living . For cold evenings in we have  a cosy  wood burning stove, wifi and Plenty of toys for younger guests whilst you watch a movie on Netflix, prime or Mubi . 
On Warm summer days, open our bifold windows onto into a charming garden with paved eating areas under Olive trees.
listings.area_description:
Leafy, peaceful Dulwich  offers  all the benefits of village life, with it's Picture gallery, cafe life, ancient Woods and Parks , yet nearby overground stations take you to  London Bridge and the vibrant South bank in 16 mins  and  the West End's Victoria Station in less than 10 mins. A ten minute bus trip takes you to Brixton with it's world famous market .

 Buzzy restaurants: Yamamomo or  Meat Liquor in East Dulwich. Rocca in Dulwich Village

It has two bedrooms- one suitable more for an infant including a crib or an older child (with a blow up mattress on request), a modern kitchen with various amenities, a comfortable double living room, and large, shared garden. The house features good Wi-Fi, a quiet workspace with a desk, and lots of natural
listings.area_description:
East Dulwich offers a nice mix of both worlds — leafy streets and green parks with good links also to central London. 
	•	5-min walk to shops, cafés, bakeries, and restaurants on Lordship Lane

There's also a direct bus to Central London, making it effortless to explore the city. The gated community is surrounded by friendly neighbors, creating a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The area is well-connected and offers easy access to parks, shops, and other amenities. The quiet surroundings make it a perfect retreat from the hustle and bustle of city

Flat has everything you need for a comfortable stay.
listings.area_description: I live in Gipsy Hill between two lovely areas of London, Dulwich and Crystal Palace. Everything is well connected by public transport. Dulwich and Crystal Palace have lovely parks, pubs and restaurants.

Looking for swops to coastal or mountains please!

A period Victorian home with high ceilings, beautiful big windows looking onto a lush garden.
listings.area_description: East Dulwich is a family-friendly neighbourhood in SE London which has a bit of everything. Bus and train routes take you directly into central London within 30/45 mins, and you are also nearby neighbourhoods like Peckham and Brixton which are fun, creative hubs to explore.

3 Bedroom house- 3 double rooms with twin beds in the children’s room and a sofa bed in the guest room. 
We have an additional double bed in a converted guest room downstairs. 
Our 1960s home is very popular with designers and architects many of whom bid for these houses when they come up - the house is filled with natural light and typical of mid century architecture.
We have off-street parking and a garage space- very rare for London. We also have a ya

Dulwich is a vibrant, safe and family friendly neighbor hood  with excellent transport links to central London and a stone’s throw from the city’s best parks and walkable to several other south London attractions and hotspots.

3 mins walk from West Dulwich Station (Victoria/Blackfriars) and 1 minute from n3 bus. (Horseguards)
Off street Parking in private residential area.
2 mins walk to Belair Park.

15 minutes walk

Our daughter has several allergies so we have no pets.

listings.title: Bright penthouse apartment in Camden Town (school holidays only)
listings.description: Corner penthouse apartment above the hustle and bustle of Camden Town. With a wrap-around balcony, we have great views of the historic market to one side, and the landmarks of the City to the other. The wall to wall glass with sliding doors off each room means that it's bright even on the dullest day. The apartment is on a lively market street with bars and restaurants and one of London's most popular tourist destinations right below, but is also in one of the greenest areas of London and an eas
listings.area_description:
Our apartment is located in the heart of buzzy Camden Town, one of the most popular London tourist destinations, with the historical market, Camden Lock and music history. 

We are above a lively market street with restaurants and bars but also a stroll away from the beautiful green spaces of Regent's Park and Primrose Hill. You can explore the charming nearby villages of Primrose Hill and Hampstead or get to the heart of the West End, with all its culture and shopping on offer, or Kings Cross
listings.other_notes: A bright and modern penthouse apartment in buzzy Camden Town. One of the greenest areas of central London: on doorstep of Regent's Park, Primrose Hill and Hampstead Heath. Superb transport links: a 10 minute journey to Oxford Circus / the West End, with easy access to national rail lines and the Eur

listings.title: London house (sorry no guest points)
listings.description: Our four storey Victorian house is in the heart of London's trendy and friendly Kentish Town district. The house is full of character with high ceilings, many original features, and it includes a wood-burning stove. It is also perfectly placed for reaching London's many attractions and soaking up its culture and character.  It is less than half a mile from Camden Market and the Roundhouse, and even closer to Kentish Town swimming pool, Talacre park and sports centre, friendly shops and great pub
listings.area_description: Kentish Town is one of London's liveliest neighbourhoods. It is full of wonderful pubs, restaurants and shops. It includes the fabulous and newly refurbished Edwardian baths, which are two minutes from our house and the well equipped Talacre Sports Centre. London Zoo, Primrose Hill, Hampstead Heath, Camden Market, and the Regents Canal are all within easy walking distance.
listings.other_notes:
Handsome Victorian house with original features
One minute from swimming pool, sports centre and park, and craft brewery 
Very near Camden Market, close to central London

listings.title: Family townhouse in central London
listings.description:
The house is a typical English townhouse on 4 floors with 1 double bedroom and 3 kids bedrooms on 3rd and 4th floor. Two Reception rooms on first floor, where one room has fire place. Kitchen with big dining space/room and another fire place is on the ground floor.
Street and garden entrance from ground floor. The Garden area at the rear of the house has a Terrace, accessed directly from the house (with dining BBQ/table/chairs), where stairs go down to garden with green lawn and another seating
listings.area_description: We have a late Georgian (1850's) family town house with big green garden situated in Camden Town, just by Regents Park. Ideal position in London with shops, cafes, restaurants, bars -a busy city life around one corner and a huge green park (Regents Park) around the other (with several play grounds and tennis courts as well as ponds, while the house itself is on a calm, nice, tree lined street. You walk across Regents Park in 20 minutes and you are in the very heart of London and all it has to of
listings.other_notes: We love travelling and exploring so always open for exchanges to all corners of the world! We have done many exchanges in the past and we think it is a fantastic way of seeing and experiencing new places!

The village offers a delightful atmosphere of London village, due to its pretty high street and wonderful views of London from the top of the hill. 

Primrose Hill has easy  access to many  of cultur

Fantastic selections of restaurants, cafes and pubs.

Has a village feel but is in central London.

calendar.summary: No summer entries — may be available

details.full_description:
My home is a historical listed Artist Studio located in the heart of Primrose Hill, which is a tranquil oasis close to Regents Park. It has been renovated to a high standard and has two bedrooms, 2 bathrooms,well equipped kitchen. Sleeping accommodation is one kingsize, one double and a single bed.

The village offers a delightful atmosphere of London village, due to its pretty high street and wonderful views of London from the top of the hill. 

Primrose Hill has easy  access to many  of cultural highlight locations. You can walk to Oxford Circus in 30 min - through Regents Park. London Zoo is 10 mins walk away. You have easy access to Covent Garden, London Museums and Theatres via an efficient bus and underground network. The bus & underground are less than 10 mins walk away.

My reviews as a guest:
1. "It is our second time welcoming Fiona home and it has been a pleasure to meet again, she has been respectful, kind and has taken care of our home as if it were her own. Looking forward to enjoying an exchange with her again."

Reviews as a host:
1. "We had a wonderful time in Fiona's beautiful home! It is perfectly located to visit the centre of London. The communication with Fiona was excellent, before, during and after the swap, making our stay easy to organize. Fiona was also the perfect guest, leaving our home immaculate so we would definitely love to swap with her again!"

2. "We had a wonderful time in Fiona´s beautiful and spacious modern house in London. We loved the unique style of the house and there was plenty of space for our family . The house was beautifully prepared and presented, impeccably clean and tidy and we loved the warm welcome. We were so grateful for the kind support of Fiona, the excellent communication and local insights - invaluable! Thank you Fiona for an unforgettable holiday!”

3. Fiona's home is just perfect! A former artist's studio converted into a comfortable flat with all modcons, it is located in a charming part of London, close to the tube for easy access to King's Cross and the West End. Located in a quiet mews, the apartment is luminous and airy with a lovely modern kitchen and mezzanine, with two bedrooms and en-suite bathrooms. Fiona was also very communicative throughout the whole swap process, making it a very enjoyable experience!

4. "We absolutely loved our stay at Fiona's artist studio. well furnished with 2 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms. was perfect for us. There is so much natural light and the details of the place are nicely done. Kitchen is well stocked and the whole place is comfortable.
Location is amazing - can walk everywhere - restaurants, pubs, cafes and shops all around the neighborhood. Easy walks to Camden market, long walks along the canal, walks in primrose hill park a couple blocks away and Regent Park right next door.
Public transport system is all within easy walking distance.
Thank you Fiona and we look forward to hosting you and returning to your beautiful place soon.
Beth and Dick"

5. I have just spent a glorious week in Fiona's home. I know London quite well, but being in this tucked away, private cottage in the centre of Primrose Hill was a real treat and a new experience exploring that part of London.
The place looks very cottage -like on the outside and is located down a little lane into a charming enclave kind of setting. Inside it is a beautifully designed open modern space.
There is a lot of natural light and plenty of room over 3 bedrooms (and 2 bathrooms) to accommodate 5 people. The place was spotless and very very quiet!
The kitchen is huge with a huge wonderful open living space and lots of natural light.
Primrose Hill is a charming little village situated on the top of a hill with a magnificent view of all of London. All this within a 10 minute tube ride (Chalk Farm Tube) to Oxford Street (Central London).
Fiona communicated quickly and clearly throughout the process and checked in with me during my stay.
I would highly recommend this as a great place to stay if visiting London! (By the way, Fiona also left my place spotless as my swap guest :)"

6. "What a wonderful home, thank you Fiona for sharing you’re slice of paradise in the heart of London. The interiors were gorgeous and the mews were idyllic and so quiet. We couldn’t believe we were in the centre of London. I had a completely unique experience of London because of the location. We were able to walk to so many areas through the parks. I can’t wait to visit again."

listings.title: Stylish and Cozy Home in Peaceful Central London

Our ground floor one-bedroom flat in Primrose Hill is a calm, comfortable base right in the heart of London. We’re just a short walk from Camden Town, Regent’s Park and the hill itself, but inside the flat it feels peaceful and quiet — a lovely place to come home to after a busy day in the city.

The bedroom is bright with beautiful natural light and has its own en-suite bathroom, which makes the space feel private and easy to live in. The living area is cosy and relaxed, with a projector for fi

Primrose Hill has a real village feel, with great cafés, bakeries and independent shops, and it’s a lovely, family-friendly area with a strong sense of community. At the same time, you’re incredibly well connected, with easy access to the Tube and buses to get anywhere in London quickly.

listings.area_description: Kentish Town sits just north of central London, approximately 20 minutes away by excellent public transport, all easily accessible from the doorstep. The neighbourhood is celebrated for its characterful pubs and outstanding food scene, alongside a thriving live-music culture with iconic venues such as the Jazz Café, the O2 Forum and the Roundhouse close at hand. Hampstead Heath is a pleasant 15-minute walk away, offering one of London’s most beautiful open green spaces. Everyday conveniences are
listings.other_notes: For summer guests, Kentish Town has a municipal indoor swimming pool, there's an outdoor pool called the lido nearby, and there are the three natural swimming ponds on Hampstead Heath. Tennis courts are nearby and a kids park with skate boarding.

- Lots of free museums (History Museum/Science Museum are all free in London!) 
- I am biased but I believe we have the best parks in the world. We are a 15 min walk to both Hampstead Heath and Regents Park. An Oasis of nature, the rose garden in Regents Park is my personal favourite. Great Playgrounds for kids
listings.other_notes: We dont have Pets, our son is super allergic to all animals (except cats strangely) so we can't have any pets in our home. We also cant go to a home with Dogs or anywhere near horses. We are non smokers and cant accept any smoking in our home or garden. Thank you

details.full_description:
We are planning to move soon so we are afraid we can't make this house available for exchange at the moment. 

We adore our home.  It is a 19th-century period property (1876) given a beautiful makeover by the architect we bought it off last year. She's created extra space and light in every room, and a wonderful feature wooden staircase up the whole height of the house. We've added our own touches to make it an elegant, cosy and peaceful living space. 

Please be aware our home is only available for the dates specified in our calendar. Please don't enquire about staying at our home at dates different from these because we won't be able to help - our time off is rather constricted by school holidays! 

We live in a quiet cul de sac, and at the back we have a glorious view along the gardens of the adjacent street (see pictures). Our own garden gets sun in the summer all day long. 

The master bedroom has a superking-size bed and overlooks the garden; the brand-new very comfortable sofa bed is on the first floor, and doubles as a book-lined second living room. These two bedrooms share the master bathroom. 

The second floor is a self contained space with single bed, desk area and ensuite shower. We can provide a further blow-up double mattress upon request. We've also got an amenties room with large washer dryer and downtairs lavatory. 

The house has a brand new boiler, central heating, a woodburning stove and underfloor heating in the bathrooms. 

We don't think the house is suitable for small children. 

We have a single request if you stay in our home. It comes with an equally beautiful and very friendly cat, Beau, and we would like you to look after him when you come. He is a wonderful companion and we hope would be an asset to any stay. 

Please do ask questions about any details you are curious about.
details.full_area_description:
The Area

The house is a 15-minute walk/ short bus journey to Notting Hill. In Notting Hill is the famous Portobello Road Market, as well as many bars, restaurants, vinyl record and antique shops. Also nearby is Golborne Road boasting vintage stores, some of the best coffee in London and Erno Goldfinger’s landmark Trellick Tower.

Chamberlayne Road with a range of useful shops, restaurants and bars is a 2-minute walk away from our house.

The gorgeous Queen’s Park itself, complete with children’s playground, petting zoo and café, is a 10-minute walk away. The park has its own boutique high street of Salusbury Road, with an excellent wine shop and a very popular farmers’ market on Sunday mornings.

 

Transport

Our house is on the Bakerloo Underground line; the nearest stop to us is Kensal Green (10-minute walk), with Queen’s Park a little further off. We are on two Overground lines: Kensal Rise stop (10-minute walk), on the Mildmay line; while Kensal Green and Queen’s Park are on the Lioness line, which takes you to Euston in ten minutes. 

The Mildmay line stops at Hampstead Heath, Camden, Islington and Hackney. It also goes to Richmond for Kew Gardens.

The Bakerloo line stops at Paddington, Marylebone, Baker Street, Regent’s Park, Oxford Circus (for Oxford Street and Soho), Piccadilly and Waterloo.

Local buses go to all of the major central London destinations.

The 52 bus takes you to Notting Hill, Kensington Church Street, Hyde Park and Harrods.

The 18 bus takes you to Marylebone High Street (which leads on to Oxford Street/ Bond Street) and Euston.

Take a train from Paddington Station for a day trip to Oxford (1 hour 30 mins door to door).

 

Recommended Restaurants

Local restaurants: 

Ida – a 5 minute walk https://www.idarestaurant.co.uk

Parlour – a 2 minute walk https://www.parlourkensal.com/

Sacre Coure for great pizza – a 5 minute walk https://www.sacrocuore.co.uk

Salusbury and Lonsdale Road in Queen’s Park and Goldborne Road/ All Saints Road in Notting Hill have many great places to choose from.

 

Nearby high end restaurants: 

Core (Three Michelin stars) – 15 min bus journey/ 30 min walk https://corebyclaresmyth.com

Julie’s (legendary!) – 20 minute bus journey https://juliesrestaurant.com

Clarke’s (for Lucian Freud lovers) – on the 52 bus route - https://www.sallyclarke.com

Other Michelin recommended local restaurants: https://guide.michelin.com/gb/en/best-of/the-best-restaurants-in-notting-hill

 

Recommended Pubs 

The Chamberlayne https://thechamberlayne.com

Salusbury https://www.thesalusbury.co.uk

The Cow https://thecowlondon.com

Walmer Castle https://www.walmercastle-nottinghill.co.uk

The Pelican https://thepelicanw11.com

Churchill Arms https://www.churchillarmskensington.co.uk/

 

Specialist food and drink

Salusbury Wine Store

https://www.thesalusburywinestore.com/?srsltid=AfmBOopiXF_ajEweci5V5XBTxpEcdMtf3kN1NaELH8jwtQ_8rcuyPCfu

Rise and Vine

https://riseandvine.co.uk/delivery/

Brooks https://www.instagram.com/brookskensalrise/?hl=en

Provenance https://provenancebutcher.com/pages/queens-park

Gracia and Sons

https://www.rgarciaandsons.com

Queens Park Farmers’ Market - https://www.lfm.org.uk/markets/queens-park/

There are a range of supermarkets within walking distance: Marks and Spencer, Sainsbury and Tesco

Local cinemas

Lexi https://thelexicinema.co.uk/TheLexiCinema.dll/Home

Electric https://www.electriccinema.co.uk/cinemas/portobello/

The Gate https://www.picturehouses.com/cinema/the-gate

There are two double bedrooms, plus a dedictaed
listings.area_description: We live in southeast London, in the borough of Greenwich, in an area called Plumstead. Although our home is in London and within easy reach of excellent transport links and shops, we are located on a quiet street which has the feel of a country lane, complete with foxes, owls, and the occasional hedgehog! There are many lovely walks just at our doorstep, and yet getting into central london is easy with the new Elizabeth line just a twenty minute walk or five minute bus-ride away.

listings.title: Georgia's house
listings.description:
A stones throw from Queens Park and walking distance from Notting Hill, our flat is cosy and the perfect hub for visitors.

Our garden is relatively large (for London standards at least!) and we are a 5 minute walk from the underground and overground stations of Queens Park and Kensal Rise. Soho is around 25 minutes away door to door, as is Euston station. 

Our home is quiet and cosy, with mid century furniture and lots of books.
listings.area_description:
We are a short walk from the heart of Queens Park which has a village feel, with lots of lovely cafes and restaurants. 

If you walk the other way, you come to Kensal Rise which is its own little village too! From there you can catch the overground for 10 mins to get to the lovely Hampstead Heath, or from QP you can get to soho on the underground very quickly.
listings.other_notes: We are looking to swap with someone in Edinburgh throughout the month of August as my partner is doing a show at the Fringe Festival.

listings.title: Stunning Mews house in the heart if Notting Hill
listings.description:
Our beautiful completely refurbished thatched house is located midway between Soho Farmhouse and Daylesford - 10 mins to both.  Chipping Norton is only a 5 minute drive away.  Long Compton is a traditional Cotswold village with its own village store, excellent pub (Red Lion), and moments from gorgeous walks.


The original house dates from the 17th Century and has been tastefully extended over the last 30 years.  It was completely refurbished two years ago and is offered in immaculate conditi
listings.area_description:
Long compton is a beautiful village with the local pub red lion and beautiful long walks you can do behind the house

Its close to chipping norton and ten minutes from Famous Soho farmhouse and 8 minutes drive to Daylesford
